gmw_00000462.name: SDSS J1004+4112
gmw_00000462.overview: SDSS J1004+4112 is a lensed quasar at redshift z = 1.734 [arXiv:2109.10720, arXiv:2307.14833] that is gravitationally lensed into five images by an intervening galaxy cluster at redshift z = 0.68 [arXiv:2109.10720, arXiv:2301.11240]. The system exhibits intrinsic optical variability [arXiv:2109.10720] and strong microlensing [arXiv:2311.08915], alongside radio emission linked to active galactic nucleus activity [arXiv:2109.10720].

sources.summary: The paper models the cluster-scale lensed quasar SDSS J1004+4112, using measured time delays and non-parametric lens reconstructions to estimate H0 and predict image E delay.

quotes.quote: We present a free-form lens model for the multiply lensed quasar in the galaxy cluster SDSS J 1004+4112 . Our lens model makes minimal assumptions about the distribution of mass in the lens plane. We pay particular attention to the model uncertainties on the predicted time delay, originating from the particular configuration of model variables. Taking into account this uncertainty, we obtain a value of the Hubble constant of H_0= 74^ +9 _ 13 , consistent with independent recent estimates.

The predicted time delay between the central image E and image C (the first to arrive), is T_ E-C =3200 200 days. Future measurements of T_ E-C will allow to impose a tighter constrain on H_0 from this cluster-QSO system.

In this work, we explored the Refsdal's method for the cluster-scale lensed quasar SDSS J1004+4112. Using its three well-measured delays and non-parametric lens mass reconstructions, we complemented the two recent studies of the system to achieve a more comprehensive perspective.

sources.summary: The paper uses SDSS J1004+4112 as a cluster-lensed quasar showing strong microlensing, rapid broad-line changes, and evidence for compact dark lenses.

quotes.quote: The most convincing evidence comes from the cluster lens SDSS J1004+4112 h20b where strong microlensing is observed in the light curves of the quasar images some 60 kpc from the cluster centre. As the optical depth to microlensing _* by the stellar population has already dropped to negligible levels 25 kpc from the cluster centre, it seems clear that the microlenses must be part of some other population of compact bodies.

In their analysis of broad line microlensing in SDSS J1004+4112 , as well as noting differences in line profile between images A and B, also find that for image A the profile of the C iv line changes by a large amount over a period of 6 months. This very short timescale should be compared with the dynamical or cloud crossing timescale p93 for the BLR in quasars, given by _ dyn = V_ FWHM 50 years where is the radius of the BLR as measured in reverberation mapping experiments, and is typically about a year for quasars l18 , and V_ FWHM is the Doppler width of the broad line with a typical value of 6000 km sec ^ 1 . Given that by comparing the spectra of different images it is established that the BLR is being microlensed, it seems reasonable to conclude that the very short timescale event in image A is also the result of microlensing. This would be in line with results from monitoring of the accretion disc of SDSS J1004+4112 for microlensing variations, where image C was observed to increase in brightness by 0.7 magnitudes in 200 days h20b .

Rapid changes in emission line shape due to microlensing are well illustrated by considering the differences in emission line profile due to microlensing in the cluster lens SDSS J1004+4112, illustrated in Fig. fig1 . The difference in light travel time between the two images is very short, image B leading image A by 41 days f08 , which implies that the observed change in emission line structure cannot be intrinsic to the emission line region.

sources.summary: SDSS J1004+4112 is described as the RBS 825 lensed QSO, a type I z=1.73 quasar with multiple images, anomalous X-ray/UV flux ratios, and image-A microlensing signatures.

quotes.quote: Strange magnification pattern in SDSS J1004+4112 The lensed QSO SDSS J1004+4112 was originally found as an X-ray source in the ROSAT All Sky Survey (RASS) and entered the ROSAT Bright Source catalogue under the designation RBS 825 schwope . The brightest optical counterpart within the RASS error circle turned out to be a type I QSO at z=1.73 and was regarded as the unique identification of the unresolved X-ray source. Its lensed nature was discovered in a survey of large separation lenses using the Sloan Digital Sky Survey (SDSS) inada*:03 . Optical imaging revealed 4 images of the quasar, with a maximum separation of 14.6'' it is the largest separation lensed QSO known so far.

The integrated X-ray flux of the system has decreased by a factor of 6 since it was observed in the ROSAT All Sky Survey in 1990, while the X-ray spectrum became much harder with the power law index evolving from =-2.3 to =-1.86 . By deblending the X-ray images of the lensed QSO we find that the X-ray flux ratios between the lens images A and B are significantly different from the simultaneously obtained UV ratios and previously measured optical flux ratios. Our optical spectrum of lens image A shows an enhancement in the blue emission line wings, which has been observed in previous epochs as a transient feature. We propose a scenario where intrinsic UV and X-ray variability gives rise to line variations which are selectively magnified in image A by microlensing.

sources.summary: The paper studies SDSS J1004+4112 as a rare cluster-lensed quasar with five images, long time delays, seven years of monitoring, microlensing events, and an inferred accretion-disk size.

quotes.quote: The wide-separation lensed quasar SDSS J1004+4112 is one of the rare examples of a quasar lensed by an intervening galaxy cluster wam2003,inada2006 . It is lensed into five images inada2005 , with a maximum image separation of 14.62 '' . The quasar has a redshift of z_s= 1.734 and the redshift of the galaxy cluster is z_ l= 0.68 fohl2008a . The lag between images A, B, and C has been experimentally measured by fohl2008a who obtained a time delay of 40.6 days between components A and B and of 822 days between A and C (the largest delay measured for a gravitational lens system).

In this paper we are presenting seven years, corresponding to eight observational seasons, of optical monitoring data for the four brightest images of SDSS J1004+4112 spanning 2505 days from December 2003 to October 2010, partly coincident with the epochs studied by fohl2008a , but that significantly extend the coverage with around 1200 additional days. Our aim is to use these data to study the existence of possible microlensing events and to estimate the size of the accretion disk of the lensed quasar. We have been able to make microlensing analysis including image D for the first time thanks to the availability of long enough observations to correct for the time delay shifting the light curves.

sources.summary: The paper reports faint LOFAR 144 MHz emission from the lensed quasar SDSS J1004+4112, deriving intrinsic flux, radio luminosity, and a possible star-formation rate.

quotes.quote: SDSS J1004+4112 SDSS J1004+4112 is a broad emission line quasar at redshift 1.730 that is gravitationally lensed by an intervening galaxy cluster at redshift 0.68 into five images with a maximum separation of 14.62 arcsec inada2003 . The positions of the lensed images are in a fold configuration, where the two closest lensed images, A and B, have the highest point-source magnifications of 29.7 and 19.6, respectively, and the three other images, C, D and E have point-source magnifications of 11.6, 5.8 and 0.16, respectively oguri2010 . Previous radio imaging with the Very Large Array at 5 GHz detected emission from lensed images A, B, C and D, implying an intrinsic (lensing corrected) flux density at 5 GHz of 2.5 0.2 Jy jackson2011 .

The LoTSS image of SDSS J1004+4112 at 144 MHz detects low frequency radio emission from lensed images A and B at the 6 level, with a combined peak surface brightness of 430 Jy beam ^ 1 . The measured flux-densities of A and B at 144 MHz are 369 76 and 271 75 Jy, respectively, which given their lensing magnifications implies an intrinsic (lensing corrected) flux density of 13 2 Jy.

Using this spectral index, the rest-frame 144 MHz luminosity density is L_ 144 MHz = 1.58 0.08 10^ 23 W Hz ^ 1 , making SDSS J1004+4112 the lowest radio-power source currently known at z 0.65 that has been detected with LOFAR (e.g. kondapally2020,sabater2020 ). Under the assumption that all of this radio emission is associated with star formation, and using the redshift-dependent radio--infrared correlation at 150 MHz derived by calistro2017 , we find that this luminosity density is equivalent to a radio-derived star-formation rate of 5.5^ +1.8 _ 1.4 M _ yr ^ 1 .

sources.summary: The paper presents optical monitoring of the cluster-lensed quasar SDSS J1004+4112, measuring delays among images A, B, and C and emphasizing the system's long-delay lensing geometry.

quotes.quote: We present 107 new epochs of optical monitoring data for the four brightest images of the gravitational lens SDSS J1004+4112 observed between October 2006 and June 2007. Combining this data with the previously obtained light curves, we determine the time delays between images A, B and C. We confirm our previous measurement finding that A leads B by t_ BA =40.6 1.8 days, and find that image C leads image A by _ CA =821.6 2.1 days.

The quasar SDSS J1004+4112 at z_s=1.734 is split into five images by an intervening galaxy cluster at z_l=0.68 . With a maximum image separation of 14 62 , it is a rare example of a quasar gravitationally lensed by a cluster . One of the most interesting applications of this system is to use the time delays between the lensed images to study the structure of the cluster.

sources.summary: The paper uses SDSS J1004+4112 as a lensed quasar source with multiple images, source redshift, and time-delay constraints in a positive-cosmological-constant strong-lensing calculation.

quotes.quote: Strong lensing by an isolated spherically symmetric mass distribution is considered in presence of a positive cosmological constant. Deflection angles and time delay are computed and compared to the multiple image of the quasar SDSS J1004+4112.

This picture, taken from Ota et al. , shows four images ( A , B , C , D ) of the same quasar lensed by a cluster of galaxies visible only via X-rays and whose center is indicated on the picture by a cross.

The position of terra nostra is indicated by T , of the cluster = lens by L and of the quasar = source by S . and ' are the physical angles between the images and the lens. They are measured in nano-seconds over nano-seconds and appear directly on the picture. The coordinate distances r_T and r_S are radial coordinates of polar coordinates centered at the lens. The coordinate distances will be computed from the measured red-shifts of cluster and quasar, z_L and z_S .

sources.summary: The paper presents EVLA observations of the lensed quasar SDSS J1004+4112, detecting four lensed images and describing its cluster lens, magnification, modeling history, and exceptionally faint intrinsic radio flux.

quotes.quote: We present new radio observations of the large-separation gravitationally-lensed quasar SDSS J1004+4112, taken in a total of 6 hours of observations with the Extended Very Large Array (EVLA). The maps reach a thermal noise level of approximately 4 Jy. We detect four of the five lensed images at the 15-35 Jy level, representing a source of intrinsic flux density, after allowing for lensing magnification, of about 1 Jy, intrinsically probably the faintest radio source yet detected.

As a beginning to such a programme, we present new, deep EVLA observations of the lensed quasar SDSS J1004+4112. This system consists of a z_s=1.73 quasar being lensed by a galaxy cluster at z=0.68 into five images, with a maximum separation of 14 6, and was discovered in the Sloan Quasar Lens Survey (Inada et al. 2003; Oguri et al. 2004; Inada et al. 2005) using the catalogue of quasars from the Sloan Digital Sky Survey (Schneider et al. 2007). It has been modelled by numerous authors (Williams Saha 2004, Kawano Oguri 2006, Saha et al. 2007, Fohlmeister et al. 2007, Liesenborgs et al. 2009, Oguri 2010) using constraints including multiple time delays (Fohlmeister et al. 2007, 2008), spectroscopy of galaxies in the cluster (Sharon et al. 2005), and Chandra X-ray observations (Ota et al. 2006).

sources.summary: The paper presents spectroscopy and polarimetry of the lensed quasar SDSS J1004+4112, emphasizing its large image separation and unresolved C IV blue-wing variability in component A.

quotes.quote: Here we present new spectroscopic and polarimetric observations of the lensed quasar SDSS J1004+4112, a four-image system with source redshift z_s =1.734 and lens redshift z_l =0.58 in03,og04 . This system exhibits an unusually large separation between images of even 15.0 ^ for more details, see in03,og04,wi04 . SDSS J1004+4112 is also interesting because of the variability of the broad emission lines in component A. The variability is observed in the blue wing of CIV and is not observed in the continuum or in the low-ionization lines see, e.g., ri04,la06,gr06,go06,fi18 .

The question of the origin of the CIV A component variation in J1004+4112 remains unsolved, and it motivates us to continue observing this lensed quasar. We obtained spectroscopic observations of J1004+4112 in three different epochs from 2007 to 2018 and polarimetric observations in the period 2014--2017 using the 6m telescope of the Special Astrophysical Observatory (SAO).

sources.summary: The paper models microlensing in SDSS J1004+4112, a cluster-lensed quasar split into five images, and argues compact dark-matter bodies can explain the amplitudes.

quotes.quote: This paper is aimed at providing a plausible explanation for the large amplitude microlensing events observed in the cluster-lensed quasar system, SDSS J1004+4112. The microlensed quasar images appear to lie well clear of the stellar population of the cluster, raising the possibility that the cluster dark matter is composed of compact bodies that are responsible for the observed microlensing. We established the exact structure of the difference light curves attributed to microlensing from photometric monitoring programmes in the literature.

SDSS J1004+4112 is a rare example of a quasar that is lensed by a galaxy cluster i03 . The quasar at a redshift of z_s = 1.734 is split into five images by a small galaxy cluster at z_d = 0.68 . The overall configuration of the quasar images and galaxies in the lensing cluster is shown in the Hubble Space Telescope (HST) composite image in Fig. fig1 .

sources.summary: The paper identifies SDSS J1004+4112 as a quadruple lensed quasar with C IV wing microlensing, outstanding asymmetry, four epochs, and blue-wing fading as the red wing strengthens.

quotes.quote: We detect microlensing in either the blue or the red wing (or in both wings with different amplitudes) of C IV in more than 50 of the systems and find outstanding asymmetries in the wings of QSO 0957+561, SDSS J1004+4112, SDSS J1206+4332, and SDSS J1339+1310. This observation indicates that the BLR is, in general, not spherically symmetric and supports the existence of two regions in the BLR, one insensitive to microlensing and another that only shows up when it is magnified by microlensing.

After consistently separating microlensing from intrinsic variability according to the criteria explained in Section sec2.2 (see also Tables table2 and table3 ), we find that nine objects are clearly affected by microlensing at the 2 level (HE 0435-1223, HS 0818-1227, FBQS J0951+2635, QSO 0957+561, SDSS J1004+4112, SDSS J1138+0314, SDSS J1206+4332, SDSS J1339+1310, HE 2149-2745) and two systems show evidence of intrinsic variability at the 2 level (HE 1104-1805, HE 2149-2745).

As discussed in Fian2018 , the distance traveled in the source plane is too small ( 0.6 light-days) for SDSS J1339+1310 to see variability in both wings. However, for SDSS J1206+4332 and SDSS J1004+4112 we obtained more interesting results, owing to larger displacements of the source ( 8 light-days for SDSS J1206+4332 and 12 light-days for SDSS J1004+4112). If we compare the four epochs in SDSS J1004+4112, we can see that the strongly magnified blue wing fades while the red wing enhances. In SDSS J1206+4332 we detect, when comparing epoch I and II, a slight demagnification of image B in the blue wing while the red wing enlarges. These observations confirm that the separation between the approaching and recending parts of the microlensed region of the BLR is about a few light-days in size.

sources.summary: The paper studies the giant lensed quasar SDSS J1004+4112 with VLA data and finds radio variability evidence favoring AGN-related emission over star formation alone.

quotes.quote: In SDSS J1004+4112 we find strong evidence for AGN-related radio emission in the variability of the source. In PG 1115+080 we find tentative evidence for AGN-related emission, determined by comparing the radio luminosity with modelled dust components.

SDSS J1004+4112 inada2003gravitationally , is a giant lens system produced by a galaxy cluster lying at redshift z=0.68 . In the optical, several background objects appear to be distorted by the intervening mass, producing 10 arcsecond-scale arcs and filaments of lensed galaxies sharon2005discovery , in addition to multiple point-like images of a lensed quasar. The quasar, at a redshift of z = 1.734 , is lensed into five images, with four magnified images in a fold configuration separated by a maximum of 14.62 arcseconds, and a fifth, de-magnified image embedded slightly West of the central galaxy in the cluster 2005PASJ...57L...7I,inada2008spectroscopic . The quasar source displays intrinsic variability in the optical (see e.g. 2008ApJ...676..761F,fian2016size ), resulting in time delays of years between the more distant components. The first radio observation of SDSS J1004+4112 2011ApJ...739L..28J used the improved sensitivity of the newly expanded VLA at 5 GHz in C configuration to produce an image of the system at a resolution of 3.5 arcsec.

sources.summary: The paper models SDSS J1004+4112 as a cluster-lensed quasar system, using a new time delay to constrain the lens mass profile, BCG offset, and background images.

quotes.quote: We have built a new model for the lens system SDSS J1004+4112 including the recently measured time delay of the fourth quasar image. This time delay has a strong influence on the inner mass distribution of the lensing cluster ( ^ ) allowing us to determine =1.18^ +0.02(+0.11) _ 0.03(-0.18) at the 68 (95 ) confidence level in agreement with hydrodynamical simulations of massive galaxy clusters. We find an offset between the brightest cluster galaxy (BCG) and the dark matter halo of 3.8^ +0.6(+1.4) _ 0.7(-1.3) kpc at 68 (95 ) confidence which is compatible with other galaxy cluster measurements.

The lens system SDSS J1004+4112 was the first example of a quasar lensed by a cluster 2003Natur.426..810I . In addition to the lensed quasar, SDSS J1004+4112 has seven lensed background galaxies at three different redshifts. In conclusion, we have made use of the recently measured time delay of image D and previous observational data to model the lensing cluster SDSS J1004+4112.

sources.summary: The paper uses SDSS J1004+4112 as a five-image cluster-lensed quasar system with HST constraints, lens modeling, and updated long-baseline time delays for H0 inference.

quotes.quote: \ was the first discovered galaxy cluster strongly lensing a quasar Inada2003 . The cluster at \ = 0.68 strongly lenses a quasar at \ = 1.734 into five images, with a maximum image separation of 14 6 (Table table:quasar_table ). The cluster also strongly lenses several background sources at \ = 2.74 Sharon2005 , \ = 3.288 Keren_thesis, Oguri2010 , and \ = 3.332 Sharon2005 (Table table:constraints ).

The \ imaging data include GO-10509 (PI: Kochanek) ACS/F814W, F555W, F435W (10 orbits); GO-9744 (PI: Kochanek) ACS/F814W, F555W (2 orbits); and GO-10793 (PI: Gal-Yam) ACS/F814W (1 orbit). These data were originally proposed to identify multiply-imaged galaxies to construct a mass model Sharon2005 , search for the fifth quasar image Inada2005 , derive _ , perform a weak lensing analysis, and search for supernovae in massive high-redshift clusters Sharon2010 .

After the completion of a 14.5 year monitoring campaign at the 1.2m Fred Lawrence Whipple Observatory (FLWO), presented new light curves for the four brightest images in , resulting in updated time delay values of t _ AB = -43.01 0.27, t _ AC = -825.23 0.46 days, and t _ AD = 1633.23 0.97 days (Table table:time_delays ).

sources.summary: The paper analyzes SDSS J1004+4112 as a cluster-lensed quasar system, showing that lens-model choices strongly affect Hubble-constant constraints and lensed-host morphology predictions.

quotes.quote: Specifically we focus on the strong lensing system SDSS J1004+4112 with the maximum image separation of 14.62 '' , the first system of a quasar lensed by a galaxy cluster with five multiple images. Incorporating the latest time-delay measurements, we investigate the lens model dependence from the combination of 16 different lens mass models. We find that the lens model dependence is indeed large, with the combined measurement of the Hubble constant of H_0=67.5^ +14.5 _ 8.9 that is obtained by summing posteriors of the Hubble constant from the 16 models with equal weighting.

As the first system of a quasar lensed by a cluster, the large-separation gravitationally lensed quasar SDSS J1004+4112 was discovered from the Sloan Digital Sky Survey (SDSS) data, which has generated much interest due to its rarity and particularity. This system is composed of five multiple images of a quasar at z_ s =1.734 with the maximum separation angle between the multiple images of 14.62 '' . A cluster of galaxies as the lensing object has been identified at z_ l =0.68 centered among the five images, the brightest galaxy and more galaxy cluster members have also been observed.

sources.summary: The paper presents SDSS J1004+4112 as the first cluster-scale lensed quasar, with four quasar images, line-profile differences, lens modeling, and cosmological implications.

quotes.quote: inada03b showed that SDSS J1004+4112 consists of four quasar images with the same redshift from the Keck spectroscopy. The colors of galaxies found by Subaru imaging follow-up observations indicated the presence of a cluster of galaxies at z=0.68 . Moreover, the configuration of the four images was successfully reproduced by a simple lens model based on a singular isothermal ellipsoid mass distribution.

All these results strongly implied that SDSS J1004+4112 is the first quasar lens system due to a massive cluster-scale object. In this paper, we describe photometric and spectroscopic follow-up observations of SDSS J1004+4112 in detail. We discuss the spectra of lensed quasar components, including puzzling differences between emission lines seen in the different images.

sources.summary: The paper monitors the lensed quasar SDSS J1004+4112, measures the A/B time delay, finds microlensing, and argues cluster-member galaxies must be included in lens models.

quotes.quote: We present 426 epochs of optical monitoring data spanning 1000 days from December 2003 to June 2006 for the gravitationally lensed quasar SDSS J1004+4112. The time delay between the A and B images is t_ BA =38.4 2.0 days ( ^2=4 ) in the expected sense that B leads A and the overall time ordering is C-B-A-D-E. The measured delay invalidates all published models.

SDSS J1004+4112 is a relatively faint quasar for monitoring with 1m-class telescopes, and the image quality of the FLWO and WISE telescopes is poor. The second important point to note is that the A/B flux ratio has changed significantly between the first and third seasons, indicating that microlensing is occurring in this system as has been previously suggested by variations in the C 4 emission line profile (Richards et al. 2004, Lamer et al. 2006, G omez- Alvarez et al. 2006).
Models including the eleven most important galaxies can simultaneously fit the A--E image positions and the measured A/B time delay with reasonable parameter values.

sources.summary: The paper uses SDSS J1004+4112 as an observed cluster-lensed quasar benchmark, noting its image separation, source redshift, central image, and cluster mass estimate.

quotes.quote: The first case was SDSS J1004+4112, a quadruply-imaged system with an separation of 14.6 arcsec ( Inada03, Oguri04 ). The cluster is at redshift of 0.68, and the lensed quasar is at redshift 1.734. A faint fifth image was later discovered ( Inada05 ), as were many other multiply-imaged background galaxies ( Sharon05 ).

Of particular interests are the central images in systems with at least four images, because for the first cluster quasar lens, J1004+4112, subsequent deep imaging reveals a faint central image, at about 0.3 of the brightest image in the system (see Table 1 in Inada05 ).

The mass distribution of SDSS J1004+4112 was studied in Ota06 using X-ray data. Assuming isothermality and an NFW profile for the cluster, the virial mass was derived to be M 4.2^ +2.6 _ 1.5 10^ 14 h^ 1 M_ .

sources.summary: The paper identifies SDSS J1004+4112 as one of the strongest microlensing cases, with CIV wing and core microlensing and variability across high-ionization lines.

quotes.quote: On the other hand, there are several systems (SDSS J1004+4112 and SDSS J1339+1310, in particular) in which microlensing is strongly affecting the wings of CIV (with respect to the core) without traces of this microlensing in the wings of MgII.

However, it is important to notice that in the two strongest cases of microlensing in the CIV line, if we match the CIII ] lines, the core of CIV is affected by microlensing with maximum amplitudes of 0.23 0.07 mag (SDSS J1004+4112) and 0.16 0.03 mag (SDSS J1339+1310). On average, we measure a weak microlensing effect in the cores of CIII ] ( 0.09 0.08 mag with respect to MgII) and CIV ( 0.12 0.11 mag with respect to CIII ] ), although in the two strongest cases of microlensing, the core of CIV is significantly affected ( 0.23 0.07 mag in SDSS J1004+4112 and 0.16 0.03 mag in SDSS J1339+1310).

The well studied case of SDSS J1004+4112 ( Richards2004,Gomez2006,Motta2012,Fian2016 and references therein) presents outstanding examples of variability induced by microlensing in all the high ionization lines. In SDSS J1004+4112 we have obtained more interesting results, owing to a larger displacement of the source ( 4 light-days), leading to variations in both wings.

sources.summary: The paper analyzes SDSS J1004+4112 as a lensed quasar whose microlensed broad-line and continuum emission constrain accretion-disk and broad-line-region sizes.

quotes.quote: SDSS J1004+4112 is the first known quasar lensed by a foreground cluster of galaxies and was discovered in the Sloan Digital Sky Survey (SDSS) while searching for large separation lenses ( Inada2003 ). The lensed quasar comprises four bright images (with magnitudes of i' =18.5 , 18.9 , 19.4 , and 20.1 ; see Inada2003 ) at a source redshift of z_s = 1.734 and a maximum separation angle of 14 .62 between components B and C, which are produced by a massive cluster at a lens redshift of z_l = 0.68 .

Optical microlensing, caused by stars in the cluster halo or nearby satellites, is known to exist in the lens system SDSS J1004+4112 . This phenomenon has been used to determine the size of the continuum-emitting source in the lensed quasar ( Fohlmeister2008,Fian2016,Foresr2023submitted ).

Recently, Hutsemekers2023 demonstrated that the deformation of the C IV emission line profile in SDSS J1004+4112 can be reproduced by employing simple broad-line region (BLR) models such as a Keplerian disk or an equatorial wind.

sources.summary: The paper uses SDSSJ1004+4112 as a cluster-lensed quasar case study with five images, microlensing features near galaxies, and source redshift z_s = 1.734.

quotes.quote: A quasar lensed by a galaxy or cluster like SDSSJ1004+4112 strong lensing system, with multiple images, is a suitable system for this study as: (i) using the time-delay method between different images, we can remove the intrinsic variations of the quasar, and (ii) strong lensing enhances signals from the transient weak lensing.
The quasar SDSSJ1004+4112 belongs to this sample . In this system, we have five images from the background quasar where some the images are close to the galaxies and exhibit microlensing features.
For the case of strong lensing images of SDSSJ1004+4112, we use the redshift of source at z_s = 1.734 .

sources.summary: The thesis reports SDSS J1004+4112 as the first large-separation lensed quasar, a four-image z=1.73 system produced by a z=0.68 galaxy cluster and consistent with CDM lensing predictions.

quotes.quote: We searched for large-separation lensed quasars from the data of the Sloan Digital Sky Survey, and succeeded in discovering the first large-separation lensed quasar SDSS J1004+4112. The system consists of four lensed images of a quasar at z=1.73 . We identify the lensing cluster at z=0.68 , from the deep imaging and spectroscopy of galaxies in the cluster. We calculate the expected probabilities and image multiplicities for lensed quasars in the SDSS, and find that the discovery of the large-separation quadruple lens SDSS J1004+4112 is quite consistent with the theoretical predictions based on the CDM model.

sources.summary: The paper uses SDSS J1004+4112 as a multiply lensed quasar test case, emphasizing its image separations, time delays, and sensitivity to foreground galaxy shear.

quotes.quote: One pair of such multiply lensed quasars with similar parameters, SDSS J1004+4112 and HE0435-1223, were reported by Fohlmeister et al 2006 and Kochanek et al 2006 respectively.

For SDSS J1004+4112 where the smallest observed image separation, between images A and B, was 4 arcsec and the comoving distances are D_s = 4.77 Gpc, D_l = 2.45 Gpc in an _m = 0.3, _ = 0.7 cosmology, Eq. (11) gives _ incoherent 24 days if we persist with the CDM standard model by adopting its breakdown of the matter budget to set _ cl = 0.15 (specifically this assumes that half the baryons, hence approximately the same fraction for dark matter also, of the low z Universe resides in galaxies and their halos, see Fukugita 2004 and Fukugita et al 1998). In fact, taking the above parameters as typical, one could proceed to recast Eq. (11) into a more convenient form: _ incoherent = 29.1 (h/0.7)^2 ( _ cl /0.15) ( /5 arcsec )^2 (D_l/2.5 Gpc )^3 1 + D_ ls D_l ^2 ^ 1 2 days . Since the observed image delay of 38.4 2.0 days is on par with _ random , any estimation of H_0 that attributes all the observed delay to physics within the strong lens system would have caused this value to vary randomly from the truth by almost 100 .

Other images of SDSS J1004+4112 can also be used as testbeds: the separation of image B from C (also A from D) is 20 arcsec, while the expected time delay between them is > 560 days ( > 800 days for A and D), due solely to the strong lens system itself.

gmw_00000463.name: NGC 4552
gmw_00000463.overview: NGC 4552 is an early-type elliptical galaxy [arXiv:1204.2590, arXiv:2111.02430] in the Virgo Cluster [arXiv:astro-ph/0609113, arXiv:2111.02430] hosting a low-luminosity active galactic nucleus [arXiv:astro-ph/0702292, arXiv:2107.02335] with a variable central source [arXiv:1305.2225, arXiv:astro-ph/9811131]. The galaxy features a kinematically distinct core [arXiv:astro-ph/0102374], an inner dust disk [arXiv:2107.02335], and a 10-kiloparsec ram-pressure stripped X-ray tail [arXiv:2401.04709].

sources.summary: NGC 4552 required variable spectral normalizations and showed soft and hard luminosity changes over two years, while surrounding X-ray sources affected aperture comparisons.

quotes.quote: NGC 4552 We used a Chandra observation taken in April 2001 and a XMM Newton observation taken in July 2003. We recall that different apertures were used (3 for Chandra and 25 for XMM Newton data). Since observations were obtained with different instruments, comparisons of HR were avoided. Both observations needed MEPL for the individual best fit. When varying parameters, the best fit was obtained when Norm_1 and Norm_2 varied (i.e. SMF2, ^2_r =1.21, see also Figure bestfig ), with 93 and 78 amplitude variations (see Table bestfit ).

We found intrinsic luminosity variations at 21.5 (14.1 ) in the soft (hard) energies, i.e., 87 (79 ) amplitude variations in a period of two years (Table lumincorr ). The Chandra image of this object revealed many X-ray sources surrounding the nucleus (see Figure images ). The contribution of the annular region to the 0.5-10.0 keV band emission in the 25 aperture Chandra data was 23 .

sources.summary: The paper monitors NGC 4552 as a nearby LLAGN, finds variability but no detected characteristic timescale, and cautions that sparse short-baseline data limit interpretation.

quotes.quote: Six LLAGNs, Centaurus A (hereafter Cen A), NGC 4374, NGC 4552, NGC 4579, NGC 4278, and NGC 5077, were selected. These sources are predicted to have variability timescales ranging from a few days to ten days, due to their black hole mass being situated between Sgr A* and M87. This is expected to provide clarity on the sub-millimeter mass scaling relationship.

We accumulate four years of observations using Submillimeter Array for Centaurus A, NGC 4374, NGC 4278, and NGC 5077 and one year of observations for NGC 4552 and NGC 4579. All sources are variable. We measure the characteristic timescale at which the variability is saturated by modeling these sources' light curve as a damped random walk. We detect a timescale for all the sources except NGC 4552. The detected timescales are comparable to the orbital timescale at the event horizon scale for most sources.

sources.summary: The paper studies M89's hot atmosphere, ram-pressure stripped X-ray tail, AGN-driven rings, and super-Solar abundance ratios using Chandra and XMM-Newton observations.

quotes.quote: For our study, we used all available archival Chandra observations of M89 with a total cleaned exposure of 201 ks (see Table table1 ). All observations were performed using the Advanced CCD Imaging Spectrometer array with ACIS-S (chip S3) in the aim point. Since the desired area of M89 does not overlap with other chips, we limited the analysis to the S3 chip.

Chandra X-ray images of M89 in the 0.7-1.1 keV and 0.5-2.0 keV bands are shown in Figure fig:image_analysis . The ram-pressure stripped tail of 10 kpc is clearly visible. In the central region, we can see the diffuse emission from the galaxy along with two ring-like structures associated with AGN-driven weak shocks.

In this paper, we investigated the chemical composition of the infalling elliptical galaxy M89 (NGC 4552), which hosts X-ray cavities and a prominent shock front due to radio mechanical AGN activity. During its voyage into the Virgo cluster, the hot galaxy atmosphere experiences ram-pressure stripping caused by the interaction with the intracluster medium. Due to the stripping, M89 hosts a bright X-ray tail.

sources.summary: The paper finds M89 is covered by tails, plumes, and shells, whose non-interleaved geometry suggests multiple accretions or a major merger rather than one simple shell-forming event.

quotes.quote: A great deal of the area near M89 is covered in substructure. Easily visible are the ``jet'' feature to the west (Region 1) and the extremely bright shell to the south (Region 2). We also find plumes to the northwest (Region 3), southwest (Region 4), and southeast (Region 5) of M89. Fainter and further away are two shells: one 9.3 \ (42 kpc) to the east (Region 6), and another 12.3 \ (57 kpc) to the northeast (Region 7).

The variety of tidal structure around M89 argues for a complicated accretion history. The W Tail (Region 1) is peculiar in that it has a high, relatively constant surface brightness across its length, and may be a long tidal tail seen curving back on itself in projection. Unlike the shell system of M49, M89's shells do not have the classic aligned and interleaved structure expected from phase wrapping from a small accretion event ( Quinn 1984).

sources.summary: The paper includes NGC 4552 among three additional early-type galaxies selected for high Na/Fe, with shorter minor-axis spectra that still support a bottom-heavy central IMF trend.

quotes.quote: In addition to these galaxies we observed three others, with the slit oriented along the minor axis: NGC 3414, NGC 4552, and NGC 4564. These galaxies were selected because of their relatively high [ Na/Fe ] values in data we had obtained previously. These earlier, as yet largely unpublished, data did not cover the Na D doublet. Exposure times were shorter for these objects (although still substantially longer than in van Dokkum Conroy 2012), and we cannot measure their IMF gradients to the same distance as for the primary galaxies.

For all six galaxies, the central M/L ratio is much higher for a varying IMF than for a Kroupa (2001) IMF. That is, all galaxies prefer a bottom-heavy IMF that is steeper than that of the Milky Way at low masses. The IMF parameter falls off steeply, from =2-3 at R=0 to 1 at 0.3 R_ e .

sources.summary: The paper uses M89 as a Virgo elliptical comparison, noting simulations where ram-pressure stripping removes much of its gas and creates diffuse side wings attached differently than in M60.

quotes.quote: The dimensions of these filamentary wings were compared with simulations by Roediger (2015a,b) of the Virgo galaxy M89 (NGC4552). The lower panel of Fig fig:wings shows a soft band X-ray projection of M89 that demonstrates how turbulent stripping can create wings of diffuse gas to the sides of the galaxy, as we observe for M60. The size of the wings scales with the size of the gas atmosphere, hence larger wings are seen in M60, compared to the simulations of the Virgo elliptical M89, since most of M89's gas has already been stripped. The double wing structure is seen in the simulation image (Fig fig:wings lower panel). The wings are attached directly to the sides of the galaxy as is the case for M60, another indicator of motion nearly in the plane of the sky.
For M89 these features are attached to the upstream edge rather than to the sides (Machacek 2006). Thus the simulation better represents M60 in this instance.

sources.summary: The paper studies M89 with optical/NIR spectroscopy, finding a bottom-heavy IMF gradient and discussing stellar-population gradients, accretion history, tidal structures, dust, and weak AGN signatures.

quotes.quote: As a test case, here we analyze data for the nearby galaxy M89, for which we have high S/N spectroscopic data that cover the 3500-9000 spectral region and allow us to study the radial variation of the stellar population properties out to 1 R _e .

The complexity of the accretion history of M89 has been already suggested by Janowiecki2010 who found the presence of a variety of tidal structures such as shells and plumes, in its nearby vicinity. These features can be connected with both multiple accretion events or a major merger that happened in the past. Another peculiarity of M89 that reaffirms its complicated assembly history is the presence of the inner dust disk.

Due to its weak emission lines compared to the underlying strong stellar emission, M89 has been classified as LINER ( ho97,maoz05 ), as well as a low-luminosity AGN gonzalez09 . In a Hubble Space Telescope (HST) spectrum of the <0".2 inner region of M89, cappellari99 detected broad emission lines ( 3000 km/s) of both forbidden and permitted transitions, confirming the presence of an AGN. This central region is also known to show significant emission variability ( cappellari99,maoz05 ) on the timescale of years, which supports its AGN nature.

sources.summary: The paper uses M89 as a Virgo example where stripped X-ray gas and CGM density imply cloud-crushing stripping with a possible CGM survival time near 1.2 Gyr.

quotes.quote: Machacek2006 , using Chandra observations, find that M89 shows an X-ray bright tail of stripped gas at 0.5 \ keV . They find that the tail and core of M89 has a gas density of about (5.4 1.7) 10^ 3 \ cm^ 3 and 10^ 2 \ cm^ 3 , respectively. The line-of-sight velocity of M89 is 1700 \ km \ s^ 1 with respect to the Virgo cluster center (M87) and it is at a projected distance of 350 kpc i.e., 0.32 \ r_ vir (given that the line-of-sight velocity is high, the actual distance of M89 can the projected distance). Roediger2015a, Roediger2015b simulate possible orbits of M89, considering a pericentric distance comparable to its present location.

Within this orbit, the gas in M89 is on an average denser ( n_e>10^ 4 \ cm^ 3 at 100 \ kpc ; cf. Figure 11 there) than the surrounding ICM. This suggests that M89 is undergoing stripping in the `cloud crushing' regime. At a mass of M_ DM =5 10^ 12 \ M_ within r_ 200 \ kpc , assuming a of 10 and a relative velocity v_ rel =1600 \ km \ s^ 1 , the CGM of M89 can survive at least a drag time t_ drag = R_ 200 /v_ rel 1.2 \ Gyr .

sources.summary: The paper presents NGC 4552 as a Virgo elliptical with a variable central mini-AGN, broad emission lines, and evidence for sporadic accretion onto a central black hole.

quotes.quote: Yet another promising technique is offered by the case of the Virgo elliptical NGC 4552, in which early HST/FOC observations revealed a central low-luminosity flare. Subsequent HST/FOS observations with a 0.21 arcsec aperture have revealed a rich emission-line spectrum with broad and narrow components with FWHM of 3000 and 700 km/s, respectively. This variable, mini-AGN at the center of NGC 4552 is most naturally the result of a sporadic accretion event on a central BH.

Comparison with another FOC image of NGC 4552 taken in 1991 [ 2 ] showed that this spike had increased its luminosity in the U band (F342W) by a factor 7 1.5 between the two epochs, reaching 10^6 (Paper I). A second point-like source is also present in the 1991 image, 0''.14 from the central spike and with nearly the same luminosity. While both sources were detected at the 4 level in the 1991 image, the offcenter source was not detectable in the 1993 image at the 2.5 level. In Paper I several possible interpretations were discussed for the origin of this flare at the center of NGC 4552, favoring an accretion event onto a central massive black hole (BH).

sources.summary: The paper focuses on Chandra observations of NGC 4552, interpreting ring-like X-ray features and hot central gas as evidence for a recent weak-AGN nuclear outburst.

quotes.quote: We use a 54.4 ks Chandra observation to study nuclear outflow activity in NGC 4552 (M89), an elliptical galaxy in the Virgo Cluster. Chandra images in the 0.5-2 keV band show two ring-like features 1.7 kpc in diameter in the core of NGC 4552, as reported previously by Filho We use spherically symmetric point explosion shock models to argue that the shape of the surface brightness profile across the rims of the rings and the temperature of hot gas in the rings are consistent with a Mach 1.7 shock carrying mean mechanical power L_ shock 3 10^ 41 produced by a 1.4 10^ 55 ergs nuclear outburst 1 - 2 Myr ago. We find the gas temperature in the central 100 pc of the galaxy to be 1.0 0.2 keV, hotter than elsewhere in the galaxy, suggesting that we may be observing directly the reheating of the galaxy ISM by the outburst.

In this work we use a 54.4 ks Chandra observation to study nuclear outburst activity in one such galaxy, NGC 4552 ( = 12^h35^m39.8^s , =12^ 33'23'' , J2000), an elliptical galaxy located 72' east of M87 in subcluster A of the Virgo cluster. In a companion paper (Machacek 2006, hereafter called Paper I) we use the same Chandra observation to measure the properties of hot gas in the outer regions of NGC 4552, showing that these properties are consistent with that expected for gas being ram-pressure stripped due to NGC 4552's supersonic motion through the Virgo ICM. NGC 4552 is a member of the large sample of nearby elliptical galaxies used to establish correlations between X-ray emission and other ISM tracers, so that its global X-ray properties are well studied (for a brief review, see Paper I and references therein). Studies of the nuclear properties of NGC 4552, across a wide range of wavelengths, strongly suggest the presence of a weak AGN at its center.

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC4552 as the gas-rich, optically luminous exception to sample winds, with a gaseous corona and ram-pressure stripping in Virgo suppressing or removing hot gas.

quotes.quote: The only exception to this is NGC4552 (M89), ,bb=10 142 570 700,clip f6.eps which is the most optically luminous galaxy in our sample, has the highest gas content, and is located in the densest environment. The Chandra image of NGC4552 shows that is has a significant gaseous corona and that it is presently being ram pressure stripped of its gas as it falls into the Virgo cluster (Machacek et al. 2005).

NGC 4552 is the only galaxy in our sample without a galactic wind. NGC4552 and NGC3115 have the lowest ratios of E_ SNIa to W in our sample with values of 2.0 and 1.9 (see Table 7). Given the uncertainties in E_ SNIa and W , it is possible that SN Ia are energetically incapable of driving a wind in NGC4552. While NGC4552 has the highest gas content in our sample, it is still less than that found in more luminous early-type galaxies. ,bb=10 142 570 700,clip f8.eps The Chandra image shows that the low gas content in NGC4552 is due to ram pressure stripping of its gas as it falls into the Virgo cluster.

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 4552 as a Virgo outlier in K-band SBF versus UV color, then notes alternative near-infrared SBF measurements that would remove the deviation.

quotes.quote: Note the outlying cases of NGC 1387, NGC 4552, and NGC 1389 with an infrared effective magnitude that is 0.7 mag too bright for their UV excess. The relevant case of the merger radio-galaxy NGC 1316 (Fornax A) is also singled out in the plot, where the arrow indicates that a ``bluer'' (1550- V ) color might be more appropriate for this galaxy (with a more negligible impact on K _s , though), as a consequence of a strong internal absorption due to the observed presence of dust lanes. Excluding these controversial objects (discussed in more detail in Sec. 4), the data sample correlates fairly well ( = -0.75 , see arrow), indicating a less deployed AGB for UV-enhanced galaxies.

If galaxy mass ( alias _v ) is considered as the leading physical parameter to compare outlier location with respect to the bulk of the galaxy distribution (see, for instance Fig. 7), one must conclude that both NGC 4552 and NGC 1389 seem to have a brighter AGB tip rather than a bluer (1550-V) color. With regard to NGC 1389, liu02 find that the K_s SBFs of NGC 1389 are also too bright compared to its ( V - I ) color, a fact that would be consistent with either a higher than average metallicity given the age of its most recent burst of star formation or a longer lifetime of its TP-AGB stars mouhcine05 . In the case of NGC 4552, however, jens96 contribute an interesting piece of information. These authors measure near-IR SBFs for several galaxies in Virgo, and their results are systematically fainter than those obtained by pahr94 . Unfortunately, the two groups use slightly different filters (Jensen et al. \ employ K^ , vs. \ K_s of Pahre Mould), but the discrepancy is larger than can be ascribed to the effect of the filters. jens96 attribute the difference to the higher S/N ratio of their data; the S/N ratio of the liu02 images is similar to that of Jensen et al.'s. In particular, Jensen et al. \ find K^ = -5.51 for NGC 4552; assuming K^ K_s , NGC 4552 would no longer be deviant in the K_s vs. \ ( 1500 - V ) plane.

sources.summary: The paper finds low-surface-brightness color pixels and a strong i-H gradient in NGC 4552, suggesting PSF-scattering systematics in its blue halo.

quotes.quote: Few pixels have colours are not covered by models, in NGC 4552, NGC 3521 and NGC 5713. Their M/L ratio must be derived via extrapolation (minimum surface curvature fitting). We have scrutinized these pixels that lie blue-ward of the model contours (in i-H ) and found that they are from the lowest surface brightness regions.

Further, in NGC 4552 the data shows at face-value a strong i-H colour gradient, with bluer values at larger radii, which does not correspond to any similar trend in g-i . A non-uniform background cannot explain this effect, since the strength of this gradient appears the same at different position angles. A metallicity gradient can also be invoked, but not as a full explanation, since we do have low metallicity models in our library and yet we are unable to recover such blue i-H . and have pointed out in the past that optical thinned CCDs can have very extended PSF wings (especially in i band), up to arcminute scales.
We can speculate that large-angle PSF wings from scattering in i (and g , but not in H ) band cause the ``blue'' i-H halo around NGC 4552 (and possibly the blue ``halo'' around NGC 3521 and 5713).

sources.summary: The paper considers M89 a strong but weaker phase-reference candidate than M84, based on its radio SED, compact core dominance, and inferred 230/345 GHz fluxes.

quotes.quote: M89 is somewhat closer to M87, at an angular separation of roughly 1.19^ . The radio SED of M89 is shown in Figure fig:M87SED , in which all of the data points were taken from the literature Hees-Conk:75,Sram:75,Dres-Cond:78,Beck-Whit-Edwa:91,Greg-Cond:91,Naga-Wils-Falc:01,Cond-Cott-Brod:02,Filh_etal:04,Voll-Thie-Wiel:04,Doi_etal:05,Naga-Falc-Wils:05,Heal_etal:07,Brau_etal:07 As with M84, there is a paucity of VLBI observations, in this case only the one by Naga-Falc-Wils:05 , though the resulting core flux density is similar to VLA observations at the same frequency, differing by roughly 30 cf. Beck-Whit-Edwa:91,Greg-Cond:91,Sram:75,Naga-Wils-Falc:01 . This again suggests that the lower-resolution observations are dominated by the compact core.

Therefore, to infer the 230 and 345 flux densities we fit a single power-law to all of the M89 radio data. This results in values that are consistent with the 347 upper limit obtained by Doi_etal:05 ; sufficiently bright for the EHT-C, and only marginal for the EHT-I. Thus, we also consider this a strong candidate mm-VLBI phase reference candidate, though clearly less so than M84.

sources.summary: NGC 4552 is modeled with Bondi and jet-power estimates from X-ray profiles and cavities; a jet-dominated model fits radio/X-ray data but overpredicts optical light.

quotes.quote: NGC 4552 The Bondi accretion rate was estimated by Merloni07 (see also Allen06 ) from the X-ray profiles of density and temperature. The kinetic power carried by the jet was estimated by Merloni07 (see also Allen06 ) from the energy deposited in the X-ray cavities. Since the X-ray spectrum is quite soft, AD models are unable to account for the X-ray emission. The JD model in Fig. fig:n4552 is roughly consistent with the radio observations and explains quite well the X-ray data, but overpredicts the optical data. The resulting jet power is roughly consistent with the value estimated by Allen06,Merloni07 .

A likely explanation for the unusually faint UV emission compared to the X-rays is source variability Maoz05 and hence we did not attempt to fit the 2500 and 3300 measurements with a stellar population as in the other LLAGNs.

sources.summary: The paper models NGC 4552 as a nearby dust-rich early-type galaxy whose quasi face-on dust disk can mimic a core and optical/UV point source, complicating AGN interpretation.

quotes.quote: ID & Reference & [ hh:mm:ss ] & [ dd:mm:ss ] & [ Mpc ] & & [ s ] & & 1 (1) & (2) & (3) & (4) & (5) & (6) & (7) & (8) NGC 4552 & 12:35:39.8 & +12:33:23 & 16.0 & ACS/ F475W & 750 & 9401 & NGC 4494 & 12:31:24.1 & +25:46:31 & 13.7 & WFPC2/ F555W & 1000 & 5454 & The test galaxies for our study are NGC 4552 (M 89, UGC 7760, VCC 1632; Virgo) and NGC 4494 (UGC 7662; Coma). Deep images clearly revealed that these objects host central disk- or ring-like dust structures (see Figure figure:fit_models ). In particular, NGC 4552 hosts a quasi face-on disk surrounded by several dust streams which spread radially beyond the effective radius of the galaxy ferrarese:2006 . The structure in NGC 4494, first observed by , resembles instead a more edge-on ring, or better a torus with a large inner hole and a small opening angle.

We applied our methodology to NGC 4552 and NGC 4494, two ETGs reported to host LLAGNs, and initially chosen to be representative of the core and core- less class (respectively). However, our analysis revealed that NGC 4552 does not host a core nor shows photometric indications for a central AGN --- at variance with previous studies based on similar optical imaging. Instead, it showed that dust-obscuration mimicked the presence of a core, while the central point-like luminosity was simply motivated by the stellar emission shining through the central hole of the dust disk (Figure figure:fit_models ).

sources.summary: The paper reports that NGC 4552 has compact radio and hard X-ray nuclear emission, with properties consistent with a weak AGN and possible jet contribution.

quotes.quote: The present new and published VLBA data show unresolved cores in NGC 524 (Fig. 1 a ), NGC 4552 (Nagar 2002) and NGC 5354 (Fig. 1 b, ). Some sources also show evidence of radio variability, as seems the case for NGC 4552 (see discussion in Filho, Barthel Ho 2000). Furthermore, all VLBA detected sample sources (including NGC 4552; Nagar 2002 and NGC 5866; Falcke 2000) show flat spectrum radio cores, although the inverted spectrum in NGC 524 is most probably an effect of resolution mismatch.

NGC 4552 The soft X-ray emission extends almost to the limits of the optical galaxy ( 2 ), exhibiting a somewhat hourglass structure. We find a strong hard X-ray nuclear source, coincident with the radio core position (Nagar 2002; Filho, Barthel Ho 2000). The spectrum of the nuclear source (1200 net counts) yields a satisfactory fit with a two component model -- power law ( =1.51) plus Raymond-Smith thermal plasma (kT=0.95 keV).

sources.summary: The paper analyzes NGC 4552 as a luminous Virgo elliptical in the survey field, deriving its globular-cluster luminosity-function turnover, color dependence, and specific frequency.

quotes.quote: Here we describe the observations, data reduction, and data analysis and present luminosity functions of GC populations around M87 and NGC 4552, another luminous Virgo elliptical in our survey field.

By fitting Gaussians to the GCLFs, the V band turnover magnitude ( V_ TO ) is estimated to be 23.62 0.06 mag and 23.56 0.20 mag for the GC population in M87 and NGC 4552, respectively. The GCLF is found to be a function of GC colour; V_ TO of the red GC subpopulation ( V-I > 1.1 ) is fainter than that of the blue GC subpopulation ( V-I 1.1 ) in both M87 and NGC 4552, as expected if the colour differences are primarily due to a metallicity effect and the mass functions of the two subpopulations are similar.

We also estimate GC specific frequencies ( S_N ) for M87 and NGC 4552. The S_N of the M87 GC population is estimated to be 12.5 0.8 within 25 ^ . The S_N value of the NGC 4552 GC population is estimated to be 5.0 0.6 within 10^ .

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 4552 as an anomalous elliptical whose pixel SEDs are almost pure stellar continua, with one dominant component and colour gradients likely linked to stellar metallicity.

quotes.quote: Contrary to all other galaxies, the PCA of the elliptical NGC 4552 results in PC1 being responsible for most (89 ) of the total SED variance. All pixels in NGC 4552 have SEDs very close to a pure stellar continuum, with no evidence for dust/PAH emission and the IR part being consistent with a Rayleigh-Jeans tail. PC1 affects the SED in the sense of changing the ``curvature'', i.e. of enhancing or decreasing at the same time all bands relative to H . PC1 has a clear gradient, such that the central regions have redder optical- H colours and bluer H IR colours, while the outskirts display the opposite. This kind of colour gradients are well known in the optical and NIR see, e.g., michard_00,michard_05 and are usually well explained by metallicity gradients kobayashi_arimoto_99, henry_worthey_99 , plus possibly minor contributions of age gradients as well. Our analysis shows that there is only one parameter driving the SED variations in NGC 4552, and this is likely to be linked to the metallicity of the stellar population.

sources.summary: NGC 4552 is treated as a UV-upturn early-type galaxy whose FUV/NUV profiles favor metal-rich stellar-population models; metal-poor models fail, while helium-enhanced models have been claimed to reproduce its spectra.

quotes.quote: Figure fig:mfit1 shows the observed FUV NUV and FUV V colour profiles (filled symbols) and the best-fit model profiles (solid lines) of NGC 4552 (blue triangle), 4278 (black circle) and 4477 (red diamond). Three open symbols of each galaxy represent the three fitted regions.

Metal-poor models, suggesting that the dominant UV sources are older and metal-poor HB stars and their post-HB progeny (see Section sec:intro ; ldz94,pl97,bg08 ), fail to reproduce the observed surface brightnesses of UV upturn galaxies (e.g. NGC 4552 and 4278) when we consider age and metallicity gradients, as shown in Figure fig:mfit2 , although this depends on the adopted models see e.g. pl97 . We note that the model-fit for UV weak galaxy (e.g. NGC 4477) based on metal-poor models is also reasonably good. A number of recent studies found peculiar globular clusters having EHB stars that can be explained by the presence of super-helium-rich populations letal05, lgc07 , which could also be a copious source of FUV flux. Indeed, cyl11 claimed that their models with helium-enhanced subpopulations can reproduce the observed spectra of NGC 4552 and 4649.

In the context of the yi03 models, the UV upturn is a stellar phenomenon associated with the metal-rich (high \ line strength) population of early-type galaxies, and the UV-bright central regions of giant elliptical galaxies (e.g. NGC 4552 and 4278) are enhanced in metals by roughly 60 compared to the UV-weak parts.

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 4552 as a detected compact radio LLAGN whose newly observed twin parsec-scale extensions indicate jet-like structure among the brightest mas-resolution compact radio sources.

quotes.quote: Of the detected nuclei, mas-scale radio cores were known to exist in NGC 4374 and NGC 4552 e.g. jonet81 .

Among the newly observed sources, the two with the highest peak flux-density at 6 cm, NGC 4374 (M 84) and NGC 4552 (M 89), clearly show extended structures (Fig. vlbamaps ) suggestive of parsec-scale ``jets.'' The pc-scale extension in NGC 4374 detected earlier by wroet96 is aligned with the larger kpc-scale FR-I radio lobes e.g. laibri87 , while the twin pc-scale extensions in NGC 4552 are, to our knowledge, the first time such structure has been detected in this galaxy. As pointed out in naget01 , of the sixteen objects in Table tabll16 the five with the highest 6 cm peak flux-densities at mas resolution -- NGC 3031 M 81; bieet00 , NGC 4278 jonet84,falet00 , NGC 4486 M 87; junbir95 , NGC 4374 M 84; this work wroet96 , and NGC 4552 (M89; this work) -- all have one- or two-sided pc-scale extensions, suggestive of jets. For these sources the peak flux-density of the extended emission is typically 10 -25 of the peak flux-density of the core.

sources.summary: The paper reports NGC 4552 UV imaging with no patchy star formation but a variable central point source, interpreted as a faint mini-AGN and possible black-hole flare.

quotes.quote: In 1993 FOC imaging in four UV bands was obtained of the central regions of the ellipticals NGC 1399 and NGC 4552 and of the bulge of the NGC 2681 spiral (PI F. Bertola). The aim was to study the spacial structure of the UV emission, checking for color gradients and if any patcheness existed due to star formation. No such patcheness was found neither strong color gradients, but instead NGC 4552 and NGC 2681 showed a central, unresolved, point-like source (Bertola et al. 1995b). To our surprize, we found that the point-like source in NGC 4552 had changed its brightness by a factor 7 1.5 in the F342W band, compared to a previous FOC image taken in 1991: a central flare had been discovered, possibly due to a red giant having been tidally stripped by a massive central black hole (Renzini et al. 1995). Subsequent, post-COSTAR FOC imaging and FOS spectroscopy confirmed that the central source is a variable mini-AGN, possibly the faintest known AGN, with broad (FWHM 3000 km s ^ 1 ) emission lines (Cappellari et al. 1998).

sources.summary: The paper compares NGC 4552 ultraviolet spectra with composite stellar-population models and says helium-enhanced hot horizontal-branch stars can reproduce its far-UV upturn and 2500 dip.

quotes.quote: Figure 3 presents our composite models constructed with and without helium-enhanced subpopulations compared with the SEDs observed by IUE satellite for two giant elliptical galaxies NGC 4552 and NGC 4649. In our models, the value of mean metallicity ( Fe/H ) was chosen so that they can reproduce the observed Mg b ( 4.6 ) index of theses galaxies (see also Figure 4), assuming that [ /Fe ] is 0.3 in giant elliptical galaxies 1992ApJ...398...69W, 2005ApJ...621..673T, 2009ApJS..182..216K . The parameters adopted in our models presented in Figure 3 are listed in Table 1. It is clear from Figure 3 that our models with helium-enhanced subpopulations can naturally reproduce not only the strong far-UV upturns but also the 2500 dips observed in NGC 4552 and NGC 4649, while the models without helium-enhanced subpopulations fail to reproduce the far-UV upturns, unless the age of underlying stellar population is increased to 16 Gyrs Park97, 1999ApJ...513..128Y .

sources.summary: The paper uses X-ray-derived Bondi and jet-power estimates for NGC 4552, finding AD models cannot fit the soft X-rays while a JD model fits radio and X-ray data.

quotes.quote: NGC 4552 The Bondi accretion rate was estimated by merloni07 (see also allen06 ) from the X-ray profiles of density and temperature. The kinetic power carried by the jet was estimated by merloni07 (see also allen06 ) from the energy deposited in the X-ray cavities. Since the X-ray spectrum is quite soft, AD models are unable to account for the X-ray emission. The JD model in Fig. fig:seds05 is roughly consistent with the radio observations and explains quite well the X-ray data, but overpredicts the optical data.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 4552 as a core early-type galaxy with a post-star-forming MIR nucleus, gas, anomalous PAHs, fine structure, and late star formation despite AGN feedback.

quotes.quote: The MIR spectra indicate nuclei with different kinds of activity from AGN (class-4, M87 and NGC 4261) to post-star forming (MIR class-2 with anomalous PAHs, NGC 4374, NGC 4636, NGC 4552) to the quiescent nucleus of NGC 4365 (class-0). Both SR, the majority, and FR show signatures of kinematical and morphological peculiarities suggesting the action of accretion episodes. Of particular interest is NGC 4382, which although showing strong shell features (see e.g. figure 9 in Kormendy09 ) has a nearly passively evolving nucleus (class-1). This is not the case for NGC 4374 and NGC 4552, that both show fine structure see Sansom00 , although less prominent than that in NGC 4382. The class-2 spectrum of NGC 4552 reveals the presence of gas and the additional presence of anomalous PAHs, as in NGC 4374, points to the occurrence of a recent star formation event.
AGN feedback does not prevent a late star formation episode in the bright Es NGC 4374, NGC 4636 and NGC 4552.

sources.summary: The paper finds a strong IMF radial gradient in massive ETG NGC 4552, with a bottom-heavy central IMF becoming closer to Kroupa-like at larger radius.

quotes.quote: img:gamma , where the radial trends of bimodal IMF slope, _b , are presented for both NGC 4552 and NGC 4387, respectively. For the massive galaxy NGC 4552, the IMF slope decreases, by _ b =-1.5 , from the centre to 0.7 . In the innermost radial bins, the inferred IMF slope ( _ b =3.05 0.2 ), implies a bottom-heavy distribution, consistent with previous work. In constrast, for the outermost bin, the best-fit ( _ b =1.9 0.4 ) is higher, but still consistent at the 1.5 level, with the value corresponding to a Kroupa-like distribution ( _ b 1.35 ).

sources.summary: M 89 is discussed as an elliptical with Fe XVII-dominated spectra, O VII excess in the forbidden line, and a low O VII resonant-to-forbidden ratio.

quotes.quote: Fig:Spectra_ratios we show the RGS spectra of three interesting sources, the Centaurus cluster, M 84 and M 89 (from top to bottom).

The M 84 and M 89 elliptical galaxies, whose spectra are dominated by the Fe XVII lines, show an O VII excess with respect to the two-phase cie model. Interestingly, in M 84 (and NGC 5846) the O VII resonant line at 21.6 is in excess, while in M 89 (and NGC 4636) the excess is shown by the forbidden line at 22.1 .
We found (/f) line ratios lower than 1.25 in the RGS spectra of NGC 4636, M 89, and NGC 1404 as already shown in Pinto2014 .

sources.summary: The paper models NGC 4552 with an outer-region narrow component because its sulfur lines are blended, and reports a very broad H component absent from C15.

quotes.quote: For the HST /STIS spectra of two galaxies (namely NGC 4374 and NGC 4552) the [ S II ] lines were so blended and the [ O I ] lines almost absent, that it was challenging to perform the modeling using them as reference (Fig. Panel_NGC4374 and Panel_NGC4552 ). Thus we decided to fit the spectrum of a region situated a few pixels away from the nucleus and use this result to model the narrow component of the nuclear region. This was motivated by the assumption that the narrow component is tracing the kinematics of the disc of the galaxy, thus it should also be visible in regions outside the very center of the system.

For three galaxies (NGC 4374, NGC 4552 and NGC 4698; Fig. Panel_NGC4374 , Panel_NGC4552 , Panel_NGC4698 , respectively) we derive a very broad component ( > 1300 km s ^ 1 ) that C15 do not report in their analysis.

sources.summary: The paper identifies a bright hard central source in NGC 4552, supported by radio detection and UV variability, and concludes its X-rays mostly come from an AGN.

quotes.quote: NGC 4552 (T2, E1) has a bright, hard central source, plus four other point sources in its central kiloparsec and soft diffuse emission. The spectrum of the central source is well fitted by a power-law or a comptonized blackbody. These models give a luminosity of L_ 0.5-10 keV =3.7 10^ 39 erg s ^ 1 . This source was clearly detected at 4.85 GHz with S_ =64 mJy and a spectral index p=-0.23 Cal91 so we calculate R_ X = -1.5 , which means that this source is radio loud.

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 4552 as a luminous Virgo elliptical whose globular-cluster colors, red and blue spatial distributions, and distance help model the survey field around M87.

quotes.quote: By investigating the GC colour distribution as a function of distance from M87 and NGC 4552, another luminous Virgo elliptical in our survey field, it is found that clear bimodality ( (V-I)_ peak 1.0 and 1.2) is seen only in the inner regions ( 10 kpc) of the host galaxies and that it becomes weaker with radius due to the decreasing contribution of the red GC ( V-I > 1.1 ) subpopulation. It is also found (both around M87 and NGC 4552) that while the spatial distribution of the red GCs is as centrally concentrated as the host galaxy halo light distribution, the distribution of the blue GCs ( V-I 1.1 ) tends to be more extended.
We model the radial profile of GC surface densities out to 0.5 Mpc from M87 by a superposition of the GC populations associated with M87 and with NGC 4552.

We adopt a distance of 16.1 Mpc (distance modulus of 31.03) to M87, and 15.4 Mpc (distance modulus of 30.93) to NGC 4552, based on measurements using the surface brightness fluctuation method (Tonry et al. 2001).

sources.summary: NGC 4552 is described as a Virgo elliptical moving supersonically through the cluster, likely affected by ram-pressure stripping, with nuclear emission and double lobes from past low-power AGN activity.

quotes.quote: This is an elliptical galaxy belonging to ``Sub-cluster A'', located East of the Virgo Cluster (see Machacek et al. . Its low radial velocity implies that it is moving supersonically through the Virgo Cluster, thus making it a prime candidate for (former) ram-pressure stripping. Our radio continuum map is the first obtained for this galaxy and shows nuclear emission and a double lobe along PA 80 . Such a double lobe is suggestive of previous activity from what is now a low power AGN in the centre of this galaxy (Cappellari et al. ).

sources.summary: The paper cites NGC4552 as a galaxy with no evident extended radio emission but with outflow signatures, a weak radio core, X-ray rings and cavities, and hot central gas.

quotes.quote: There are also a few galaxies without currently evident extended radio emission, but with signs of an outflow and hot central gas, as NGC4552 (Machacek et al. 2006). This galaxy shows a weak core radio source unresolved by the VLBA, and in the (unsharp masked) X-ray image two conspicuous ringlike features at 1.3 kpc from the galaxy center, surrounding two cavities; these features have been found consistent with shocked gas driven outward by recent nuclear activity, as in a bipolar nuclear outflow. The gas temperature in the central 100 pc of the galaxy is 1 0.2 keV, hotter than elsewhere in the galaxy, suggesting that we may be directly observing the reheating of the galaxy ISM by the outburst (Machacek et al. 2006).

sources.summary: The paper summarizes NGC4552 as M89, an S0 galaxy whose Sersic or bulge/disk model results vary strongly between 1D, core-Sersic, and 2D decompositions.

quotes.quote: NGC4552 (M89) tex:CC93 first profiled this S0 galaxy with a single S e rsic index of n=13.87 excluding the inner 2   arcsec while KF09 applied the same model and found n=9.1 . tex:FC06a applied a core-S e rsic model and found n_ g =7.1 . Finally in two-dimension modelling tex:D01 and H09 used a bulge/disk model ( n_ bulge =4.2,4.6 ) yielding a much lower n value.

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 4552 as a multicomponent core case whose inner excess is uncertain and whose inferred deficit becomes far smaller than in a single-Sersic fit.

quotes.quote: (In the cases of NGC 1407 and NGC 4552, it is not clear that their inner excesses can be modeled with a Gaussian, so we exclude the data at < 0.16 for NGC 1407 and < 0.04 for NGC 4552.) For six galaxies (including NGC 1407), we find that significantly better fits are achieved by including one or two extra \ or exponential components, representing outer halos.
In the end we found that approximately one fourth of our sample galaxies were best fit using multiple components: NGC 1399, NGC 1407, NGC 4552, NGC 5516, NGC 5813, and NGC 6086.

The most common effect is to lower the estimated deficit (the median deficit is 0.44 times what it is when a single-- \ fit is used), with an extreme in the case of NGC 4552, for which the deficit is only 0.9 of the value for the single-- \ fit.

sources.summary: The paper treats VCC 1632 as a Type 1b globular-cluster color-distribution example and notes its active nucleus, nuclear outflow, and stripped interstellar gas.

quotes.quote: VCC 798 and VCC 1632 show the typical GC CDF shapes of this type.

VCC 1632 (NGC 4552). This lenticular galaxy is well known for its active galactic nucleus 1999ApJ...519..117C and nuclear outflow activity 2006ApJ...648..947M . 2006ApJ...644..155M found a gas-stripping feature in the galaxy's interstellar medium with a Chandra observation.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 4552, M 89, as a Virgo giant elliptical with point-like inner radio emission, diffuse butterfly-like emission, jets, and X-ray cavities.

quotes.quote: NGC 4552 The radio emission from the giant elliptical NGC 4552 or M 89, which resides inside of the subgroup A of the Virgo cluster, is presented in Figure fig:chandra_vla_c b. \ The red contours are showing the innermost radio point-like emission from the archival high resolution VLA A configuration. More recent (Project ID: 16A-275) previously unpublished C configuration data revealed a more diffuse butterfly-like emission extending on both sides to about 0.8 kpc.

sources.summary: The paper highlights NGC 4552 as a Virgo-cluster ETG with a massive NSC and SMBH whose NSC mass behavior may reflect SMBH-inhibited cluster growth.

quotes.quote: One of those ETGs is NGC 4552 (M 89 or VCC 1632), an ETG in the Virgo cluster, in which did not report a stellar nucleus due to the presence of dust, but reported a NSC with M_ NSC = 9 10^7 M_ . NGC 4552 is also known to host a supermassive black hole (SMBH) with M_ BH = 5 10^8 M_ Gebhardt2003 . On the other hand, the two ETGs NGC 4552 and IC 1459 discussed above now stand out for having larger f_ in, NSC values than other galaxies with log (M_ NSC /M_ GCS ) between 1.5 and 1.0 .

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 4552 as M89, a Virgo elliptical with a kinematically distinct core, statistically significant possible bimodality, and a fairly continuous globular-cluster color distribution.

quotes.quote: NGC 4552 This Virgo elliptical is also known as M89, and is known to posses a kinematically distinct core sim97 . kun99 found possible bimodality, which we confirm as statistically significant with the same mean colors (to within 0.01).

Some galaxies exhibit strikingly bimodal color distributions with roughly equal numbers of blue and red GCs (like e.g. \ NGC 1404, NGC 4649, NGC 4472), while others show a much reduced number of red GCs (NGC 4406) or even just a single (but still significantly broadened) metal-poor component (NGC 4365), and still others appear to show a fairly continuous color distribution, spanning a range similar to that observed in the truly bimodal systems (NGC 4552).

sources.summary: The paper includes NGC 4552 as a low-luminosity AGN with variable HST UV measurements and Chandra nuclear X-ray fluxes.

quotes.quote: The various UV measurements of this nucleus with HST are: 1.5 10^ 17 (at 2300 ) and 1.8 10^ 17 (at 2800 ), with HST/FOC in 1993 (Cappellari et al. 1999); 2 10^ 17 at 2500 with HST/FOS in 1996 (Cappellari et al. 1999); M05 observed it at two epochs between which it brightened by 20 per cent in both F250W and F330W. I adopt the means of the measurements of M05, 2 10^ 17 (F250W), and 1.5 10^ 17 (F330W), and take 20 per cent of them as lower limits on the AGN flux. Flohic et al. (2006) used Chandra to derive unabsorbed fluxes for the nuclear source of 5.0 10^ 14 at 0.5--2 keV, and 6.0 10^ 14 at 2--10 keV, and a photon index of 2.0 0.2 .

gmw_00000464.name: HG031203
gmw_00000464.overview: HG031203 is a compact, metal-poor galaxy at a redshift of approximately 0.105 [arXiv:0709.0198, arXiv:astro-ph/0405449] that resembles active blue compact dwarf galaxies [arXiv:1010.1783]. It exhibits extreme star formation [arXiv:1010.1783], potential active galactic nucleus contamination [arXiv:1901.00872], and variable emission associated with a supernova [arXiv:astro-ph/0405449].

sources.summary: The paper characterizes HG 031203 as a nearby, metal-poor, actively star-forming GRB host with measured reddening, nebular conditions, low metallicity, and doubtful Wolf-Rayet classification.

quotes.quote: The host galaxy of the long-duration gamma-ray burst (GRB) 031203 (HG 031203) offers a precious opportunity to study in detail the environment of a nearby GRB. The aim is to better characterize this galaxy and analyse the possible evolution with time of the spectroscopic quantities we derive. We analyse HG 031203 using a set of optical spectra acquired with the ESO-VLT and Keck telescope. We compare the metallicity, luminosity and star formation properties of this galaxy and of the other supernova-long gamma-ray burst hosts in the local universe ( z<0.2 ) against the KPNO International Spectroscopic Survey. HG 031203 is a metal poor, actively star forming galaxy (star formation rate of 12.9 2.2 M_ yr^ 1 ) at z=0.1054 .

We find an interstellar medium temperature of 12500 K and an electronic density of N_ e =160 cm^ 3 . After investigating for possible Wolf-Rayet emission features in our spectra, we consider dubious the classification of HG 031203 as a Wolf-Rayet galaxy.

On the other hand, we find that half of the hosts follows the metallicity-luminosity relation found for star-burst galaxies; HG031203 is a clear outlier, with its really low metallicity ( 12+ ( O/H ) =8.12 0.04 ).

sources.summary: The paper presents HG 031203 as an extreme star-forming GRB host resembling active blue compact dwarf galaxies, with hard radiation, hot dust emission, young stellar age, and UV-optical colors like z-band dropouts.

quotes.quote: The strong nebular emission lines, extreme hard line ratios, lack of PAH emission features, and probable hot dust temperature, suggest a close similarity between HG 031203 and blue compact dwarf galaxies. This suggestion was made by 2005NewA...11..103S when analysing the optical properties of low redshift SN-GRB host galaxies, including HG 031203. In particular, HG 031203 is similar to those classified as being in an ``active'' mode of star-formation e.g. 2005A&A...434..849H . The IR line ratios in fact, suggest that HG 031203 is among the more extreme BCDs in terms of the hardness of its radiation field (Fig. fig:line_ratios ).

We conclude that HG 031203 is an extreme star-forming galaxy with a radiation field among the hardest known for such a galaxy, in both its obscured and unobscured regions. The broadband SED of HG 031203 has a moderate luminosity dust emission peak, likely with a high temperature, similar to local blue compact dwarf galaxies. If dust can be formed in association with supernovae, it is possible that such a dust peak may exist even in very young galaxies in the early universe, making such galaxies potentially much easier to detect with JWST . The lack of a 4000 \ break and the high-ionisation emission line ratios in the MIR spectrum of HG 031203 suggests a dominant stellar age 10 Myr. HG 031203 has a UV-optical SED similar to z band dropout galaxies at z 7 .

sources.summary: The paper models spectra of the GRB031203 host across multiple epochs, attributing narrow and broad line components to starburst debris, winds, shocks, and photoionization.

quotes.quote: The detailed modelling of the spectra observed from the long GRB031203 host galaxy at different epochs during the 2003-2009 years is presented. The line profiles show FWHM of 100 . A broad line profile with FWHM 400 appears in the line sockets from the 2009 observations.

We suggest that the narrow lines show the velocity of star-burst (SB) debris, while the broad ones are due to the wind from SB stars.
