sources.summary: The review discusses Tol1326-379 as a compact FR 0 radio galaxy associated with a 3LAC source, with FR I-like properties, steep gamma-ray spectrum, and likely large orientation angle.

quotes.quote: proposed the association of a 3LAC source with the FR 0 Tol1326-379, offering the opportunity to investigate for the first time the behavior of these objects in the high-energy domain. FR 0s are similar to FR Is in the optical and X-ray properties, i.e. they are classified as low-excitation galaxies and their X-ray radiation is of non-thermal origin, likely produced by the jet ( ). In rays, the flux of Tol1326-379 is similar to FR Is, F_ (>1 GeV) =(3.1 0.8) 10^ 10 ph cm^ 2 s^ 1 but the spectrum is steeper, =2.78 0.14. The SED of Tol1326-379 resembles that of Cen A, known to lie close to the plane of the sky, 50-80^ . Therefore, also for the compact FR 0 source the orientation angle could be large and the process responsible for the ray emission is likely the same as extended FR Is.

sources.summary: The review describes Tol 1326-379 as a claimed gamma-ray-detected FR 0 whose radio-GeV SED resembles jet-dominated AGN, is modeled as a misaligned radio galaxy, and has a questioned LAT identification.

quotes.quote: grandi16 claimed the first Fermi ray detection of a FR 0, Tol 1326-379, with a GeV luminosity of 2 10^ 42 erg s ^ 1 , similar to FR Is. Its radio-GeV SED is double-peaked maraschi92 , similar to other jet-dominated RLAGN (Fig. fig:sedtol , see the SEDs of M87, abdo09 , and Cen A, abdalla20 ), where non-thermal synchrotron and inverse-Compton emission dominate in any band over the disc and host emission. While the GeV luminosity reconciles with the detection of local FR Is, the prominent Compton peak, brighter than the synchrotron one, makes this source similar to flat-spectrum radio quasars, while the steep ray spectrum makes it conversely more similar to low-luminosity BL Lacs. Nevertheless, the best scenario which can reproduce the whole SED is a misaligned RG which emits synchrotron and synchrotron self-Compton radiation with a total energy flux of the order of a few 10^ 44 erg s ^ 1 grandi16 .

Unfortunately, no evident FR 0s have been listed among the non-blazar AGN list in the recently released Fourth LAT AGN Catalog (4LAC, fermi20,fermi4 ) and the ray identification of Tol 1326-379 has also been questioned fu22 .

gmw_00000473.name: GSN 069
gmw_00000473.overview: GSN 069 is a low-mass Seyfert 2 galaxy [arXiv:1106.3507, arXiv:2007.06441] that was the first system discovered to exhibit X-ray quasi-periodic eruptions [arXiv:2312.01737, arXiv:2306.14972]. These eruptions recur approximately every nine hours [arXiv:2203.12137, arXiv:2306.14972] and are associated with a long-term tidal disruption event [arXiv:2312.01737, arXiv:2109.03471].

sources.summary: The paper identifies GSN 069 as a strongly variable soft X-ray Seyfert II candidate, arguing that its long-lasting thermal soft spectrum implies an absent rather than obscured BLR.

quotes.quote: During a slew on July 14, 2010 XMM-Newton detected soft X-ray emission at 1.5 0.3 counts s ^ 1 from a position consistent with the nucleus of GSN 069 (also known as 6dFg0119087-341131; z=0.01816) (see Fig. fig:gsnim ). Previous ROSAT survey and pointed observations failed to detect the source and give upper limits, to the unabsorbed flux, 30--360 times fainter than the XMM-Newton slew detection (Table tab:obslog ). Two, consistent, optical spectra, taken in 2001 and 2003 in the 2dF and 6dF surveys show unresolved Balmer lines ( FWHM 200 km s ^ 1 ) and line ratios consistent with a Sy 2 classification (Fig. fig:gsnoptical ). The source has been monitored with the SWIFT-XRT since its discovery.

Discussion The lack of a cold absorber in the X-ray spectrum of GSN 069 is difficult to reconcile with the classical interpretation of a Sy II where the broad lines are removed by absorption. From the 6dF optical spectrum we find a ratio of [ OIII ] 5007 / H _ 11 which is symptomatic of a missing BLR rather than absorption in Sy II . From this we conclude that the BLR is absent rather than obscured in this case. In other words, GSN 069 appears to be another example of an AGN classified as a Sy II galaxy but with little or no intrinsic absorption in the X-rays and with optical line ratios inconsistent with heavy extinction.

sources.summary: The paper treats GSN 069 as a low-mass Seyfert galaxy that brightened after ROSAT non-detection, developed rapid quasi-periodic eruptions, and challenges the simple toy model.

quotes.quote: GSN 069 This relatively low mass Seyfert galaxy ( M_ BH = 4.5 10^5 M_ , Miniutti2019 ) was inactive when measured by ROSAT. In 2010 it showed a spectacular rise in the nuclear luminosity, followed by a slow decay. During the late decay phase, in December 2018, the source showed spectacular rapid large amplitude oscillations with the period of roughly 9 hours Miniutti2019 . The behavior was still observed in February 2019.

We represented the variations in the disk luminosity using our toy model. We assumed the black hole mass of 4.5 10^5 M_ , after Miniutti2019 , and we adjusted the remaining parameters to reproduce the timescale. QPE time separation can be indeed reproduced, although it requires small radius and large value of the viscosity coefficient. The external accretion rate favored by our model (0.013 in Eddington units) is much lower than the bolometric luminosity 0.46 estimated by Miniutti2019 .

sources.summary: The paper models GSN 069 as a low-mass Seyfert QPE source with 9-hour eruptions, inferred black-hole and X-ray properties, and light curves qualitatively fit by disk instability.

quotes.quote: Quasi-periodic eruptions (QPEs) are new phenomena exhibiting quasi-periodic rapid and high-amplitude bursts in soft X-ray, which were reported by 2019Natur.573..381M firstly in a low-mass Seyfert 2 galaxy GSN 069. The burst duration and period of GSN 069 are about 1 and 9 hours, respectively. Except for GSN 069, four other QPE sources, i.e., RX J1301.9+2747 2013ApJ...768..167S, 2020A&A...636L...2G , 2MASS 02314715-1020112, 2MASX J02344872-4419325 Arcodia_2021 and XMMSL1 J024916.6-041244 2021ApJ...921L..40C , have been discovered recently. Compared with others, more physical properties of GSN 069 have been inferred, such as the central black hole mass ( M_ BH 4 10^5 M_ with a factor of few uncertainty), the X-ray spectrum in various stages during outbursts and the nearly constant eruption period with alternating long/short QPE time-separation as well as alternating strong/weak QPEs.

GSN 069 is observed to display QPEs about every 9 hours. During the outbursts, the X-ray flux can increase by about two order of magnitudes 2019Natur.573..381M . We compare the results of our model with the observed quasi-periodic 0.4-2 keV light curve of GSN 069 in Figure fig:light_curve . It is found that our theoretical results can qualitatively fit both the period and the outburst duration, although the alternating strong/weak peaks and short/long recurrence times are not well reproduced.

sources.summary: The paper treats GSN069 as a Seyfert QPE source whose luminosity decline, reappearance, recurrence modulation, and quiescent oscillations can be explained by AGN-disk collisions.

quotes.quote: So far, five QPE events have been reported, whose emission is coming from the central regions of low-mass galaxies harbouring less massive supermassive black holes (SMBHs) with masses of M_ SMBH 10^5 - 10^6 . All of the host galaxies have been classified as active by re-observing them using high-resolution optical spectroscopy Wevers2022 , although no broad emission lines, common in active galactic nuclei (AGNs), have been detected. The first QPE source, GSN069, was reported from a Seyfert-2 galaxy Miniutti2019 . Thereafter, the second and fifth QPE source, RX J1301.9+2747 Giustini2020 and XMMSL1 J024916.6-041244 Chakraborty2021 , were discovered by XMM-Newton Jansen2001 , and the third and fourth, eRO-QPE1 and eRO-QPE2 Arcodia2021 , by the wide-field X-ray telescope eROSITA Predehl2021 . It is remarkable that the QPEs in GSN069 and XMMSL1 J024916.6-041244 followed flares interpreted as tidal disruption events Miniutti2019,Chakraborty2021,Miniutti2022 .

Miniutti2022 and Chakraborty2021 , respectively, reported that the luminosity for the events GSN069 and XMMSL1 gradually decrease by an order of magnitude in 2 and 10 years. Recently, after the disappearance of QPEs in GSN069, their reappearance has been reported Miniutti2023_reQPE . Prior to this reappearance, the quiescent luminosity is again enhanced, possibly due to additional partial disruption or intense mass loss from a star. This suggests that QPEs occur when the accretion rate is lower than some critical value.

sources.summary: The paper describes GSN 069 as the first QPE galaxy, with hour-long recurring X-ray eruptions and an unusual wavelet spectrum containing persistent frequencies.

quotes.quote: GSN 069 GSN 069 ( z = 0.018 ) was the first galaxy discovered to show QPEs in its X-ray flux miniutti2019nine_gsn . These eruptions are short-lived X-ray flares with a duration of approximately 1 hour occurring approximately every 9 hours. The QPEs are transient in GSN 069 (e.g. miniutti2023_gsn ) and several scenarios have been proposed to describe the behaviour, including unstable mass transfer occurring between a low mass star and the supermassive black hole lu2022_gsn_qpe_unstable_mass_transfer , or instabilities in the inner accretion disk pan2022_gsn069_disk .

The wavelet power spectrum of GSN 069 might be the most unusual one we have examined here (Fig. fig:gsn_cwt ). The wavelet power exhibits amplitude peaks across a range of frequencies during each of the eruption events in the light curve. However, there are also a number of persistent frequencies throughout the light curve. Comparing the wavelet power spectrum to the simulations in Section sim_section , we note a striking resemblance with Fig. fig:simulated_qpe that displays harmonic behaviour over a white noise spectrum. We can further examine the wavelet power by calculating the global wavelet transform (Fig. fig:gsn_gwt ), which effectively integrates the total power at each frequency over all times (as defined in torrence1998 ).

sources.summary: The paper describes GSN 069 as the first QPE galaxy and uniquely associated with a repeating TDE, arguing its TDE may be disruption of a common envelope.

quotes.quote: GSN 069 is the first galaxy found to have X-ray quasi-periodic eruptions(QPEs), which originate in the region around the supermassive black hole(SMBH) miniutti2019nine . The nature of the QPEs is still unclear. The most competitive model is the tidal stripping model suggested by . The eruptions are produced by SMBH accretion of material which is stripped from a star moving around the SMBH at the periastron.

The star can be the white dwarf(WD), main-sequence star, or the evolved star . In such a model, QPEs from GSN 069 are produced from a WD-SMBH system with high eccentricity king2020gsn,wang2022model . Although QPEs have since been seen in other galaxies giustini2020x,song2020possible,arcodia2021x,chakraborty2021possible , GSN 069 remains the most special one. It is the only galaxy with QPEs that is associated with a TDE, which is found to be re-brightening after 9yr since the main peak appeared miniutti2023repeating .

sources.summary: The paper treats GSN 069 as a Seyfert 2 QPE source and tests whether its regular X-ray flares can arise from a close self-lensing binary black hole.

quotes.quote: Remarkably sharp, high amplitude, regular flares -- dubbed quasi-periodic eruptions (QPEs) -- have recently been discovered in the X-ray light curves of two low mass active galactic nuclei (AGN): GSN 069 Miniutti2019 and RX J1301.9+2747 (hereafter RX J1301; Giustini2020 ). Both AGN have been optically classified as Seyfert 2 galaxies Sun2013,Shu2018 , and both exhibit a thermal X-ray spectrum consistent with a standard thin accretion disc Shakura1973 around a black hole (BH) of mass M 10^ 5-6 M_ . In GSN 069, QPEs are observed in XMM-Newton and Chandra data from 2018 and 2019 to have a duration of 1 hr and a spacing of 9 hrs, and the peak 0.2-2 keV count rate is 1 order of magnitude higher than the quiescent level. There is evidence that the true period in GSN 069 is 18 hours, with a larger flare being followed by a smaller flare just over 9 hrs later, followed by another flare just under 9 hrs later, and so on (Miniutti et al in prep).

Here we explore a model whereby GSN 069 and RX J1301 are both close binary massive BH systems. The implied orbital periods ( P 18 hrs for GSN 069 and P 9 hrs for RX J1301) are very short, requiring both systems considered here to be very close binaries ( r_a as small as hundreds of gravitational radii). In this paper, we explore two tests of our binary interpretation of QPEs.

sources.summary: The paper models GSN 069 as the best-studied QPE source, reproducing five XMM-Newton peaks, alternating recurrence/amplitude behavior, and a 100 eV peak temperature.

quotes.quote: Following their discovery in GSN 069 Miniutti2019 , QPEs have been observed so far from the nuclei of other four galaxies: RX J1301.9+2747 Giustini2020 , eRO-QPE1 and eRO-QPE2 Arcodia2021 , and XMMSL1 J024916.6-04124 Chakraborty2021 . Only 1.5 QPEs were detected in the latter source, but all observed properties indicate it is nevertheless a robust QPE candidate. QPE host galaxies can be classified as relatively low-mass post-starburst galaxies Arcodia2021,Wevers2022 , a population that is very similar to the preferred tidal disruption events (TDEs) hosts 2020SSRv..216...32F with which they also share a preference towards low mass MBHs of 10^ 5-6.7  M _ Wevers2022 .

GSN 069 is the most studied source that has shown QPEs in different epochs. We here model 5 peaks observed during an XMM-Newton observation performed on 31 May 2019, XMM5 in . The quiescence bolometric luminosity is 2-5 10^ 43 erg s ^ 1 for this source, implying m _1 0.1 for a 10^6M_ primary black hole.

As shown in Figure  fig:GSN , our model nicely reproduces not only the observed alternating long-short recurrence times, but also the associated alternating strong-weak QPEs 2023A&A...670A..93M . Our model also predicts a peak temperature of 100 eV, in agreement with the observed values, i.e. 90-95 eV 2023A&A...670A..93M .

sources.summary: The paper fits GSN 069 with a disk-instability model including a hardening factor, discusses TDE-like long-term behavior, and links rebrightening to longer QPE periods.

quotes.quote: GSN 069 To investigate the effect of hardening factor on GNS 069, we also compare our numerical results with the observed light curve and X-ray spectra in Figure fig:GSN . It is found that, by including the hardening factor, we can still achieve satisfactory results with a higher black hole mass ( M_ BH =6 10^ 5 M_ ) compared with those in 2022ApJ...928L..18P .

For example, we also adopt the hardening factor given by equation (9) in 2019ApJ...874...23D to fit the observational data of GSN 069, where the parameters employed are shown in Table tab:pars GSN 069 (2). A similar good fitting result can be achieved by slightly reducing the mass of black hole.

showed that the QPEs of GSN 069 disappeared when a rebrightening of quiescent state happened after January 2020. According to the above discussion, the unstable zone in a thin disk will expand to larger radius, resulting on the increase of periods in QPEs when the accretion rate increases. Therefore, if the rebrightening of GSN 069 is caused by a repeating TDEs, the period of QPEs will be greatly increased with the sudden increase of accretion rate.

sources.summary: The paper uses GSN 069 as a QPE source whose outbursts, recurrence changes, quiescent variability, and post-outburst eruption pattern motivate disk-star evolution models.

quotes.quote: Changes in the QPE recurrence pattern following such outbursts, similar to that observed in GSN 069, could arise from temporary misalignment between the EMRI-fed disk and the SMBH equatorial plane as the former regrows its mass after a state transition. While many models have been proposed for QPEs Zalamea+10,King20,Ingram+21,Xian+21,Metzger+22,Pan+22,Zhao+22,King22,Krolik&Linial22,Linial&Sari17,Kaur+23,Linial&Metzger23,Franchini+23,Tagawa&Haiman23 , only a few account for an important clue: a regular alternating behavior, observed in at least two of the QPE sources GSN 069 Miniutti+19,Miniutti+23 and eRO-QPE2 Arcodia+21 , in which the temporal spacing between consecutive eruptions varies back and forth by around 10 . Eight years prior to the discovery of QPE activity, the well-studied source GSN 069 exhibited starting in 2010 an X-ray outburst similar to a TDE Shu+18,Sheng+21 , though exhibiting a longer duration and slower post-maximum decay than most TDE flares.

After a year of QPE activity, the eruptions from GSN 069 become undetected at the onset of the second quiescent-emission outburst in 2019; however, as the outburst faded, the periodic eruptions recently reappeared Miniutti+23b , albeit initially with different recurrence properties than before (G. The quiescent light curves in GSN 069 Miniutti+19,Miniutti+23b , XMMSL1 J024916.6-041244 Chakraborty+21 , eRO-QPE3, eRO-QPE4 Arcodia+24 exhibit significant (factor of few ) changes on timescales from years to decades, including both rising and decaying emission (or phases of both in GSN 069; Miniutti+23 ), broadly consistent with our thermally-unstable accretion solutions. We speculate this could explain why the repetition pattern of the eruptions from GSN 069, after their reappearance following the second quiescent-outburst, temporarily differed (it remains to be seen how well the repetition pattern will settled back into that observed prior to the outburst; G. Even temporary changes in the star-disk inclination angle resulting from the angular momentum of the deposited stripped mass (e.g., XiangGruess+16 ) could result in short-lived changes to the QPE eruption recurrence time pattern, similar that observed in GSN 069 following their recent reappearance.

sources.summary: The paper analyzes HST images of GSN 069, finding compact and extended nuclear [O III] emission, estimating a young accretion system, and linking dense nuclear gas to molecular clouds ionized by soft X-rays.

quotes.quote: In this paper, we present an analysis of archival Hubble Space Telescope (HST) images to study the narrow-line [ O III ] emission in the QPE source GSN 069. We find strong evidence for a compact nuclear [ O III ] emission region of size 35 pc, overlaid on top of extended [ O III ] emission up to 2 kpc away from the nucleus. The age of the accretion system is estimated to be between 10 and 100 yr.

The [ O III ] luminosity of the compact region was measured to be (2.1 0.3) 10^ 40 erg s^ 1 . Based on Cloudy simulations, we constrain that the [ O III ] emitting gas has a hydrogen number density in the range 5 10^ 3 < n_ H 10^ 8 cm^ 3 and volume filling factor f_ V < 2 10^ 3 . We suggest that the dense gas in the nuclear region of GSN 069 originates from molecular clouds (with total mass 3 10^ 3 M_ ), which are freshly ionised by the soft X-ray photons from the accretion disk.

sources.summary: The paper describes GSN 069 as a supersoft low-mass AGN with huge X-ray outburst and long-term decay, possibly from a tidal disruption event though variable AGN activity remains possible.

quotes.quote: GSN 069 is an optically identified very low-mass AGN which shows supersoft X-ray emission. The source is known to exhibit huge X-ray outburst, with flux increased by more than a factor of 240 compared to the quiescence state. We report its long-term evolution in the X-ray flux and spectral variations over a time-scale of decade, using both new and archival X-ray observations from the and .

The long decay of X-ray flux and spectral evolution, as well as the supersoft X-ray spectra, suggest that the source could be a tidal disruption event, though a highly variable AGN cannot be fully ruled out. Further continued X-ray monitoring would be required to test the TDE interpretation, through better determining the flux evolution in the decay phase.

sources.summary: The paper uses GSN 069 as the prototype QPE galaxy, describing its recurring eruptions, supersoft quiescent spectrum, black hole mass estimate, and shared behavior with RX J1301.9+2747.

quotes.quote: The phenomena known as X-ray quasi-periodic eruptions (QPEs) have recently been detected in the nucleus of the galaxy GSN 069 2019Natur.573..381M as high-amplitude, recurrent X-ray flares over a generally stable flux level (quiescent level). In GSN 069, QPEs last about one hour and recur about every nine hours, with a subtle alternating pattern of long-short recurrence times and strong-weak amplitudes. The QPE amplitude in GSN 069 is energy-dependent and up to two orders of magnitude in the 600-800 eV band. The X-ray spectrum of GSN 069 in the quiescent level is super-soft (most of the emission is at E< 2 keV) and it can be described via the thermal emission of an accretion disk with kT 50 eV. During the overall X-ray decay between December 2010 and January 2019, the long-term evolution of the quiescent emission is consistent with the L T^4 relation expected from a constant-area emitting accretion disk.

sources.summary: The paper applies a relativistic star-disc collision model to GSN069, explaining its alternating QPE recurrence times with low eccentricity and precession around a roughly 3e5 solar-mass massive black hole.

quotes.quote: By applying the model to the observed QPEs of GSN069, we find that the star is in a near-circular orbit ( e_ =0.05^ +0.02 _ 0.02 ) with semimajor axis of 365^ +54 _ 49 r_ g around a MBH with =3.0^ +0.9 _ 0.6 10^5 . The alternative short and long recurring time of the QPEs of GSN069 can be well explained by the small eccentricity and the orbital precession of the star. We find that the QPEs of GSN069 are possibly driven by a striped stellar core colliding with accretion disc after partial tidal disruption event around the massive black hole (MBH). For GSN069-like galaxies, if continuous X-ray monitoring of QPE events can be accumulated with uncertainties of TOA 100-150 s, the spin of massive black hole can be constrained by fitting to QPEs. Our results show that the timing of QPEs can provide a unique probe for measuring the spinning of MBH and tests of no-hair theorem.

sources.summary: The paper treats GSN069 as a QPE source discovered in an AGN, obtains new optical spectroscopy, and places it in the Seyfert region of diagnostic diagrams.

quotes.quote: Two sources (GSN069 and RXJ1301) were discovered in active galactic nuclei (AGN). Their amplitude and duration have an energy dependence, being stronger and shorter at higher energies. During quiescence, their X-ray spectrum is consistent with a featureless, cool (kT 40--60 eV) accretion disk model (e.g. Miniutti19 ), and the inferred black hole masses are 10 ^ 5-6 M _ .

For three QPEs (GSN069, eRO-QPE1 and eRO-QPE2) we present new, medium resolution optical spectroscopy obtained with the Magellan Echelette spectrograph (MagE, mage ), mounted on the Magellan Baade telescope at Las Campanas Observatory, Chile.
Three out of five sources (2MASXJ0249, GSN069 and RXJ1301) lie within the Seyfert part of the diagrams.

sources.summary: The paper suggests GSN 069's quasi-periodic eruptions could arise from Hills capture of a white dwarf that is partially disrupted by its central supermassive black hole.

quotes.quote: This process could, for example, be applicable to systems that display quasi-periodic eruptions on much shorter timescales, e.g., those observed in the nucleus of GSN 069 miniutti19 , provided that the radius of the star is correspondingly much smaller. king20 recently suggested that GSN 069 could be powered by a white dwarf being partially disrupted by the SMBH in that system. Applying the analysis in Section 2 to this system suggests that the Hills mechanism may be a possible route to capturing a white dwarf into the required orbit (in contrast, king20 suggested that the white dwarf is a relic of a past, partial disruption of a red giant). However, a caveat with this possible origin of the short-timescale, quasi-periodic eruptions is that the SMBH mass must be small to avoid swallowing the white dwarf. For a stellar radius of 0.02 R_ and a stellar mass of 0.21 M_ king20 , the direct capture radius ( 4 R_ g ) coincides with the tidal radius when M_ 2.5 10^ 5 M_ , which is slightly smaller than the value inferred for GSN 069.

sources.summary: The paper describes GSN 069 as the first QPE source, a Seyfert 2 galaxy whose X-ray decay may be TDE-related, with an asymmetric QPE light curve and a low-mass SMBH.

quotes.quote: The first QPE was discovered with XMM-Newton in the central region of Seyfert 2 galaxy GSN 069 Miniutti_2019 . The decay of X-ray emission of GSN 069 could be a tidal disruption event (TDE) source according to the long-term evolution of X-ray flux and spectrum Shu_2018 and the carbon and nitrogen ratio [ C/N ] abundance Sheng_2021 .

The first QPE in GSN 069 also shows asymmetric behavior from its phase-folded light curve. The SMBHs associated QPEs have typical low masses. For GSN 069, the mass of SMBH is 4 10^5 M_ Miniutti_2019 .

sources.summary: The paper focuses on GSN 069, testing whether compact magnetized accretion disks can reproduce its flare duration, recurrence time, and lack of variable jet emission.

quotes.quote: In this work we test the possibility that the known QPEs can be explained by accretion from a compact accretion disk with an outer radius r_ out 10-40 r_ g , focusing on a particular object GSN 069.

Another feature distinguishing these sources from classical AGNs is the lack of broad optical or UV emission lines, although in GSN 069 there are narrow line regions that might indicate recent activity. According to Miniutti2019 , the lack of variability in the radio source detected in GSN 069 implies that the production mechanism of the associated jet is likely unrelated to the QPE process.
In this work we explored the possibility that the QPEs observed in GSN 069 occur due to regular cycles of mass buildup in a small accretion disk around the SMBH.

sources.summary: The paper models GSN 069's QPEs with a tidally stripped white dwarf in an accretion disk, explaining intensity decay and linking the disk to a tidal disruption event.

quotes.quote: Then we suggested a model of a tidal stripped WD moving inside an accretion disk for QPEs in GSN 069. The drag force by the disk can effectively reduce mass transfer and thus explain the observed decay in the intensity of the QPEs in GSN 069. The disk is likely a fallback disk of the tidal disruption event in GSN 069. Considering the evolution of its accretion rate, the increase in the intensity of the latest eruption can also be explained.

sources.summary: The paper describes GSN 069 as an AGN-like QPE host recovered by the search, physically similar to J0249, lacking significant UV activity and paired with J0249 as an XMM slew QPE source.

quotes.quote: Four systems exhibiting QPEs have been found to date: the first two, GSN 069 and RX J1301.9+2747 Miniutti19,Giustini20 , are consistent with active galactic nuclei (AGN) on the basis of their emission line profiles, though it is worth noting that both galaxies show no broad components to the H or H lines, and that the observed narrow emission lines may be the signature of past nuclear activity.

J0249 falls near the shorter end of QPE characteristic timescales, closely matching the durations and recurrence times seen in eRO-QPE2 in spite of its host galaxy sharing the most physical similarities with GSN 069, a comparatively intermediate QPE source. eRO-QPE1 is a conspicuous outlier compared with the four other QPE hosts in terms of characteristic timescales, quiescent luminosity, flare amplitude, and blackbody temperature.

Similarly, no significant UV activity was seen in GSN 069 Miniutti19 , though a re-analysis of RX J1301.9+2747 from the May 2019 observation did show that one of the three X-ray flares is accompanied by a slight dip in UV (but much smaller amplitude than in J0249).

sources.summary: The paper models GSN 069’s TDE-like flare and later QPEs as connected phases of a white-dwarf and massive-black-hole interaction, predicting about 1.2 years remaining.

quotes.quote: The first QPE source GSN 069 detected by XMM-Newton in December 24, 2018, has a peak X-ray luminosity of L 5 10^ 42 erg/s and a nine-hour recurrence time Miniutti2019 .

In this work, we propose a model to connect the TDE-like flare and the followed QPEs as found in GSN 069 based on a WD binary is tidally detached by a MBH, where the TDE-like event is triggered by the tidal disruption of WD envelope and the QPEs are produced by the tidal disruption of the surviving WD.
For the GSN 069 with M_ BH 4 10^5 M_ and L 5 10^ 42 erg/s , we find the remaining time is 1.2 years .

sources.summary: The paper discusses GSN069 as an AGN QPE source with decreasing accretion after two TDE-like outbursts; eruptions reappeared at lower accretion rates, motivating future X-ray and UV monitoring of its eruption SED.

quotes.quote: An example of such sources is provided by the AGN QPE source, GSN069, with its generally decreasing accretion rate following two TDE-like outbursts. Eruptions in the source were not detected for accretion rates m 0.5 , but (re)appeared for lower accretion rates Miniutti2019,2023A&A...670A..93M,2023A&A...674L...1M . Future monitoring of this source by X-ray and UV instruments will reveal how the eruption SED changes with the drop in the accretion rate.

gmw_00000474.name: NGC 3921
gmw_00000474.overview: NGC 3921 is a late-stage, single-nucleus merger remnant of two disk galaxies [arXiv:astro-ph/0404424, arXiv:1911.00549] that is classified as a protoelliptical [arXiv:1208.4502, arXiv:astro-ph/0404424]. The galaxy features a LINER nucleus [arXiv:astro-ph/0309377], outer tidal tails and plumes [arXiv:astro-ph/0309377], and centrally concentrated recent star formation [arXiv:astro-ph/9910275].

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 3921 as a protoelliptical major-merger remnant with an AGN, adopts an 80 Mpc distance from its redshift, and detects a central VLA source.

quotes.quote: Here, we present , archival Very Large Array (VLA), and archival HST/WFPC2 and HST/WFC3 observations of the bright ULX in first found by 2004MNRAS.353..221N called NGC 3921 X--2. is classified as a protoelliptical that was formed during a major merger taking place approximately 0.7 Gyr ago, where one of the merging galaxies was gas rich and the other gas poor ( 1996AJ....111..109S ; 1996AJ....112.1839S ). Given that has an AGN the recoiling black hole scenario desribed in 2010MNRAS.407..645J is not applicable for the ULX under study in this galaxy. We adopt _m= 0.3 , _ =0.7 , and H_0 = 70 km s ^ 1 Mpc ^ 1 to convert the redshift of to a distance measurement.

Using standard cosmological parameters the redshift converts to a distance of 80 Mpc for NGC 3921, which makes the X-ray luminosity in the range 0.3--8 keV L_X 8 10^ 39 . For comparison the 0.5--10 keV luminosity is the same when assuming a powerlaw spectral model with an index of 1.7 and the Galactic interstellar extinction given above.
The only significantly detected source corresponds to the center of the galaxy, at RA=11:51:06.8724(2) Dec=55:04:43.307(2) (J2000), at 1.8 mJy. The errors on those positions are purely statistical.

sources.summary: NGC 3921 is described as a post-merger system with one nucleus, elliptical-like center, tidal tails and plumes, LINER nucleus, H disk emission, and color gradients affected by dust.

quotes.quote: NGC 3921 Only one nucleus is visible in NGC 3921 (Figure f:n3921 ). This galaxy is a post-merger system that has developed an elliptical-like central region see, e.g., schw96a,schw96,hib99 , although numerous dust lanes still criss-cross this area (see Fig. f:n3921 ). The evidence for a recent merger can be seen in the outer region where tails and plumes prevail hib96 . Several compact open star clusters or globular clusters can also be seen in the HST optical images kindly provided by B. Whitmore see also schw96 .

The H emission is centered in a disk around the nucleus, but extends to larger radii. The nucleus is classified as a LINER by sta82a,sta82b and daha85 . It lies in a location where the V-I color gradient is very steep as seen in the V-I image, but the nucleus itself has normal values of V-I around 1.1.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 3921 as a recent disk-galaxy merger remnant and protoelliptical likely to remain luminous, with young globular clusters near its center showing halo kinematics.
sources.paper_coordinate_text: 3921 itself is located at _ J2000 =11^ h 51^ m 07 0 , _ J 2000 =+55 04 43 (SMWF96)

quotes.quote: The present paper describes exploratory spectroscopic observations aimed at verifying the nature of two candidate young globular clusters in 3921, and at determining their ages and metallicities. Similar to, but slightly younger than the more well-known 7252, 3921 is a very recent remnant of two merged disk galaxies tt72 and shows many signs of being a 0.7 0.3 Gyr old protoelliptical ( schw96a , hereafter S96). Predicted to fade by only 1.5 mag from its present M_V = -22.1 over the next 12 Gyr, this remnant is likely to remain a luminous field elliptical.

We have described exploratory Keck-LRIS spectroscopy of two candidate globular clusters of apparent magnitude V 22.2 in the merger remnant 3921. The main results are as follows: (1) Both objects are confirmed to be young star clusters of visual absolute magnitude M_V 12.5 within 3921. They lie at 5 kpc ( 0.9 ) projected distance from the center and show halo kinematics.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 3921 as a late-stage merger remnant with tidal tails, high nuclear luminosity density, blue central colors, and centrally concentrated recent star formation.

quotes.quote: The two latest-stage mergers in the list are NGC 3921 and NGC 7252, which have tidal tails but show no remaining signs of two galaxies with a separate identity. To create a sample for our study, we sought galaxies with morphological properties similar to NGC 3921 and 7252 from the Catalogs of Arp (1966) and Vorontsov-Velyaminov (1977), and from the imaging survey of (UV-bright) Markarian galaxies by Mazzarella Boroson (1993). This yielded a sample of 19 galaxies with cz < 10000 km s ^ 1 , of which we imaged 14 galaxies (including NGC 3921 and 7252).

The J-H and H-K color profiles of NGC 3921 and 7252 show that they become bluer towards the center, presumably due to recent star formation. This is consistent with the detection of strong Balmer absorption lines in ground-based spectra of these galaxies (KSB98). NGC 34 becomes redder towards the center, probably as a result of dust absorption. NGC 34 is the most IR-luminous galaxy of those that we observed, suggesting the presence of ongoing or recent star formation in this galaxy as well. The high luminosity densities observed in NGC 34, 3921 and 7252 are probably a direct consequence of recent star formation triggered by a merger.

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 3921, an advanced single-nucleus merger remnant, as a resolved ESM test target and reports improved J-band image quality from a mosaiced LUCI observation.

quotes.quote: As part of the characterization of ESM using large resolved objects and observations at < 2 m observations of the advanced (single nucleus) merger remnant NGC 3921 were obtained under poor seeing conditions. NGC 3921 was selected from a larger sample of 51 advanced merger remnants (Rothberg Joseph 2004 ).

NGC 3921 was selected based on visibility and a nearby AO Reference star. Preliminary results clearly demonstrate that the image quality at J band can significantly benefit from the use of ESM, even in poor conditions. Figure 4 shows a mosaiced field of NGC 3921 created from four exposures of 62.75 seconds, processed and combined using IRAF as described earlier.

sources.summary: The paper presents NGC 3921 as a roughly 1 Gyr-old merger remnant resembling giant ellipticals in luminosity, structure, globular clusters, and E+A spectral signatures.

quotes.quote: First, the similarities between recent, 1 Gyr-old merger remnants like NGC 3921 or NGC 7252 and giant Es (e.g., Toomre 1977; Schweizer 1982, 1996; Barnes 1998) are worth re-emphasizing. The above two remnants currently have luminosities of 2.8 L^ _V and will still shine with 1.0 L^ _V after 10 - 12 Gyr of evolution. They feature ^ 1/4 type light distributions, power-law cores, U BV I color gradients, and velocity dispersions typical of Es. Both also possess many young, metal-rich halo globular clusters. They show integrated ``E + A'' spectra indicative of b 10 starbursts (Fritze-von Alvensleben Gerhardt 1994), as do many other similar young merger remnants in the local universe (Zabludoff et al. \ 1996).

sources.summary: NGC 3921 is discussed as an older merger remnant whose young star-cluster system helps test whether Antennae-like mass functions could show luminosity-function turnover.

quotes.quote: A somewhat similar procedure is used for YSCs in NGC 7252 and NGC 3921 by Miller Fall (1997), who find that power-laws are preferred over Gaussians for the MFs of these YSC systems. NGC 7252 and NGC 3921 are the two oldest merger remnants from Toomre's (1977) list. Large enough YSC systems have been detected in both of them to define the bright end of their LFs (Whitmore 1993, Miller 1997, Schweizer 1996). Distances, however, are larger than to NGC 4038/39 by factors 3 and 4 for NGC 7252 and NGC 3921, respectively, pushing the completeness limit to significantly higher luminosities. The higher mean ages of the YSCs in these cases ( 650 - 750 Myr for NGC 7252 and 250 - 750 Myr for NGC 3921, depending on metallicity) add another argument in favour of them being young GCs, since they have survived 10 t_ cross (cf. Schweizer 1996).

sources.summary: NGC 3921 is treated as a late-stage merger remnant with a single slightly elongated core, elliptical-like properties, and unusually low hot diffuse gas X-ray luminosity.

quotes.quote: We also analyzed archival images of NGC 3921. The nuclear region shows a slightly elongated core in the NNE to SSW direction (see Figure f:thirtyninetwentyonevk ), with only one nucleus detected. Our findings are consistent with earlier studies sch96a and with the fact that NGC 3921 is generally regarded as a late-stage merger remnant with properties resembling elliptical galaxies. In a recent X-ray study by nol04 , however, the X-ray luminosity of the hot diffuse gas was found to be two orders of magnitude less than that typical of elliptical galaxies.

sources.summary: The review treats NGC 3921 as a recent gas-rich merger remnant hosting many young globular clusters, mostly formed over 100-200 Myr, centrally concentrated, and near solar metallicity.

quotes.quote: HST studies of recent merger remnants such as NGC 3921 , NGC 7252 , and NGC 3597 show that these galaxies typically host about 10 ^2 -10 ^3 point-like sources that appear to be mostly young GCs ( 1 Gyr old). (It is not that there are no old GCs in these relatively distant remnants, only that the YGCs are much brighter and more easily studied.) Age-dating based both on broad-band photometry and spectroscopy shows that the majority of these YGCs formed in relatively short, 100--200 Myr time spans during the mergers. The YGCs appear strongly concentrated toward their hosts' centers, half of them lying typically within 5 kpc from the nucleus. The few spectroscopic studies that have so far been made of such YGCs invariably show them to be of approximately solar metallicity: [ Z ] = 0.0 0.1 in NGC 7252 , 0.0 0.5 in NGC 3921 , and---for the intermediate-age, 3--5 Gyr old GCs in more advanced remnants--- [ Z ] = 0.0 0.15 in NGC 1316 and 0.1 0.2 in NGC 5128 .

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 3921 as a late-stage merger with widespread PAH populations, a mainly neutral PAH component, and signs of H2 tail emission not associated with PAH.

quotes.quote: For instance, NGC 4676 (primarily ionized PAHs) and NGC 520 (primarily neutral PAHs) show a very concentrated distribution in the PAH interband strength ratios, while, e.g., NGC 6621 (ionized and neutral PAHs) and NGC 3921 (primarily neutral PAHs, including the nucleus) exhibit more widespread PAH populations.
NGC 3921 is mainly populated by neutral PAHs.

Our comparison between the flux distribution of the strongest H _2 emission line (the extinction corrected 9.7 m H _2 emission line) and PAH (using the combined main PAH features in the SL wavelength range at 6.1, 7.7, and 11.3 m) shows that (1) H _2 has typically a larger extent than PAH with an average ratio of H _2 /PAH radius of 1.5 (ranging from 1.1 to 1.9, including PSF size corrections), (2) some galaxies show a significantly different H _2 /PAH flux ratio between different merger components, most significantly in the early merger systems NGC 4676 and NGC 7592 (see sec:indgalaxies ), and (3) at least one of our galaxies (NGC 3921) shows signs of a tail of H _2 emission not associated with a strong PAH feature (see Fig.

sources.summary: The paper describes Arp 224 as having a stable inner center, a high A1 peak of 0.3 at 12 arcseconds, and Fourier amplitudes ordered from A1 to A4.

quotes.quote: Arp 224: The centre (x0, y0) remains constant in the inner 6". The value of A _1 is high showing a peak of 0.3 at 12". In this case also, A _1 > A _2 > A _3 > A _4 .

gmw_00000475.name: NGC 7252
gmw_00000475.overview: NGC 7252 is a late-stage merger remnant of two gas-rich spiral galaxies [arXiv:1509.05404, arXiv:2309.06478] that features a single nucleus [arXiv:1509.05404], stellar shells, loops [arXiv:2002.07312], and two extended tidal tails [arXiv:2002.07312, arXiv:2309.06478]. The galaxy contains a central star-forming disk [arXiv:0807.4687] and a large population of young, massive star clusters [arXiv:1302.1683].

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 7252 as a merger-remnant exception whose brightest clusters trace a past starburst, implying very high peak star formation and HLIRG-level luminosity.

quotes.quote: To demonstrate this effect we use the merger remnant NGC 7252 as an example. This galaxy is likely to have been produced by an equal mass gas-rich spiral/spiral galaxy merger (Hibbard Mihos 1995) approximately 500-1000 Myr ago (Schweizer Seitzer 1998). NGC 7252 is an outlying point in the relation, being significantly above the fit to the other galaxies ( >4 Where is calculated from the difference between the observed M_ V ^ brightest and the best fit relation and error by Weidner et al. (2004). , i.e. its brightest cluster is too bright for its current SFR), and as such, was not included in the fit by Weidner et al. (2004). During this merger, many extremely massive star clusters were formed, including a handful of clusters with masses exceeding 10^7 \ (Schweizer Seitzer 1998, Maraston et al. 2004, Bastian et al. 2006b). After this strong starburst, the SFR has presumably been decreasing to its present rate of 5.4 (Kneirman et al. 2003), which is localized mainly to a relatively small inner star-forming disk (Schweizer 1982).
NGC 7252 stands out among the others in its estimated star formation rate ( >4000 ) and its peak luminosity would clearly place it in the range of Hyper-luminous infrared galaxies (HLIRGs).

If, instead of W3, we used the second brightest cluster in the NGC 7252, W30, the implied SFR and peak would be 600 and 10^ 12.5 , respectively. While still well within the ULIRG category, this shows the sensitivity and limitations of the method used here.

sources.summary: The paper studies NGC 7252 as a merger remnant whose young clusters show an AGB phase transition, while its stellar populations lack element overabundance and may not become a normal elliptical.

quotes.quote: 2, 85748 Garching, Germany email: mkissler@eso.org University of California Observatories / Lick Observatory, University of California, Santa Cruz, CA 95064, USA email: brodie@ucolick.org Havard-Smithsonian Center for Astrophysics, 60 Garden St., Cambridge, MA 02138, USA email: pbarmby@cfa.harvard.edu, huchra@cfa.harvard.edu Received September 2, 2000; accepted January 29, 2001 We have extended the study of the young star clusters observed in the merger remnant galaxy NGC 7252 by obtaining K band photometry for these clusters.

The comparison with the colour distribution of the NGC 7252 star clusters shows that they are indeed intermediate age clusters undergoing the AGB phase transition. The AGB phase transition is observed for the first time outside the Local Group. Most of the studied clusters span the very narrow age range 300 - 500 Myr, and likely have metallicities 0.5 - 1 Z_ . A very important exception is the cluster W32, which has already completed its AGB epoch, its colours being consistent with an age of 1 - 2 Gyr.

Due to its very isolated position, NGC 7252 would become a field elliptical, likely an L^ * object. In Section 5 we showed that neither the cluster population nor the diffuse light of NGC 7252 show any sign of overabundance in elements. Therefore, the present average stellar population of NGC 7252 cannot evolve into an average enhanced stellar population, in contrast to the observations of present day ellipticals of the same luminosity.

sources.summary: The paper describes 7252 as a gas-rich merger remnant with a faint EELR ionization echo, implying Seyfert-like AGN activity that faded by orders of magnitude after the merger.

quotes.quote: For general reference, the merger remnant 7252 is also known as Arp 226 and the ``Atoms for Peace" galaxy. It is located at _ J2000 =22^ h 20^ m 44 78 , _ J 2000 =-24 40 41 8 mill97 and has a recession velocity relative to the Local Group of = +4831 \ value revised by + 3 s82 , which places it at a distance of 66.2 Mpc for H_0 = 73 \ Mpc ^ 1 free10 . At that distance, adopted throughout the present paper, 1 = 321 pc.

There is little doubt that two gas-rich spirals began merging less than 1 Gyr ago, coalesced about 220 Myr ago, and have transformed themselves into some sort of modern-day protoelliptical t77,s82,schw98 . Much is known about its gas contents and kinematics wang92,hibb94 , post-starburst stellar populations s82,schw90,liu95 , and system of massive young star clusters formed during the merger whit93,mill97,ss98,bc03,bast13 . Yet, until the present study no sign of AGN activity in 7252 was found, leaving us in the dark concerning the possible presence of any supermassive black hole (SMBH), its mass, activity, and recent growth history.

The nucleus of 7252 itself must have reached at least Seyfert-like luminosity to still excite the nebula as observed, yet has since faded by several orders of magnitude to a very low, so far undetected activity level.

sources.summary: The paper uses UVIT observations of post-merger NGC7252 to map recent star formation in its tidal tails and main body, measuring an integrated uncorrected star-formation rate of 0.81 M_/yr.
sources.paper_coordinate_text: The post-merger galaxy NGC7252 (J2000) = 22h20m44.7s and (J2000) = 24d40m42s according to https://ned.ipac.caltech.edu/

quotes.quote: The post merger galaxy NGC7252 ("Atoms-for-Peace" galaxy) is observed with the Astrosat UV imaging telescope (UVIT) in broadband NUV and FUV filters to isolate the star forming regions in the tidal tails and study the spatial variation in star formation rates. Based on ultraviolet imaging observations, we discuss star formation regions of ages < 200 Myrs in the tidal tails. We measure star formation rates in these regions and in the main body of the galaxy. The integrated star formation rate of NGC7252 (i.e., that in the galaxy and tidal tails combined) without correcting for extinction is found to be 0.81 0.01 M _ /yr.

NGC7252 ("Atoms-for-Peace" galaxy) is an archetypical advanced merger galaxy with a single-nucleus merger remnant, its tidal tails spread in almost opposite directions. NGC7252 is considered to satisfy the "Toomre Sequence" of merging, where the merger remnant will eventually deplete the fuel for star formation and evolve into an elliptical galaxy Toomre_1972,Toomre_1977,Schweizer_1982 . Previous studies have demonstrated that NGC7252 is the remnant of an advanced merger between two gas-rich spiral galaxies, with two tidal tails and a main body of the galaxy, exhibiting shells and ripples in optical images Schweizer_1982,Dupraz_1990,Wang_1992,Fritze_1994,Hibbard_1994 .

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 7252 as a prototypical merger remnant whose luminous cluster W3 provides near-infrared evidence for intermediate-age AGB-dominated stellar populations, possibly including carbon-rich stars.

quotes.quote: One of the best systems where to study supermassive clusters is the prototypical merger remnant NGC 7252. Using ground based observations, Schweizer (1982) identified many very luminous point-like sources in this galaxy. Whitmore et al. (1993) and Miller et al. (1997) performed Hubble Space Telescope UBVI observations and confirmed these sources to be globular clusters. They estimated that the most luminous (massive) ones have ages between 0.1 and 1 Gyr and masses of up to 10^ 8 M_ .

We have observed the most massive cluster of NGC 7252 in the NIR. These observations allow us to report the first observations of a stellar population dominated by AGB stars beyond the Local Group. Comparisons with our model cluster spectra lead us to argue that the use of an effective temperature scale calibrated on static giant branch stars for TP-AGB stars is, at best, questionable.

sources.summary: NGC 7252 is the most advanced Toomre-sequence merger, with an elliptical-like remnant, central spiral disk, active star formation, reaccreted tidal gas, and possible nuclear activity.

quotes.quote: NGC 7252 (``Atoms--for--Peace'' Galaxy) We use the HST V and I images from mil97 to study the most advanced merger in the Toomre Sequence. The remnant from the merger is probably between 0.5 and 1 Gyrs old sch82,hib95b . It has a single nucleus and is characterized by a ^ 1/4 profile typical of elliptical galaxies sch82,mil97,hib99 . It was quite a surprise, therefore, when HST revealed a central spiral disk ( mil97 and Fig. f:n7252 ).

Our new HST H image of this galaxy shows the disk to be actively forming stars, while the V-I color map shows dust to be intertwined between the arms. In light of the results from recent hydrodynamical simulations bar02 , and the observation of tidal gas streaming back into the central regions of this remnant from the tidal tails hib94, hib95b , it seems likely that this central disk structure results from the re-accretion of tidally raised gas into the now-relaxed central potential. The nucleus appears resolved, and is very bright, as we show in Section s:mergerstage .

sources.summary: The paper models NGC 7252 as a proto-elliptical merger remnant formed 620 Myr ago, using young cluster ages and spatial distributions to favor shock-induced first-passage star formation.

quotes.quote: Using interactive software barnes09 to match dynamical models to the observed morphology and kinematics of mergers, we built new models of NGC 7252 with the two star formation rules described above chien09a . In our models, this proto-elliptical galaxy formed by the merger of two similar gas-rich disk galaxies which fell together 620  Myr ago. Fig.  fig4 shows the spatial distribution of stellar particles of different ages using the two rules. Although on-going star formation occurs in the central regions in both simulations, the shock-induced simulation predicts that the products of past interaction-induced star formation are also dispersed around the remnant and along the tails.

In addition to comparing our simulations with the observed kinematics and morphologies, we performed a detailed analysis of cluster ages in NGC 7252 (Fig.  fig5 ). hereafter SS98 schweizer98 found 6 young clusters which lie between 3-15  kpc ( 10-35 ) from the center of NGC 7252 and have ages of 400-600  Myr, indicating that they formed early in the recent merger; the ages of these clusters are plotted in Fig.  fig5 .

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 7252 as the “Atoms for peace” merger remnant, with long tails, loops, many shells, a dusty disk, complex kinematics, and recent disk-galaxy coalescence.

quotes.quote: NGC 7252, Fig. fig:7252 , is a famous merger-remnant galaxy, also known under the nickname “Atoms for peace.” The galaxy is equipped with two long tails, several loops, and many (more than ten) shells. The HST image also reveals a dusty disk in the inner parts. The VLT-VIMOS integral-field spectrograph observations uncover complex stellar kinematics with a clear prolate-rotating component, while the gas shows precise oblate rotation in the inner parts and prolate rotation in the outer parts wea18 . A major merger of two disk galaxies is a widely accepted scenario for the origin of NGC 7252 sch82,sch83,br82,br91,mbr93,hmc95 .

Combining the information from the on-going and past star formation and the gas and stellar morphology and kinematics with the results from theoretical studies, derived a probable time of the final coalescence of the merger to be within the last 200 Myr.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 7252 as a young merger system whose globular clusters have well-modeled Balmer strengths, uncertain metallicities, and a power-law luminosity function expected to evolve.

quotes.quote: On the other hand, Schweizer Seitzer showed that similarly old ( 500 Myr) GCs in NGC 7252 show Balmer line strengths that are well reproduced by the Bruzual Charlot (1996; BC96) models (which indeed produce larger Balmer equivalent widths than their 1995 models).

Metallicity estimates from spectra of young GCs in NGC 1275 and NGC 7252 were hampered by relatively large uncertainties, since 500 Myr old populations are dominated by A-type stars with intrinsically weak metallic features.

The luminosity functions (LFs) for GCs in young mergers (such as the Antennae or NGC 7252) are power laws with indices of 2 ; . This distribution contrasts markedly with the Gaussian profiles found for LFs of old GC systems such as that of the Milky Way or giant ellipticals . However, various destruction mechanisms ---such as 2-body evaporation, bulge and disk shocking, and stellar mass loss--- should remove the less massive and/or more diffuse clusters as the galaxy ages. Hence, a system of GCs created during a merger may eventually yield a peaked distribution of clusters similar to what is seen for old GCs , .

sources.summary: The paper presents NGC 7252 as a gas-rich, roughly 1 Gyr-old merger remnant resembling giant ellipticals in luminosity, structure, halo clusters, and E+A spectrum.

quotes.quote: Locally, M_ H_2 ranges from 0.6 10 ^ 10 M_ for an aging merger remnant like NGC 7252 through 1.5 10 ^ 10 M_ for an ongoing merger like The Antennae to 3 10 ^ 10 M_ for extreme ULIRGs, while M_ H_2 2 10^ 10 M_ in a QSO at z=2.56 (Solomon et al. \ 2003) and also in one at z=6.42 (Walter et al. \ 2003).

First, the similarities between recent, 1 Gyr-old merger remnants like NGC 3921 or NGC 7252 and giant Es (e.g., Toomre 1977; Schweizer 1982, 1996; Barnes 1998) are worth re-emphasizing. The above two remnants currently have luminosities of 2.8 L^ _V and will still shine with 1.0 L^ _V after 10 - 12 Gyr of evolution. They feature ^ 1/4 type light distributions, power-law cores, U BV I color gradients, and velocity dispersions typical of Es. Both also possess many young, metal-rich halo globular clusters. They show integrated ``E + A'' spectra indicative of b 10 starbursts (Fritze-von Alvensleben Gerhardt 1994), as do many other similar young merger remnants in the local universe (Zabludoff et al. \ 1996).

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 7252 as an older merger-remnant comparison whose young cluster population, age, metallicity, and effective radii contrast with those in the Antennae.

quotes.quote: We briefly compare the young star cluster population of the Antennae to the older one in the merger remnant NGC 7252 and point out that the intercomparison of young cluster populations in an age sequence of interacting and merged galaxies may become an interesting approach to study in detail the role of external dynamical effects. Comparison with young star clusters in NGC 7252 (Whitmore 1993), the two brightest of which have spectroscopy available (Schweizer Seitzer 1993), confirmed a metallicity of Z Z_ predicted from our global starburst modelling in this Sc Sc merger remnant. The mean age of the young star cluster population was shown to agree well with the global burst age of 1.3 Gyr, and ages derived from solar metallicity models would differ by a factor 2 (see Fritze v. Alvensleben Burkert 1995 for details).

Similar to NGC 7252, though less advanced, NGC 4038/39 seems to be a merger of two gas-rich spirals of comparable mass. Though not known very accurately, the progenitor spirals of the Antennae may probably have been of type Sc Sc as in NGC 7252 on a similar kind of reasoning as in that case. And indeed, the mean effective radius of the NGC 7252 clusters is 7 pc, significantly smaller than the R_ eff = 13 pc of all the Antennae's clusters, despite the considerably larger distance to NGC 7252.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 7252 as a late-stage merger remnant with tidal tails, high nuclear luminosity density, blue central colors, and merger-triggered recent star formation.

quotes.quote: The two latest-stage mergers in the list are NGC 3921 and NGC 7252, which have tidal tails but show no remaining signs of two galaxies with a separate identity. To create a sample for our study, we sought galaxies with morphological properties similar to NGC 3921 and 7252 from the Catalogs of Arp (1966) and Vorontsov-Velyaminov (1977), and from the imaging survey of (UV-bright) Markarian galaxies by Mazzarella Boroson (1993). This yielded a sample of 19 galaxies with cz < 10000 km s ^ 1 , of which we imaged 14 galaxies (including NGC 3921 and 7252).

The J-H and H-K color profiles of NGC 3921 and 7252 show that they become bluer towards the center, presumably due to recent star formation. This is consistent with the detection of strong Balmer absorption lines in ground-based spectra of these galaxies (KSB98). NGC 34 becomes redder towards the center, probably as a result of dust absorption. NGC 34 is the most IR-luminous galaxy of those that we observed, suggesting the presence of ongoing or recent star formation in this galaxy as well. The high luminosity densities observed in NGC 34, 3921 and 7252 are probably a direct consequence of recent star formation triggered by a merger.

sources.summary: NGC 7252 is described as the latest-stage Toomre merger remnant, with a nuclear spiral, one nucleus, and a power-law nuclear surface-brightness profile.

quotes.quote: Our images of the latest-stage merger remnant of the Toomre sequence, NGC 7252, show a nuclear spiral (see Figure f:seventytwofiftytwovk ), as already detected in the optical and described in our previous study (Paper I). Only one nucleus is identified in all three filter passbands, consistent with previous ground-based studies e.g., sch82,hib94 and with the general notion of NGC 7252 being a prototype merger remnant, with an estimated age of about 1 Gyr e.g., sch98 .

The profiles for the nuclei NGC 3256 N, NGC 3509, NGC 4039, NGC 4676 B, NGC 7252 and NGC 7592 W are essentially pure power-laws with an almost constant slope through the fitted region. The _ 0.15 values vary between 0.94 and 1.58, therefore belonging comfortably to the regime of power-law galaxies. Some deviations from the best fit are seen in the profiles of NGC 3256, NGC 7252, and NGC 7592, most likely due to intrinsic structure in these nuclei.

sources.summary: The paper presents HST/WFC3 imaging of NGC 7252, describing it as a merger remnant with many young massive clusters and analyzing their extended luminosity profiles and radii.

quotes.quote: We present Hubble Space Telescope (HST) Wide-Field Camera 3 (WFC3) images of the merger remnant NGC 7252. In particular, we focus on the surface brightness profiles and effective radii \ of 36 young massive clusters (YMCs) within the galaxy. All the clusters have masses exceeding 10^5 \ and are, despite the 64 Mpc distance to the galaxy, (partly) resolved on the HST images.

NGC 7252 is a prototypical merger remnant that hosts one of the largest populations of young massive star clusters known (Whitmore et al. 1993; Miller et al. 1997, hereinafter M97), including two clusters with stellar masses exceeding 10^7 \ (Maraston et al. 2004, hereinafter M04; Bastian et al. 2006, hereinafter B06). Interested by the extreme nature of the YMCs in NGC 7252, i.e. their high masses and relatively large ages ( 400 Myr), we have obtained HST /WFC3 images of NGC 7252 to study the luminosity profiles of these clusters in detail.

sources.summary: NGC 7252 is described as a late-stage merger with a single-nucleus remnant, de Vaucouleurs profile, tidal-tail dwarf candidates, and new multiwavelength observations.

quotes.quote: NGC 7252 (``Atoms for Peace'') is a prototype late-stage merger from the Toomre sequence of interacting galaxies Toomre77 . Numerical models have been able to reproduce its morphology and kinematics by simulating the merger of two disc galaxies falling together 600-700 Myr ago Hibbard95, Chien10 . The central remnant has a single nucleus and a de Vaucouleurs luminosity profile, suggesting the morphological transformation into an elliptical galaxy Schweizer82, Hibbard94 . Using VLA observations, Hibbard94 identified two TDG candidates along the tidal tails, but the spatial and velocity resolutions of the data were too low to study their internal kinematics. In this paper we show that (i) both TDG candidates are associated with a steep velocity gradient that represents a kinematic discontinuity along the tail (Sect. sec:N7252kin ), (ii) both galaxies have relatively high metallicities, confirming that they are not pre-existing dwarfs but are forming out of pre-enriched tidal material (Sect. sec:N7252metal ), and (iii) the TDG to the North-West has a complex H kinematics, which is likely dominated by turbulent motions (Sect. sec:N7252ha ). For both TDGs we present 3D disc models in Sect. sec:3Dmodels . In this section we describe the collection, reduction, and analysis of new multiwavelength data of NGC 7252

sources.summary: The paper studies NGC7252 as a post-merger galaxy whose UV colors reveal a circumnuclear star-forming ring and suppressed central star formation possibly linked to AGN feedback.

quotes.quote: Here we explore the pattern of recent star formation in the central region of the post-merger galaxy NGC7252 using high resolution UV images from the UVIT on ASTROSAT. The UVIT images with 1.2 and 1.4 arcsec resolution in the FUV and NUV are used to construct a FUV-NUV colour map of the central region. The FUV–NUV pixel colour map for this canonical post-merger galaxy reveals a blue circumnuclear ring of diameter 10" (3.2 kpc) with bluer patches located over the ring. Based on a comparison to single stellar population models, we show that the ring is comprised of stellar populations with ages 300 Myr, with embedded star-forming clumps of younger age ( 150Myr).

The suppressed star formation in the central region, along with the recent finding of a large amount of ionised gas, leads us to speculate that this ring may be connected to past feedback from a central super-massive black hole that has ionised the hydrogen gas in the central 4" 1.3 kpc.

sources.summary: The paper uses deep imaging of merger remnant NGC 7252 to show that its blue main body and faint merger features could masquerade as a blue elliptical at higher redshift.

quotes.quote: Based on deep optical imaging data of the post-merger galaxy, NGC 7252, we demonstrate that the galaxy can appear as an elliptical galaxy if it is observed at higher redshifts. The main body and the low surface brightness merger features found at the outskirts of the galaxy are blue in the optical g- colour map. We argue that the higher-redshift blue elliptical galaxies discovered in surveys as shallow as the SDSS or DECaLS may be advanced mergers whose defining tidal features fall below the detection limits of the surveys.

NGC 7252 (RA:22:20:44.7,Dec:24:40:42) is a nearby major merger remnant with a spectroscopic redshift (z) = 0.0159 Rothberg_2006 . The optical g,,z band imaging data of NGC 7252 were taken from Data Release 10 of the legacy survey DECaLS Dey_2019 .

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 7252 as a post-merger benchmark with shells, tidal tails, halos, ongoing star formation, compact central spiral features, and merger signatures that fade when degraded.

quotes.quote: The formation of massive elliptical galaxy is thought to arise from the merger of two equal-mass spiral galaxies Toomre_1972 , such as the famous post-merger system NGC 7252. This galaxy system has strong tidal features in the form of shells, tidal tails, and halos when analyzed in optical imaging. The current consensus is that the system is a post-merger remnant formed from the recent merger of two similarly massive disk galaxies, and that it hosts significant ongoing star formation from a recent merger event Schweizer_1982 . The central regions of the galaxy show compact spiral features in high-resolution HST imaging Whitmore_1993 . When this galaxy system is placed at higher redshift by slightly degrading the image quality, the strong merger features can fade to faint halos as are detected in the blue ETGs presented here George_2023 .

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 7252 as an isolated merger remnant whose roughly 1 Gyr-old interaction formed W3 and may exemplify mergers capable of producing ultracompact massive stellar systems.

quotes.quote: We have determined the dynamical mass of the most luminous stellar cluster known to date, i.e. object W3 in the merger remnant galaxy NGC 7252. The dynamical mass is estimated from the velocity dispersion measured with the high-resolution spectrograph UVES on VLT. Our result is the astonishingly high velocity dispersion of =45 5 km/s . Combined with the large cluster size R_ eff =17.5 1.8 pc , this translates into a dynamical virial mass for W3 of (8 2) 10^7 . This mass is in excellent agreement with the value ( 7.2 10^7 ) we previously estimated from the cluster luminosity ( M_ V =-16.2 ) by means of stellar M/L ratios predicted by Simple Stellar Population models (with a Salpeter IMF) and confirms the heavy-weight nature of this object. This results points out that the NGC 7252-type of mergers are able to form stellar systems with masses up to 10^8 .

Tidal stripping might be more efficient, but as stated in Section 1, its impact is difficult to assess for our object since current N-body simulations (Baumgardt Makino 2003) refer to clusters in spiral-like gravitational potential for the parent galaxy, while NGC 7252 has a de Vaucouleurs profile (Schweizer 1982). However, according to these simulations, the amount of dynamical mass-loss decreases strongly with an increasing number of particles, therefore it is probably negligible for W3 because of its very large number of stars (cfr. Section 4).

In any case, we have learned that the galaxy interaction that has originated NGC 7252 roughly 1 Gyr ago was also capable to form a stellar system whose structural properties fall into a domain of rare objects. These objects at a given mass are more compact than dwarf galaxies, and at given compactness are more massive than GCs, and start populating a previously empty region of the fundamental plane. It would be valuable to explore under which conditions current simulations of NGC 7252-type mergers (e.g. Mihos Hernquist 1996) can produce W3-like objects.

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 7252 as a late-stage merger with massive TDG candidates in two tails whose HI kinematics argue against projection-induced condensations.

quotes.quote: NGC 7252: The late-state merger NGC 7252 contains a massive TDG candidate in its north-western tidal tail. The latter is located at a distance of 80 kpc from the parent galaxy, at the extremity of the stellar tail and just before a significant decrease in the observed HI column density (Hibbard et al. 1994) A faint HI tidal extends much further than the optical counterpart . The linear structure of the stellar tail raises again the question of projection effects.

In the eastern tail of NGC 7252, a massive TDG candidate, very similar to that present in the north-western tail, is also observed (Hibbard et al. 1994). According to the HI kinematical data of Hibbard et al. (1994), the velocity gradient is nearly constant between the parent galaxy and this TDG candidate, so that this object is likely a real condensation of matter.

sources.summary: NGC 7252 is discussed as an older merger remnant whose young star-cluster luminosity function, age, distance, and completeness limit are compared with the Antennae system.

quotes.quote: A somewhat similar procedure is used for YSCs in NGC 7252 and NGC 3921 by Miller Fall (1997), who find that power-laws are preferred over Gaussians for the MFs of these YSC systems. NGC 7252 and NGC 3921 are the two oldest merger remnants from Toomre's (1977) list. Large enough YSC systems have been detected in both of them to define the bright end of their LFs (Whitmore 1993, Miller 1997, Schweizer 1996). Distances, however, are larger than to NGC 4038/39 by factors 3 and 4 for NGC 7252 and NGC 3921, respectively, pushing the completeness limit to significantly higher luminosities. The higher mean ages of the YSCs in these cases ( 650 - 750 Myr for NGC 7252 and 250 - 750 Myr for NGC 3921, depending on metallicity) add another argument in favour of them being young GCs, since they have survived 10 t_ cross (cf. Schweizer 1996).

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 7252 as a late Toomre-sequence merger remnant with optical loops, tidal tails, concentrated nuclear star formation, and radio emission.

quotes.quote: NGC 7252 -- This well-known merger, called ``The Atoms for Peace'', shows remarkable optical loops and tidal tails surrounding it (Dopita et al. ). This is the last system within the Toomre ( ) sequence of interacting galaxies. The star formation is strongly concentrated towards the nucleus and has a counterpart in the radio emission at 4.86 GHz. According to Mihos et al. ( ), the SFR had dropped in this object by two thirds from the time of coalescence, estimated to occur 1 Gyr ago.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 7252, “Atoms for Peace,” as a late-stage merger remnant with gas-rich tidal tails whose morphology and kinematics simulations reproduce.

quotes.quote: NGC 7252 (also known as ``Atoms for Peace'') is a late-stage merger remnant with two gas-rich tidal tails extending to the east and north-west. Numerical simulations borne1991merger,hibbard1995dynamical, chien2010dynamically were able to reproduce the observed morphology and kinematics of NGC 7252 from the merging of two disk galaxies. hibbard1994cold identified two TDG candidates in the North-Western and Eastern tails (NGC 7252NW and NGC 7252E). These star-forming complexes were confirmed as bona-fide TDGs based on their H kinematics Bournaud2004 , \ kinematics lelli2015gas and relatively high gas metallicities lelli2015gas .

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 7252 as a gas-rich merger remnant whose H I lies mostly in tidal tails, with atomic gas, star formation, and molecular gas depletion making it likely to resemble E/S0 galaxies later.

quotes.quote: Hibbard et al. (1994) studied the gas properties of the merger remnant NGC 7252 and found that although it is gas rich compared to ellipticals and S0s, it is likely to resemble these galaxies in few Gyrs. In particular, the H I is mostly found in the tidal tails, while the atomic gas content of the remnant body is typical to that of E/S0s. There is also some evidence for on-going conversion of the returning tidal tail H I into stars. Moreover, the molecular gas in the NGC 7252 is also likely to be depleted within the next few Gyrs, due to modest on-going star-formation.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 7252 as a gas-rich spiral merger remnant whose strong global starburst formed many young globular clusters that survived the remnant's violent relaxation.

quotes.quote: The post-burst spiral -- spiral merger remnant NGC 7252 with two long, gas-rich tidal tails pointing at an age of 1 Gyr after the onset of the strong interaction and its blue and radially constant colors and very strong Balmer absorption line spectrum must have experienced a very strong and global starburst increasing its stellar mass by as much as 40 between 600 and 1000 Myr ago. Conservative estimates still lead to a very high SFE 30 (Fritze -- v. Alvensleben Gerhard 1994). A large number of Star Clusters (SCs) formed throughout the main body, many of them apparently so strongly bound that they managed to survive for 500 900 Myr the violent relaxation phase that restructured the remnant into a de Vaucouleurs profile (cf. Fritze -- v. Alvensleben Burkert 1995 and Schweizer 2002 for a recent review). Most of these star clusters are young Globular Clusters (GCs) based on their ages, luminosities, and radii.

sources.summary: The review treats NGC 7252 as a recent gas-rich merger remnant hosting many young globular clusters, mostly formed over 100-200 Myr, centrally concentrated, and near solar metallicity.

quotes.quote: HST studies of recent merger remnants such as NGC 3921 , NGC 7252 , and NGC 3597 show that these galaxies typically host about 10 ^2 -10 ^3 point-like sources that appear to be mostly young GCs ( 1 Gyr old). (It is not that there are no old GCs in these relatively distant remnants, only that the YGCs are much brighter and more easily studied.) Age-dating based both on broad-band photometry and spectroscopy shows that the majority of these YGCs formed in relatively short, 100--200 Myr time spans during the mergers. The YGCs appear strongly concentrated toward their hosts' centers, half of them lying typically within 5 kpc from the nucleus. The few spectroscopic studies that have so far been made of such YGCs invariably show them to be of approximately solar metallicity: [ Z ] = 0.0 0.1 in NGC 7252 , 0.0 0.5 in NGC 3921 , and---for the intermediate-age, 3--5 Gyr old GCs in more advanced remnants--- [ Z ] = 0.0 0.15 in NGC 1316 and 0.1 0.2 in NGC 5128 .

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 7252 as a merger-remnant modeling benchmark, citing H I velocity constraints, a model matching its morphology and kinematics, and a direct-disk collision solution.

quotes.quote: The methodology adopted by HM95 in their model of the well-known merger remnant NGC 7252 includes several key practices found in other successful models: Detailed velocity information, in the form of H I data HGvGS94 , was available to constrain the model.
Simulation particles were plotted over orthogonal projections of the data cube to show that the final model reproduced both the morphology and the kinematics of NGC 7252.

Models of NGC 7252 illustrate this point; early attempts using very limited velocity data concluded that this object resulted from a merger of two retrograde disks BR91 , but once high-quality H I data was available a very convincing solution involving a collision of two direct disks was soon found HM95 .

sources.summary: The review describes NGC 7252 as a merger remnant with two countermoving H I tidal tails, displaced gas and stellar extents, shells, and loops.

quotes.quote: NGC 7252 (Arp 226) . --- Hibbard et al. (1994) detect two extended, countermoving H i tidal tails in the "Atoms-for-Peace" galaxy NGC 7252 with a combined length 243 kpc, while no H i is found in the central stellar body. While the stars and H i emission in the eastern (E) tail have similar extent, the H i emission in the north-western (NW) tail extends 50 further than the stars. This is one of many signatures of the original spiral galaxy progenitors whose major merger resulted in an elliptical galaxy remnant, notable for its stellar shells and loops (see also Hibbard Mihos 1995).

sources.summary: The survey recovers the known EELR/cloud in merger remnant NGC 7252, noting it lies about 5 kpc from the nucleus and only partly meets the 10-kpc threshold.

quotes.quote: We also recover the previously-known region in NGC 7252.

The EELR in NGC 7252 is centered about 5 kpc from the nucleus in projection and barely extends outward to our minimum search radius of 10 kpc, so we do not always include it in the sample comparisons which follow.
The cloud in the merger remnant NGC 7252 was previously known NGC7252 and only partly passes our 10-kpc threshold.

sources.summary: The review describes NGC 7252 as direct evidence for a disk-disk merger transitioning toward an elliptical while retaining interstellar gas and prominent tidal tails.

quotes.quote: The first direct observational evidence for the transition from a disk-disk merger toward an elliptical was presented in the optical study of NGC 7252 by . The brightness distribution over most of the main body of this galaxy which is shown in Figure fig3 is closely approximated by a de Vacouleurs ( ^ 1/4 ) profile. However, NGC 7252 still contains large amounts of interstellar gas and exhibits a pair of prominent tidal tails (see Figure fig3 b); neither property is typical of ellipticals.

gmw_00000476.name: Andromeda XXV
gmw_00000476.overview: Andromeda XXV is a large, bright dwarf spheroidal galaxy located at a projected distance of 90 kpc from Andromeda [arXiv:1102.2902, arXiv:1302.6590]. It hosts a single, metal-poor stellar population estimated to be 9 to 13 billion years old [arXiv:1606.06862, arXiv:1808.08087], along with a central stellar overdensity that may be a star cluster or nucleus [arXiv:1606.06862, arXiv:1704.06262].

sources.summary: The paper studies Andromeda XXV’s variable stars, distance, old metal-poor stellar population, and a central stellar overdensity that may be its nucleus.

quotes.quote: We present B and V time-series photometry of Andromeda XXV, the third galaxy in our program on the Andromeda's satellites, that we have imaged with the Large Binocular Cameras of the Large Binocular Telescope. The field of Andromeda XXV is found to contain 63 variable stars, for which we present light curves and characteristics of the light variation (period, amplitudes, variability type, mean magnitudes, etc.). The sample includes 58 RR Lyrae variables (46 fundamental-mode RRab, and 12 first-overtone RRc, pulsators), three anomalous Cepheids, one eclipsing binary system and one unclassified variable. The average period of the RRab stars ( Pab = 0.60 =0.04 days) and the period-amplitude diagram place Andromeda XXV in the class of the Oosterhoff-Intermediate objects. From the average luminosity of the RR Lyrae stars we derive for the galaxy a distance modulus of (m-M) _0 = 24.63 0.17 mag.

The color-magnitude diagram reveals the presence in Andromeda XXV of a single, metal-poor ( [ Fe/H ] = 1.8 dex) stellar population as old as 10-12 Gyr traced by a conspicuous red giant branch and the large population of RR Lyrae stars. We discovered a spherically-shaped high density of stars near the galaxy center.
