There is also evidence of tidally induced SF, as the intermediate-age and young SF events may correspond with pericentric passages of its orbit around the Milky Way Pa22 .

sources.summary: The paper uses Fornax as a worked example, finding its stellar mass and central density are hard to reproduce without strong feedback and better matched by NIHAO-like core formation.

quotes.quote: In particular: Using our proposed technique on the Fornax dwarf galaxy provides interesting results. Specifically, we find that observations of the central density and stellar mass of Fornax are difficult to reproduce under assumptions of minimal baryonic feedback. A model including stronger DM core formation is required to produce both parameters simultaneously.

For example, the only satellites that simultaneously matched the observed M_ and M_ 1/2 of Fornax in the APOSTLE feedback emulator (which prefers cuspier halos) had lower pericenter and higher central density than expected from observations. On the other hand, the NIHAO feedback emulator, which cores satellite galaxies more efficiently, yielded a population of high- M_ , low- M_ 1/2 galaxies in much better agreement with the observed parameters for Fornax.

sources.summary: The paper applies a non-parametric kinematic mass estimator to the Fornax dSph galaxy, finding an extended dark halo and a large plausible mass range.

quotes.quote: The technique is applied to a sample of 181 stars in the Fornax dSph galaxy. We show that the galaxy contains a significant, extended dark halo some ten times more massive than its baryonic component.

We therefore consider the two plateaux in the M() curve of Figure fig:fornax22 to bracket the region of plausible Fornax mass as measured from an N 200 sample by the estimation technique. The resulting interval of 1.0 10^8 M _ < M _ Fornax < 3.5 10^8 M _ is of considerably larger mass than the results of the classical analysis. The estimation approach then offers an attractive alternative to variants of the classical analysis that require parametric dynamical models to interpret kinematic data for dSphs.

sources.summary: The paper images a stellar overdensity in the Fornax dwarf spheroidal galaxy, finding a 1.4 Gyr burst population and arguing its gas likely belonged to Fornax rather than an accreted companion.

quotes.quote: We have imaged the recently discovered stellar overdensity located approximately one core radius from the center of the Fornax dwarf spheroidal galaxy using the Magellan Clay 6.5m telescope with the Magellan Instant Camera (MagIC). Superb seeing conditions allowed us to probe the stellar populations of this overdensity and of a control field within Fornax to a limiting magnitude of R=26. The color-magnitude diagram of the overdensity field is virtually identical to that of the control field with the exception of the presence of a population arising from a very short (less than 300 Myr in duration) burst of star formation 1.4 Gyr ago. Coleman et al. have argued that this overdensity might be related to a shell structure in Fornax that was created when Fornax captured a smaller galaxy. Our results are consistent with this model, but we argue that the metallicity of this young component favors a scenario in which the gas was part of Fornax itself.

sources.summary: The paper measures RGB-star metallicities for Fornax as one of four nearby dSph galaxies, finding no stars below -3 dex and a metal-poor tail differing from the Galactic halo.

quotes.quote: As part of the Dwarf galaxies Abundances and Radial-velocities Team (DART) Programme, we have measured the metallicities of a large sample of stars in four nearby dwarf spheroidal galaxies (dSph): Sculptor, Sextans, Fornax and Carina. The low mean metal abundances and the presence of very old stellar populations in these galaxies have supported the view that they are fossils from the early Universe. However, contrary to naive expectations, we find a significant lack of stars with metallicities below 3 dex in all four systems. This suggests that the gas that made up the stars in these systems had been uniformly enriched prior to their formation. Furthermore, the metal-poor tail of the dSph metallicity distribution is significantly different from that of the Galactic halo.

sources.summary: For Fornax, caustic cleaning mostly prunes outliers; the caustics vary little, its MOND behavior differs from the other dSphs, and its final M/L is appropriate.

quotes.quote: The caustic technique has not significantly altered the magnitude of the losVD for the most distant and luminous dSphs (Leo I and Fornax): for these larger dSphs, it has merely pruned some outlying stars. However, for Sextans and Sculptor, the losVDs have changed considerably. The typical error bars on each losVD bin in are 1.5 , but we have used fewer bins, thus increasing the number of stars per bin and decreasing the uncertainty on each losVD value. Additionally, the three sets of losVDs identified by the caustic technique, found using different star masses, do not systematically differ by more than 0.5 until the last data point for Carina, Sextans and Sculptor (see Figure fig:veldisp_caus ). The three caustics of Fornax and Leo I vary even less.
Apart from Fornax, all the other dSphs are consistent at 1- with any between -3 and -0.5, with a suitably chosen _ DM,0 and r_ DM .

Following exactly the same method, we have re-analysed the dSphs after removing the interloper stars and have found that it leaves all three more luminous dSphs (Fornax, Leo I and Sculptor) with appropriate M/L s (Figure fig:MLmond ), Sextans has a borderline M/L (2.7 0.5), but the least luminous dSph Carina still has a suspiciously high M/L (5.9 ^ +1.4 _ 1.1 ).

sources.summary: The paper estimates Fornax as one of the most massive Milky Way dwarf spheroidals and uses its datapoint to compare dark-halo models with CDM expectations.

quotes.quote: We produce a mass measure for each of the dSphs and find that the two most massive of the Milky Way dSphs are the most luminous, namely Sgr ( M (< 1.7 ) 2.8 10^8 ) and Fornax ( 1.3 10^8 ).

Thanks to the labours of a number of groups see e.g., Ma93,Kl02,Kl04,Wi04,Wa09 , radial velocity surveys with multi-object spectrographs have now provided datasets of thousands of stars for the bright dSphs, like Fornax and Draco.

For the specific case of Fornax, this allows us to compare the expectations of a CDM cosmological model with our results. It is interesting to note that the models selected by the CDM scenario for the Fornax dSph are those at the highest end of the _0(r_0) relation, and that thus have a luminous and a dark component with similar scale lengths ( 1 ).

sources.summary: The paper includes Fornax among the bright Milky Way dwarf spheroidals, treats it as dark-matter dominated, and finds its luminosity implies unusually high star-formation efficiency for its inferred halo.

quotes.quote: Our final sample therefore contains 9 dwarf spheroidals: Fornax, Leo I, Sculptor, Leo II, Sextans, Carina, Ursa Minor, Canes Venatici I, and Draco. All of these galaxies are known to be dark matter dominated at mateo1998 : wolf2010 find that their dynamical mass-to-light ratios at range from 10-300 .

Two of the three least luminous dwarfs, Draco and Ursa Minor, are consistent with the most massive hosts, while the three most luminous dwarfs (Fornax, Leo I, and Sculptor) are consistent with hosts of intermediate mass ( 18-20 ).
For example, the most luminous dSph of the MW, Fornax, has an inferred star formation efficiency of 0.2 , a value that is approached only at the scale of MW-mass halos.

sources.summary: The paper treats Fornax as a bright Milky Way dwarf spheroidal whose kinematic constraints test SIDM subhaloes; its large half-light radius makes it inconsistent with the 15 most massive simulated subhaloes.

quotes.quote: The dSphs sample consists of the 9 galaxies used in Boylan2011b : Fornax, Leo I, Sculptor, Leo II, Sextans, Carina, Ursa Minor, Canes Venatici I and Draco, selected with the criterion L_V>10^5 M _ .

Looking at Figure fig:sub_vcirc we see that at least three of the dSphs (Fornax, Sextans and Canes Venatici I, that have a value of r_ 1/2 1 kpc) are inconsistent with the circular velocity profile of the 15 most massive subhaloes in all our SIDM simulations, particularly for the cases with a velocity-dependent cross section. This however, does not mean that there are no subhaloes in these simulations that are a good match to these galaxies. These subhaloes exist but are considerably less massive than the ones we show in Figure fig:sub_vcirc and are more affected by the limited resolution of our simulations.

sources.summary: The paper compares Fornax with a simulated subhalo and cites evidence that Fornax favors a cored dark-matter profile, while noting alternative cusp-compatible studies.

quotes.quote: The total rotation curve (DM and baryonic mass) for an SPH simulated subhalo is shown in Figure fornax , which closely matches the derived v_c at the half light radius of Fornax, along with the rotation curve for the same subhalo in a DM-only run without baryons.

Studies using this method Walker2011, Amorisco2012, Battaglia2008, Agnello2012 conclude that both Fornax (with slope measured out to 1 kpc) and Sculptor (measured interior to 500 pc) favor cored density profiles, and exclude cuspy density profiles at high significance. It has also been argued that the fact that Fornax's globular cluster orbits have not decayed by dynamical friction requires a cored DM profile Goerdt2006, Cole2012 .
Despite the results above that favor a cored profile in Fornax, other studies have found it to be consistent with a cusp Strigari2010, Breddels2013b .

sources.summary: The paper includes Fornax proper-motion data, notes agreement with independent measurements, and finds its orbital pole consistent with the common satellite-plane direction.

quotes.quote: Compared to the PM list compiled by Metz2008 , we also include a new HST based PM measurement for Sagittarius 2010AJ....139..839P and an additional ground-based PM measurement for Fornax 2011AJ....142...93M .

Using this method, 2008ApJ...688L..75W have estimated the PM of the four MW satellites Sculptor, Sextans, Carina and Fornax. They find agreement of their Fornax and Carina PMs with independent PM measurements but disagreement for Sculptor, which might be due to a rotational component in that galaxy.

The orbital poles of at least six satellites (LMC, SMC, Ursa Minor, Leo II, Draco and Fornax) are closely aligned with each other, being inclined by less than 27^ \ from their average direction (which points to [ l,b ] = [ 177^ ,-3^ ] ) \ and close to the minor axis of the VPOS.

sources.summary: The MOND modeling finds Fornax effectively isolated from the external field, with predicted dynamical mass-to-light ratios and central slopes matching observational estimates.

quotes.quote: Fornax Due to its large distance to the Galactic Centre and its large total luminosity (i.e. mass), Fornax (see Fig.  fig:fornax ) is effectively isolated and unaffected by the EFE. The resulting dynamical mass-to-light ratio, which is presented in the lower panel of Fig.  fig:fornax , depends on the radius, , and ranges between 10 and 50 / depending on the model. Contrary to the external field-dominated dSphs, the accuracy of the model has a strong influence on the resulting M_ dyn /L as a function of radius. Because this dSph is effectively isolated and has a cored baryonc matter density distribution, the central shape of the PDM profile has a power-law slope of = _ ph ()/ _ 0 0.5 .

sources.summary: The paper cites Fornax as a luminous Milky Way dwarf spheroidal whose chemical and dynamical peculiarities have prompted recent-merger interpretations, making it relevant to dwarf-dwarf merger histories.

quotes.quote: This may be a valid assumption for many of the dwarf galaxies of the Local Group, though observations of kinematic and chemical peculiarities in, for example, Fornax battaglia06,deboer12,hendricks14 , Sculptor e.g., tolstoy04 , Carina e.g., venn12 and And II ho12 have led to the suggestion that external factors, such as dwarf-dwarf encounters, should be considered more seriously.

Fornax is the second most luminous ( L 10 ^ 7 L_ ) dwarf spheroidal galaxy within the MW halo, after the Sagittarius dwarf. Several studies have found that it displays complex chemical and dynamical properties e.g., battaglia06, deboer12, hendricks14 , which has led to the suggestion that Fornax underwent a recent merger e.g., coleman04, yozin12, amorisco12 .

Although dwarf-dwarf mergers are not particularly common, neither are they extremely rare. Thus, our results highlight the importance of considering mergers in interpreting the star formation histories, metallicity distribution functions, and chemical abundance patterns of dwarf galaxies, particularly for systems such as Fornax or And II which display kinematic irregularities.

sources.summary: The review treats Fornax as a bright satellite whose low velocity dispersion remains difficult for a 0.6 cm^2/g SIDM model, implying another mechanism may be needed to lower luminous-satellite densities.

quotes.quote: However, even this value would not fully explain Fornax. As one of the brightest satellites, Fornax is expected to have been formed in one of the largest subhalos. It's observed low velocity dispersion cannot be fully fit by the 0.6 cm ^2 /g model. This tension suggests that even with core creation, some additional mechanism is still necessary to reduce the densities of the most luminous satellites enough to match observations.

sources.summary: The paper models Fornax as a classical Milky Way dwarf, using stellar kinematics to constrain its dark matter profile and finding relatively small uncertainty in its annihilation J value.

quotes.quote: Figure fig:mcmc displays samples from the posterior PDFs returned by MultiNest for Fornax and Segue 1---the most luminous classical dwarf and one of the least luminous ultra-faints, respectively. As the model that we adopt for the halo density profile is free (and unconstrained by, e.g., N-body considerations) the kinematic data of each dwarf is compatible with a wide range of profiles. Therefore, we apply three additional filters to the kinematically-allowed dark matter density profiles.

However, Sculptor and, to a lesser extent, Fornax and Carina have the most constraining kinematic sample, giving a very small range of allowed values for J and thus the smallest uncertainties (in agreement with the analysis of 2011MNRAS.418.1526C ).

sources.summary: The paper models Fornax dSph UV luminosities and says reionization scenarios may require its z=7 progenitor; lensing could approach sensitivity to observe it.

quotes.quote: Figure fig:fornax_burst_muv shows the probability distribution P( ) for the Fornax dwarf spheroidal (dSph) galaxy, computed from 1000 realizations of its SFH, at z 7 .

If (z 7) 13 galaxies are required to maintain reionization, as is the case in many models, then the progenitors of the Sagittarius and Fornax dSphs must contribute. And finally, if galaxies as faint as (z 7) 9.5 are required, then progenitors galaxies such as Draco, CVn I, and Phoenix -- which have L_V(z=0) (0.2-1) 10^ 6 L_ V, - may be necessary contributors to reionization. These results also provide context for what observations in the \ \ and with \ will see. At z 7 , \ can probe galaxies as faint as 16.5 , assuming magnifications of 5 in luminosity (1.75 mags); such observations may approach the depth required for observing the main progenitor of the LMC. \ deep field observations are expected to have a limiting magnitude of m_ AB =31.5 windhorst2006 , corresponding to 15.5 ; \ is therefore likely to reveal the z 7 progenitors of Magellanic irregulars. If a like campaign with \ could obtain a factor of 10 in magnification (2.5 mags), it would observe objects as faint as 13 ; this would just approach the sensitivity required to observe the progenitors of Fornax and Sagittarius, potentially revealing the faintest galaxies required for reionization.

Using the modeled UV luminosities, we have shown that Local Group galaxies such as the Fornax dSph and IC 1613 were, at z 2 , slightly less luminous ( 12.5 ) than the faintest galaxies \ is currently observing at that epoch ( 13 ). The LMC and SMC are predicted to be substantially more luminous at that time; many of the faint ( 15 ) galaxies observed at z 2 may evolve to become Magellanic irregulars in the local Universe.

sources.summary: The paper cites Fornax as a likely cored Milky Way dwarf spheroidal, arguing its globular clusters and light profile favor a central dark matter core.

quotes.quote: However, the brightest Milky Way dSph, Fornax, is almost certainly cored For Fornax, arguably the most robust constraint comes from its globular cluster (GC) system. Its GCs should fall to the centre of the dwarf in less than a Hubble time, creating a nucleated dwarf that is inconsistent with Fornax's observed light profile. This can be avoided if there is a central constant density core that causes dynamical friction to stall Goerdt:2006rw,2006astro.ph..6636R . Such a timing argument has been shown to be remarkably difficult to circumvent; it holds even if Fornax is triaxial or affected by tides 2012MNRAS.426..601C,2013MNRAS.431.2796K .

sources.summary: The paper models Fornax as a representative dwarf spheroidal whose low star-formation efficiency and truncation epoch suggest ionizing-background suppression after reionization.

quotes.quote: In this work, we choose Fornax and Leo II as two representative cases, where the epochs of tidal truncation under a constraint of the common surface-density scale of 20 M_ pc ^ 2 are given as z_ TT = 3.75 and 7.57 , respectively, i.e. subsequent and prior to the completion of the cosmic reionization. For these dSphs, the values of F indicating star formation efficiency are estimated as 0.065 (Fornax) and 0.212 (Leo II), thereby suggesting that the former dSph with z_ TT being lower than the redshift for the completion of the cosmic reionization is subject to the suppression of star formation caused by the ionizing background radiation.

Figure fig: inflow suggests that the observed peak of the star formation rate in Fornax and Leo II is indeed in good agreement with the epoch when the peak of the gas infall rate is achieved. The infall rate of gas inferred from the growth rate of dark halos in the common surface-density scale shows a peak at the epoch of tidal truncation and this peak epoch is reasonably in agreement with the epoch at which the star formation rate derived for the Milky Way satellites (Fornax and Leo II) is maximum.

sources.summary: The paper treats Fornax with Sculptor as a dwarf galaxy whose two metallicity subpopulations have been used to infer a central dark-matter core and reject an NFW cusp at 96 percent confidence.

quotes.quote: The existence of two kinematically and chemically distinct stellar subpopulations in the Sculptor and Fornax dwarf galaxies offers the opportunity to constrain the density profile of their matter haloes by measuring the mass contained within the well-separated half-light radii of the two metallicity subpopulations. Walker and Pe n arrubia have used this approach to argue that data for these galaxies are consistent with constant-density `cores' in their inner regions and rule out `cuspy' Navarro--Frenk--White (NFW) profiles with high statistical significance, particularly in the case of Sculptor.
walk used a likelihood method to separate samples of stars in Sculptor and Fornax into two metallicity subcomponents and applied these mass estimators to each of them.
walk derived values of for Sculptor and Fornax which exclude an NFW cusp at 99 and 96 per cent confidence levels, respectively, instead favouring a core.

sources.summary: The review highlights Fornax as a bright Milky Way satellite whose low central density remains difficult for CDM plus baryonic simulations to reconcile.

quotes.quote: In fact, it appeared that only lower-mass simulated subhalos had circular velocity curves that were more consistent with the observed v_ 1/2 points, but even then it was a challenge to match some surprisingly low-density satellites (e.g., Fornax, which is the brightest Milky Way satellite other than the Magellanic Clouds and the disrupting Sagittarius dwarf).

However, the surprisingly low densities of Fornax (a big satellite), Canis Venatici I, and Sextans remain difficult to reconcile with simulations of CDM + baryons . The conclusion is that baryons can address TBTF on halo mass scales 10^ 10 M_ , but are still not bringing about perfect agreement. Fornax, in particular, remains an intriguing problem .

sources.summary: The paper uses Fornax dwarf spheroidal globular clusters as comparison data, describing their metallicities and alpha-element ratios and contrasting Fornax 4 with more metal-poor Fornax clusters.

quotes.quote: In a pilot study we used integrated-light spectroscopy to determine chemical abundances for three globular clusters (Fornax 3, Fornax 4, and Fornax 5) in the Fornax dwarf spheroidal galaxy. We confirmed that Fornax 3 and Fornax 5 are relatively metal-poor compared to typical Milky Way and M31 halo GCs (both having [ Fe/H ] <-2 ), while Fornax 4 has [ Fe/H ] =-1.4 . The [ / Fe ] ratios ( [ Ca/Fe ] , [ Ti/Fe ] ) of Fornax 3 and Fornax 5 were found to be enhanced, although possibly slightly less so than in Milky Way GCs, while the [ / Fe ] ratio in Fornax 4 was only slightly elevated compared to Solar-scaled abundance patterns hereafter L12 Larsen2012a .
In the more metal-rich clusters, the [ Na/Fe ] ratios are generally lower than in Milky Way GCs, in particular in Fornax 4 and the cluster SC7 in NGC 6822.

sources.summary: The paper reconstructs Fornax and its globular clusters across redshift, finding cluster UV output can dominate the field and bias high-redshift luminosity and halo-mass inferences.

quotes.quote: We present the reconstructed evolution of rest-frame ultra-violet (UV) luminosities of the most massive Milky Way dwarf spheroidal satellite galaxy, Fornax, and its five globular clusters (GCs) across redshift, based on analysis of the stellar fossil record and stellar population synthesis modeling. We find that (1) Fornax's (proto-)GCs can generate 10-100 times more UV flux than the field population, despite comprising < 5 of the stellar mass at the relevant redshifts; (2) due to their respective surface brightnesses, it is more likely that faint, compact sources in the Hubble Frontier Fields (HFFs) are GCs hosted by faint galaxies, than faint galaxies themselves.

To illustrate the potential of this technique we focus on the Fornax dwarf spheroidal and its five GCs e.g., McConnachie2012TheGroup, deBoer2012TheGalaxy, Larsen2012ConstraintsDSph, deBoer2016FourDSph .

The right panel of fig:lumfunc shows that for the case of Fornax at z 7 , if its UV luminosity is dominated by a proto-GC, then the assigned halo mass would be over-estimated by a factor of 20 given our fiducial faint end slope.

sources.summary: The paper includes Fornax in its classical-dSph Jeans analysis and finds its soliton-plus-NFW transition radius is smaller than the stellar sample scales.

quotes.quote: More precisely, we solve axisymmetric Jeans equations and apply them to the kinematic data of the eight classical dSphs: Draco, Ursa Minor, Carina, Sextans, Leo I, Leo II, Sculptor and Fornax in order to investigate the global shape of ULA dark halo as well as to constrain on particle mass of ULA dark matter.

The Galactic Dwarf Spheroidal Galaxy Data In this work, we adopt the photometric and spectroscopic data of the resolved member stars in the eight luminous dSphs in the Milky Way: Draco, Ursa Minor, Carina, Sextans, Leo I, Leo II, Sculptor, and Fornax. This is because each have 200 stars with line-of-sight velocities from spectroscopy and well-constrained stellar distributions from photometry, so that these sample volumes might be enough to obtain more stringent limits on the mass range of m_ than those from other fainter dSphs such as ultra faint dwarf galaxies.
By contrast, however, it is found that for the case of Ursa Minor, Leo I and Fornax dSph, their transition radii are much smaller than their b_ and r_ 90 .

sources.summary: The paper presents Fornax as a well-studied dwarf spheroidal whose soliton-like core and surviving globular-cluster orbits support the ultralight dark-matter interpretation.

quotes.quote: In particular, the best studied Fornax dSph is determined by several different methods to have a core radius of 1.0 kpc with a density profile that is accurately fitted by the soliton form of DM, as shown in Figure 1, for which a Jeans analysis yields a boson mass of, m_ =0.8 0.2 10^ 22 eV . Furthermore, Fornax provides another independent and compelling argument for DM implied by the presence of ancient globular clusters on large orbits around Fornax. This is unexpected for discrete dark matter, such as CDM or black holes, that would be focused gravitationally by an orbiting globular cluster into a "wake", so the globular clusters should have migrated long ago to the center of Fornax . This ``dynamical friction" is not significant for DM, which cannot be confined to less than the de Broglie scale because of the Uncertainty Principle , leaving the Fornax globular cluster orbits little affected .

sources.summary: The paper says Fornax has supersolar [Eu/Mg] values that are difficult to explain, but its more extended measurements show no clear increasing [Eu/Mg] trend with metallicity.

quotes.quote: On the other hand, the trends of [ Eu/Mg ] with [ Fe/H ] are more difficult to understand in the larger dSphs, Fornax and Sagittarius. Both galaxies have supersolar values of [ Eu/Mg ] that are not straightforward to explain, highlighting that our understanding of the process is by no means complete. At the lowest metallicities in Sagittarius, [ Fe/H ] <-1 , the observed [ Eu/Ca ] =-0.04 Hansen18 is consistent with the solar value, therefore it is likely that this enhancement of [ Eu/Mg ] occurred late in the history of the galaxy. However, we note that the abundance ratios of [ Eu/Mg ] in Fornax (which has more extended measurements) show no clear signs of increasing trends of [ Eu/Mg ] with [ Fe/H ] .

sources.summary: The paper describes Fornax dSph as a massive, dark-matter-dominated Milky Way satellite whose six old globular clusters create a dynamical-friction timing problem.

quotes.quote: The Fornax dwarf spheroidal (dSph) galaxy is the most massive galaxy of its type orbiting the Milky Way (that shows no strong signs of tidal disruption) and has thus been extensively studied in the literature Coleman08,Poretti08,Larsen12,Cole12,DeBoer16,DelPino17, Reid19Fornax,Kowalczyk19,Boldrini18,WangMeiDES18 . The tendency for dSph galaxies to be heavily dynamically dominated by dark matter Aaronson83,Mateo98,Gilmore07 has made them ideal test beds for the nature of dark matter on cosmologically small scales WP11,MBKJBRev17 . One of Fornax's unique features is the set of six globular clusters associated with it Cole12,WangMeiClusterSix . These globular clusters have long puzzled astronomers as they all appear to be old ( 10 Gyr), yet dynamical friction calculations show that globular clusters with similar orbits should have long ago sunk to the center of the Fornax dSph, assuming that they had been in these orbits for a significant fraction of their lifetimes Tremaine76,Hernandez98,Oh2000,Goerdt06,Cole12 .

sources.summary: The paper includes Fornax among the largest Milky Way dSph satellites, adopts its SFH from deBoer12f, and links its rising [Eu/Mg] with metallicity to a delayed nucleosynthetic source.

quotes.quote: With the goal of better understanding the process and its nucleosynthetic site(s), we gathered the available chemical abundances of stars in the Milky Way and its three largest satellite dwarf spheroidal (dSph) galaxies: Sagittarius, Fornax, and Sculptor. The aim is to put these chemical abundances in context with the star formation histories (SFHs) of these galaxies.
The SFH of Fornax is adopted from deBoer12f .
However, the larger dSph galaxies, Sagittarius and Fornax have an unambiguous increase in [ Eu/Mg ] with [ Fe/H ] , suggestive of a delayed process.

sources.summary: The paper treats Fornax as one of the few known satellites expected to host LISA detections, using its constant star-formation history to predict detectable DWDs.

quotes.quote: We forecast that out of the known satellites, Sagittarius, Fornax, Sculptor, and the Magellanic Clouds can be detected with LISA.

We list stellar masses and distances of known satellites from mcc12 and report the number of LISA detections for our fiducial model in Table table:known_satellites . Of all known satellites, only Sagittarius, Fornax, Sculptor, and the Magellanic Clouds host detectable LISA sources.
For the Fornax galaxy wei14 reports a constant star formation history, that based on our binary population modelling predicts 0.2 detectable DWDs (cf. Fig. fig:detections ).

sources.summary: The paper compares Fornax abundances with prior studies, strongly revises its barium values downward, and places its s-process onset at higher metallicity than Sculptor.

quotes.quote: For a subset of common stars, we compared our derived abundances with previous studies and found an overall agreement, but strongly revised the barium abundances in Fornax. We showed that the previously reported high values in the Fornax dSph galaxy are an artifact of the abundance analysis that had been applied in the past.

We investigated the onset of the s-process for Sagittarius ( [ Fe/H ] =-0.57 ), Fornax ( [ Fe/H ] = 1.04 ) and Sculptor ( [ Fe/H ] =-1.57 ). [ Eu/Ba ] versus [ Ba/H ] shows a decreasing trend for high Ba contents similar to the MW.
We note a late onset in Sagittarius and Fornax, relative to that of type Ia SNe.

sources.summary: The paper applies spherical Jeans modeling to Fornax dwarf spheroidal kinematics, comparing two core-halo relations for fuzzy dark matter and finding halo-mass degeneracy hides the relation's impact.

quotes.quote: To this end, we apply the spherical Jeans analysis to the kinematic data of the Fornax dwarf spheroidal galaxy, which has the largest data set among the Galactic dwarf satellites. We perform the Jeans analysis For the dynamical analysis we adopt in this work, the interested reader may find further details in Kohei2021 . using two different core–halo relations, which are suggested by Schive2014b and this work, and then we map the posterior probability distributions of the FDM mass through the MCMC technique based on Bayesian statistics. Comparing the posteriors, there is no clear difference in the shape of those distributions, including that of FDM mass, but this is due to the fact that there exists a degeneracy between halo mass and FDM mass.Therefore, this degeneracy makes it hard to see the impact that the core–halo relation has in the Jeans analysis.

sources.summary: The paper cites the Fornax Dwarf Spheroidal timing problem as an orbital-decay tension: CDM predicts too much dynamical friction, while FDM or SFDM may reduce it.

quotes.quote: In another context, the Fornax globular clusters timing problem, i.e. the observed tension in the orbital decay of clusters of stars as seen in the Fornax Dwarf Spheroidal, could be addressed by SFDM models. Indeed, CDM numerical simulations lead to a faster orbital decay, and so a higher dynamical friction, than obtained in observations (e.g., as reviews). For FDM, this tension disappears for low DM masses m<10^ 21 eV , but this range of scalar masses is in potential tension with other observables. In the SFDM, dynamical friction is also expected to be lower than in CDM and this could help in resolving this tension.

sources.summary: The review describes Fornax as a massive dwarf spheroidal with process elements, enhanced Eu/Mg, delayed-source contributions, and some inhomogeneous enrichment in metal-rich stars.

quotes.quote: Sculptor, Fornax, and Sagittarius are sufficiently massive that they should be dominated by chemical evolution rather than enrichment. Sculptor is one of the best-studied dwarf galaxies with a very simple star formation history and smooth process chemical evolution Duggan2018, Hill2019, Skuladottir2019, Molero2021, delosReyes2022 . There is still debate about the relative importance of prompt vs delayed process sources in Sculptor, but it clearly has reached the point of continuous chemical evolution with [ Eu/Mg ] 0 . Fornax Letarte2010, Lemasle2014 and Sagittarius Bonifacio2000,McWilliam2013,Hansen2018 are two of the more massive dwarf spheroidal galaxies with process elements, both with enhanced [ Eu/Mg ] 0.4 . These indicate that Fornax and Sagittarius have a relatively large contribution from delayed sources of process elements compared to Sculptor, which matches the difference in star formation durations seen in the bottom panel of Figure fig:dwarfprop . Note that there is clearly some inhomogeneous process enrichment in Fornax, as some of its most metal-rich stars have extreme Eu enhancements Reichert2021 .

sources.summary: The paper cites Fornax as a dwarf spheroidal whose density, central young population, metallicity gradient, and likely stellar mass loss are consistent with tidal-stirring expectations.

quotes.quote: Sextans, Sagittarius and Fornax) , namely 0.01 < _0 < 0.1 M_ /pc^3 (Mateo 1998) The remnant of GR8 is comparable to the densest dSphs, Draco, Sculptor and Ursa Minor, having _0 > 0.1 M_ /pc^3 (Lake 1990).

Due to the bar driven inflow, young stars in the remnant would be principally located in its central part. This is what is observed in many LG early type dwarfs, with Fornax, Carina, Sextans and NGC147 being well known examples (Grebel 1999). The much stronger bursts in HSB satellites should give rise to a higher metal enrichment compared to LSB satellites because the supernovae rate should be higher. As tidal stirring necessarily implies that the remnants of the robust HSBs are more luminous than the remnants of the fragile LSBs, one would expect a correlation between metallicity and luminosity, similar to that observed in the LG, where bright dEs are more metal-rich than dSphs (Mateo 1998). On the other end, a higher metallicity spread should be present in dSphs as a consequence of their star formation being more extended in time. Radial metallicity gradients, with the more metal rich population sitting in the center of the galaxy, as observed in Fornax (Grebel 1999), are also a natural consequence of the present model.

If, as suggested by the results of the LSB runs, we assume that Carina, Fornax, Leo I, Leo II, Sculptor, Sextans and Sagittarius have typically lost 60 of their original stellar mass, we find out that dwarf spheroidals should have contributed at least 10^9 solar masses to the old stellar halo.

sources.summary: The paper measures Fornax’s proper motion from HST fields, finding a Galactocentric velocity near perigalacticon that may leave it unbound and outside proposed Galactic-halo streams.

quotes.quote: Four independent preliminary measurements of the proper motion of Fornax for three fields agree within their uncertainties. The weighted average of these measurements is _ = 49 13 milliarcseconds century ^ 1 and _ = -59 13 milliarcseconds century ^ 1 . The Galactocentric velocity derived from the proper motion implies that Fornax is near perigalacticon, may not be bound to the Milky Way, and is not a member of any of the proposed streams of galaxies and globular clusters in the Galactic halo. If Fornax is bound, the Milky Way must have a mass of at least (1.6 0.8) 10^ 12 M _ .

sources.summary: The paper describes Fornax as a massive Milky Way-associated dwarf spheroidal with five old globular clusters, adopting an optical centre and comparing its cluster core-radius distribution with other nearby systems.

quotes.quote: The Fornax and Sagittarius dSph galaxies are the two most massive of the 11 dwarf galaxies associated with the Milky Way, and the only two to possess globular clusters (see e.g., Forbes et al. forbes ). Fornax contains five, while Sagittarius has four definite members, plus at least one (Pal 12, see e.g., Mart nez-Delgado et al. pala ) probably previously associated. Unlike the Magellanic globular clusters however, the Fornax and Sagittarius clusters are exclusively old ( 7 15 Gyr).

For the Fornax dSph we adopted the optical centre from Simbad, at = 02^ h 39^ m 59 3 , = -34 26 57 (J2000.0) -- this is consistent with the centroids derived by Demers et al. demersb , and Irwin Hatzidimitriou dwarfpars .

sources.summary: The paper uses the Fornax dwarf elliptical and its globular clusters to argue MOND-like tidal/Roche predictions overestimate cluster sizes, highlighting Fornax's cluster orbits and central distances.

quotes.quote: There are five bright old globular clusters orbiting around the Fornax dwarf elliptical, 140kpc away from the Milky Way centre. These globulars have a typical baryonic mass of 10^ 4-5 enclosed in a limiting radius of 30-50 with a half-mass radius of 4-12 (using data compiled in Mackey Gilmore 2003 and Rodgers Roberts 1994). One of clusters, No. 1, has a distorted profile and appears to be tidally disturbed. The clusters No. 3,4,5 appear to have excess stars near their limiting radii. The clusters No.1,2,5 are on the outskirts of Fornax, between 1-1.5kpc from the Fornax centre. Our model of relaxation-driven expansion predicts (cf. Fig. rhage ) that Fornax No.1 ( 1 10^4 ) should have grown to a half-mass size of about 40pc, and more massive clusters No. 2 and No. 5 ( 5 10^4 ) should grow to 12pc. The predicted sizes are significantly greater than the observed half-light radius of 12pc (No.1) and 6pc (No.2 and No.5). Our model also predicts (cf. eq. roche ) that the Roche lobe of these Fornax clusters should have a radius of (650-1000) if they are on a 140kpc orbit around the Milky Way ( 5 10^ 10 ), or a Roche lobe radius of (100-130) if they are on a 1.5 kpc orbit around Fornax ( 2 10^7 ). Both of these predictions are much greater than the observed limiting radius of (40-50)pc for all Fornax clusters.

sources.summary: The paper uses the Fornax dwarf spheroidal galaxy as an astrophysical application, arguing that a central constant-density core can stall globular clusters and resolve their orbital timing problem.

quotes.quote: The Fornax dwarf spheroidal galaxy in the Local Group has 5 GCs at a range of projected radii. Application of Chandrasekhar dynamical friction suggests the clusters should rapidly fall to the centre of Fornax from their current positions; fine tuning is required to have them arrive at their present positions at the current epoch. In Goerdt:2006rw we show that a small core of radius greater than 0.24 kpc can solve this problem by causing some, or all, of Fornax's GC to stall.
This point was investigated in a companion paper Goerdt:2006rw , where we suggested that a constant density core could solve the `timing problem' for the GCs in the Fornax dwarf galaxy.

sources.summary: The paper discusses Fornax as a dwarf spheroidal whose velocity-dispersion profile can be interpreted through multiple stellar components, with possible alternative merger-driven kinematics.

quotes.quote: This is illustrated in Figure fig:ldf , where we show data for Leo I, Fornax, and Draco, compiled from the literature. In each of these three cases, _p appears to rises by about a factor of 2 from its central value before starting to decline again. In the case of Fornax, we refer the reader to .
Similar testable inferences can be made for Fornax and Draco.
Alternatively, suggest that there is tentative evidence for non-equilibrium kinematics in their data for Fornax, which might imply a merger-driven scenario.

sources.summary: The paper treats Fornax as a special dwarf spheroidal whose surviving globular clusters favor a shallow, cored dark-matter profile over a steep cusp.

quotes.quote: The Fornax dSph galaxy has five surviving globular clusters. The orbit of a compact massive system, such as a globular cluster, should decay due to dynamical friction as it orbits through the background dark matter halo particles. The rate of this orbital decay is faster in a steep (cusped) dark-matter density profile, and is slower in a shallow (core) dark-matter density profile. The (projected) distribution of the surviving clusters has been analysed most recently by , who show that a cored mass distribution is strongly preferred.

sources.summary: The paper highlights Fornax as a luminous dSph companion with a globular-cluster system; WFPC2 data show four of its clusters have nearly identical old ages.

quotes.quote: The two most luminous of the Galaxy's dSph companions, Fornax and Sagittarius, possess their own globular cluster systems, and for both these dSphs there are new results on the ages of their star clusters.

For the Fornax dSph, Buonanno et al. \ (1998) have used WFPC2 images to produce -m diagrams that reach below the main sequence turnoff for four of the five Fornax globular clusters. They find that these four Fornax globular clusters have identical ages to within 1 Gyr. As for Draco, the similarity of these cluster ages contrasts rather strongly with the expected age range of 2 Gyr based on the horizontal branch morphology differences shown by the Fornax cluster -m diagrams and the assumption that age is the ``second parameter''.

sources.summary: The paper uses Fornax dSph as an example whose five ancient metal-poor globular clusters and farthest projected cluster radius imply a core region larger than 1.6 kpc.

quotes.quote: For example, the Fornax dSph galaxy has five GCs which are metal poor and as old as the universe, thus resembling the GCs of Milky Way Buonannoetal98,Buonannoetal,Straideretal,MackeyGilmore,Grecoetal .
For instance, in the Fornax dSph, the furthest GC from the galactic center has a projected distance of 1.6kpc MackeyGilmore . The core region of the Fornax would be larger than 1.6kpc .

sources.summary: The paper treats the Fornax galaxy as a possible source or comparison for young globular clusters near the FLS great circle, linking their ages to its field stars and possible tidal stripping.

quotes.quote: In Fig. 9 we show the location of these 10 GCs, along with the 10 Gyr old field stars in the Fornax galaxy from Coleman de Jong (2008). Seven of the 10 GCs reveal ages younger by 3 Gyr than the general distribution, and consistent with the field stars that formed 10 Gyr ago in the Fornax galaxy. These GCs account for most of the remaining intermediate metallicity objects that deviate from the general distribution. One possibility is that some of these GCs were tidally stripped from the Fornax galaxy as it crossed the orbit of the LMC (Dinescu et al. 2004).

sources.summary: The paper discusses Fornax RGB metallicities, noting CaT/Ca abundance offsets, distance-limited bright-star targeting, and a larger total sample but lower observed fraction than Sextans.

quotes.quote: However, in Fornax we generally find that the important electron donors Fe and Mg are increased relatively to the values in our grid at similar [ Ca/H ] , while we still measure the CaT lines to be too strong relative to the [ Ca/H ] measurements directly from Ca I lines.

Most of the discrepant stars are from the Fornax dwarf galaxy, where we were just able to target its brightest population of RGB stars due to the relatively large distance to this dwarf galaxy (see also Section lowmettails ).
For instance, a much larger percentage of the Sextans stars have been observed than Fornax stars, although the total sample in Fornax is larger (it is a larger galaxy).

sources.summary: The paper compares its model to Fornax, finding predicted metallicities below one central-star data point but closer to wider low-resolution measurements across the dwarf galaxy.

quotes.quote: At the brightest end (i.e., Fornax) our model predicts average metallicities that are below the data point of norris10 . However, this measurement is likely to be an overestimate of the actual value because the sample used preferentially contains stars close to the centre of the galaxy (which have higher resolution spectra), and here the stars are more metal-rich letarte10 . Our data are in better agreement with the average Z values inferred from lower resolution spectra that cover a larger extent of this dwarf galaxy battaglia06b,kirby10 .

sources.summary: The paper notes indirect hints of a cored distribution in Fornax, finds its J reconstruction robust to binning, and classifies it as well constrained but less promising.

quotes.quote: There are two dSphs|Fornax and Ursa Minor|that show indirect hints of a cored distribution 2003ApJ...588L..21K,2006MNRAS.368.1073G ; however, in both cases the presence of a core is inferred based on a timing argument that assumes we are not catching the dSph at a special moment.
For Fornax and Sculptor, the profiles are insensitive to the binning chosen, so that the reconstruction of the J values median and 68 CLs (right panel) is robust.

With these criteria in mind, we find three categories: well-constrained and promising (Ursa Minor, Sculptor and Draco), well-constrained but less promising (Carina, Fornax and Leo I), and poorly constrained (Sextans and Leo II).

sources.summary: The paper tests whether Fornax could have entered the Milky Way along a filament and concludes no tenable orbit or parameter set reconciles it with that scenario.

quotes.quote: Here we test this scenario by making use of the proper motions of the Fornax, Sculptor, Ursa Minor and Carina dwarf spheroidals, and trace their orbits back through several variations of the Milky Way's potential and account for dynamical friction.
Using a simple model we find no tenable set of parameters that can allow Fornax to be consistent with filamentary infall, mainly because the 1 error on its proper motion is relatively small.
It would appear, therefore, that there is no scenario which can reconcile Fornax with the filamentary infall, regardless of the proper motion, assuming it is within 3 of the mean measurement.

sources.summary: The paper models Fornax as one of eight classical dwarf spheroidals and reports that its isotropic Jeans solutions prefer a mild cusp, though with large uncertainty.

quotes.quote: We use a spherical Jeans analyses to constrain the density profile slopes of the eight classical MW dSphs: Carina, Draco, Fornax, Leo I, Leo II, Sculptor, Sextans, and Ursa Minor.

Carina (black dotted) demonstrates a strong preference for living in a cored dark matter halo ( 0 ), while Fornax (red dashed) and Sextans (orange dot-dashed) prefer moderate cusps ( 0.7 ), but their distributions are too wide to draw any strong conclusions.
Sculptor strongly favors a cuspy inner profile ( 1 ), while Fornax and Sextans prefer mild cusps ( 0.7 ), albeit with large uncertainties.

sources.summary: The paper uses Fornax dSph as an observed mass-profile case, modeling its halo and arguing baryonic mass loss can preserve enough matter for its stellar mass.

quotes.quote: We apply this approach to fit the observed mass profile of the Fornax dSph.

They also obtained an observed luminosity weighted line-of-sight velocity dispersion =11.3^ +1.0 _ 1.8 We set the initial mass of Fornax dSph's entire halo to be 1.5 10^9 and choose a length scale which matches the observationally inferred mass distribution (Figure fig:normalize left panel).

Despite the loss of more than 50 of the initial baryonic mass (which corresponds to a loss of 10 of the initial total mass), Fornax would still be able to have retained a sufficient amount of baryonic matter to account for its observed stellar mass Mateo:1998xi,Jardel:2012fk and some loss of baryonic matter during the subsequent stellar and dynamical evolution.

sources.summary: The paper finds Fornax dSph has a binary fraction consistent with Milky Way field binaries and compatible with Milky-Way-like binary population parameters.

quotes.quote: We find that the Fornax, Sculptor, and Sextans dSphs are consistent with having binary populations similar to that of Milky Way field binaries to within 68 confidence limits, whereas the Carina dSph is remarkably deficient in binaries with periods less than 10 years.
By this standard, Fornax, Sculptor, and Sextans all have binary fractions consistent with Milky Way field binaries to within 68 confidence limits.

This set of parameters lies squarely within the allowed parameter region for the Fornax, Sculptor, and Sextans dSphs, indicating that a Milky-Way like binary population is compatible with the data in those galaxies.

sources.summary: Fornax is modeled as a Milky Way dSph with confidence maps for its dark halo, and the paper finds its lower-M/L halo is less flattened than high-M/L dSphs.

quotes.quote: In this section, we describe the best-fit dark halo model for each of seven MW dSphs (Carina, Fornax, Sculptor, Sextans, Draco, Leo I, and Leo II) and five M31 dSphs (And I, And II, And III, And V, and And VII) and present confidence maps between each parameter.

In Figure  fig:fig2 and  fig:fig3 , we present 68  ( 1 ), 95  , and 99  confidence levels of contours in the two-dimensional plane of Q- _z , _0- _z , _z , b_ halo _z , and i- _z for all sample dSphs: Carina, Fornax, Sculptor, Sextans, Draco, and Leo I are shown in Figure  fig:fig2 , and Leo II, And I, And II, And III, And V, and And VII are shown in Figure  fig:fig3 .

In light of these predictions, it is interesting to remark that the dark halos of Fornax, Sculptor, Leo I, and Leo II with relatively low M/L s tend to be less flattened than Carina, Sextans, and Draco with high M/L s.

sources.summary: The paper uses Fornax kinematic data in its axisymmetric modeling and reports that Fornax has among the smallest J-factor uncertainties because of abundant kinematic data.

quotes.quote: For the classicals, we utilize stellar-kinematic data published by Waletal2015 for Draco, by Matetal2008 for Leo I, by Kocetal2007 for Leo II, and by Waletal2009a,Waletal2009b for Carina, Fornax, Sculptor, and Sextans.
For instance, the member stars for Carina, Fornax, Sculptor and Sextans are identified by the expectation-maximization algorithm described in Waletal2009b .

It is found from Table table3 and this figure that among the classical dSphs, Draco has the largest J factor value, while Fornax and Sculptor have the smallest uncertainties of J owing to an abundance of kinematic data.

sources.summary: The paper describes Fornax as a well-studied dwarf spheroidal with 147 neutron-capture abundance measurements and ten stars qualifying as r-process I or II objects.

quotes.quote: Fornax The dSph Fornax is well studied, with neutron-capture element abundances published for 147 stars shetrone2003,tafelmeyer2010,letarte2010,lemasle2014 . The majority of these are enhanced not only in Eu but also in s process elements such as Ba and La, such that only six qualify as I stars; three from the sample of lemasle2014 and three from the sample of letarte2010 . Furthermore one star from lemasle2014 and three stars from letarte2010 qualify as II stars, resulting in a total of 10 I and II stars discovered in this galaxy.

sources.summary: The paper uses the Fornax dwarf spheroidal as the best-studied low-mass Local Group globular-cluster system, noting its five clusters and possible cored dark matter distribution.

quotes.quote: The best-studied GC system of a low-mass galaxy in the Local Group is that of the Fornax dwarf spheroidal ( M_V = -13.4; McConnachie 2012), which has five clusters with absolute magnitudes ranging from M_V = --5.1 to --8.1 (Strader et al. 2003). One of these GCs, with M_V = -7.3, is more metal-rich than the others and is located near the center of the galaxy, and hence is sometimes cited as a candidate nuclear star cluster, though its radial velocity appears offset from the field stars in the galaxy center (Hendricks et al. 2016). The dynamical friction timescale for the Fornax GCs is less than a Hubble time, so the survival of its GC system is a puzzle; one proposed solution is that the dark matter distribution in the Fornax dSph is cored rather than cusped, leading to a longer inspiral time (Goerdt et al. 2006).

sources.summary: The paper places Fornax among classical dwarf spheroidals with VMP alpha enhancement and abundance trends resembling other massive classical dwarfs.

quotes.quote: We show that once the NLTE effects are properly taken into account, all massive galaxies in our sample, that is, the MW halo and the classical dSphs Sculptor, Ursa Minor, Sextans, and Fornax, reveal a similar plateau at [ /Fe ] 0.3 for each of the process elements: Mg, Ca, and Ti.

Previous LTE abundance analyses conducted at similar high spectral resolution and signal-to-noise ratios, by 2010A&A...524A..58T,Starkenburg2013,2015A&A...583A..67J , and 2015ApJ...802...93S , deduced a common conclusion that the Sculptor, Sextans, and Fornax dSphs are enhanced in the VMP regime, at similar level to the MW halo.

In the [ /Fe ] versus metallicity diagrams, all classical dSphs: Sculptor, Ursa Minor, Sextans, and Fornax scatter around the mean of the Milky Way halo stars, [ /Fe ] 0.3, suggesting enrichment of these galaxies by the massive stars, in numbers following classical IMF.

sources.summary: The paper uses Fornax as a classical dwarf spheroidal with an extended star formation history, five old orbiting star clusters, and a flat velocity-dispersion profile.

quotes.quote: They show that the SFH of classical dSphs range from intense star bursts, where almost all stars of the galaxies were formed at the same time, as Sculptor for example, until galaxies with a constant star formation rate like Fornax or Leo I.

The presence of surviving SCs in our simulations, could give us predictions for future observations to corroborate our formation scenario, for example in Fornax there are five old SCs discovered orbiting this galaxy and in several dwarf galaxies we see young star clusters e.g. grasha2017 .
In Sextans we see a slight drop in velocity dispersion around 1 kpc, while Sculptor, Draco and Fornax show flat profiles their figure 2 Walker2007 .

sources.summary: The paper uses the Fornax galaxy as the dwarf-spheroidal benchmark for wave-dark-matter soliton scaling, citing its phase-space distribution, dark-matter domination, and persistent outer globular clusters.

quotes.quote: A boson mass of 10^ 22 eV has been derived in this context, corresponding to a solitonic core of 1 kpc of mass 10^8 M_ , by matching the phase space distribution of stars within the well studied Fornax galaxy , that is representative of the most common class of dwarf spheroidal galaxies, where dark matter dominates over stellar mass. Furthermore, the relatively weak dynamical friction of wave-DM, compared to CDM, can help to account for the otherwise puzzling presence today of ancient globular clusters at large radius in this Fornax galaxy .

The scale of this core for the Milky Way has been predicted by Schive et al. (2014) , to be 100 pc, with a mass of 10^9M_ , based firmly on the derived soliton scale for the large core of the Fornax dwarf spheroidal galaxy, which provided the associated boson mass for this model.

sources.summary: The paper describes Fornax as a bright Milky Way dwarf spheroidal with relatively low inferred dark-matter density and a lower-density subhalo orbit that has not approached the Milky Way disk closely.

quotes.quote: The dSphs that have not come close to the Milky Way center (like Fornax, Carina and Sextans) are less dense in DM than those that have come closer (like Draco and Ursa Minor).
From figure fig:corr_rho_pericenter , we see that Fornax, Carina and Sextans have lower _ 150 values in cISO fits than NFW ones, because their half-light radii are considerably larger than 150 pc.

To explain TBTF for the bright MW dSphs in light of the Gaia data using collisionless DM models, one must host Draco and Ursa Minor in the densest subhalos with pericenter distances close to 30 - 40 kpc, while explaining why Carina, Fornax and Sextans are hosted by lower-density subhalos that did not come close to the MW disk.

sources.summary: The paper treats Fornax as a massive Milky Way satellite with a long inferred quenching timescale, unusually circular orbit, large pericenter, and reduced environmental-quenching efficiency.

quotes.quote: As evident in Figure fig:tqmstar-punchline , however, there are three dwarfs (Sagittarius, Fornax, and Carina) with inferred quenching timescales significantly longer than the short quenching timescales inferred from previous work.

The other two satellites (Fornax and Carina), for which we measure _ quench > 4 Gyr , suffer far less from the issues associated with modeling Sag I, such that the long quenching timescales inferred for these systems are much more reliable.

Taken together, the orbital eccentricity and pericentric distance for Carina and Fornax are consistent with orbits about the MW that would lead to less efficient quenching via environmental processes (e.g. ram-pressure stripping), which operate with greater efficiency deeper in the host potential.

sources.summary: The paper uses Fornax as a dwarf-galaxy abundance-pattern comparison, noting similar barium behavior to TriAnd but differences in europium and other elements such as sodium.

quotes.quote: It is interesting to note that those stars in our sample that are not from TriAnd (open red circles) show a very different behavior when compared to TriAnd, including the star 10 that shows again an abundance pattern in line with those of dwarf spheroidals, in this case Fornax, having a high value of [ Eu/Fe ] . To summarize, the [ Eu/Fe ] ratios are overall lower than results from dwarf spheroidals, showing a distinct behavior when compared to the s-process element barium that follows the pattern observed for Fornax.

The [ Ba/Fe ] ratio of the TriAnd stars also resembles the pattern presented by the Fornax dwarf galaxy, however, we observe significant differences between these populations for other elements (like Na).

sources.summary: The paper finds Fornax has comparable internal and external fields, disturbed phantom dark matter with spikes, and line-of-sight profiles with two density peaks for low mass-to-light ratio.

quotes.quote: For Sculptor and Fornax, where the internal and external gravitational fields are comparable, the phantom dark matter distribution appears disturbed with spikes at the locations where the two fields cancel each other; these features have little connection with the distribution of the stars within the dwarfs.
For the two remaining dwarf galaxies, Sculptor and Fornax, the internal and external fields are comparable.

Along the line of sight between the Milky Way centre and the dwarf, Sculptor and Fornax (and to a lesser extent Leo II) exhibit an additional effect, namely the presence of two peaks in the phantom dark matter density profile for mass-to-light ratio M/L=3 M_ /L_ for Sculptor and M/L=1 M_ /L_ for Fornax and Leo II.

sources.summary: The paper describes Fornax as a dSph whose stellar populations and dark-matter core profile match a soliton model, with younger metal-rich populations attributed to later gas accretion or mergers.

quotes.quote: The predicted soliton profile has been shown to match in detail the cored mass profile of the Fornax dwarf for which the dynamical data extends beyond the core radius allowing a determination of the boson mass of m_ 10^ 22 eV (Schive et al. 2014).

This behaviour is inferred most clearly for Fornax, the largest and brightest galaxy classed as dSph, where the presence of a DM core is implied from multiple stellar populations (Amorisco Evans 2012, Brownsberger et al. 2019, del Pino et al. 2015 Schive et al. 2014) the profile of which agrees in detail with the soliton profile of DM with an estimated radius of 0.7 kpc. This scale matches the oldest stellar population with a flat velocity profile indicating the old population of stars formed together with the bulk of the DM initially, with subsequent episodes of gas accretion (Rusakov et al. 2020) and/or merging with other dwarfs (Leung et al. 2020) responsible for the younger metal rich stellar populations in Fornax, with similar behaviour claimed for Sculptor and Ursa Minor (Amorisco Evans 2012, del Pino et al. 2015) where the younger populations and much concentrated and of lower velocity dispersion, implying metal enriched gas from which these stars cooled deep within their dark cores.

sources.summary: The paper uses Fornax as a dwarf-spheroidal benchmark for the smallest observed dark-matter halo, adopting a typical 1 kpc radius and 10^8 solar-mass scale.

quotes.quote: These ultracompact DM halos correspond typically to dwarf spheroidal galaxies (dSphs) like Fornax. To be specific, we shall assume that Fornax is the smallest halo observed in the Universe.
We have identified this minimum (ultracompact) halo with dSphs like Fornax with a typical radius R_ min =1 kpc and a typical mass M_ min =10^8 M_ .

sources.summary: The paper uses the Fornax dwarf spheroidal as an observed motivation for core-stalling dynamics, emphasizing its globular clusters and their survival away from the galactic center.

quotes.quote: The Fornax dwarf spheroidal, which has motivated the core stalling problem for decades, has six GCs orbiting its core Buonanno1998, Strader2003,Wang2019,Pace2021 .

Before proceeding, we note that while this discrepancy is quite intriguing theoretically, LBK approach, due to extremely slow inspiral inside r_ , can still explain in principle the presence of globulars well away from the galactic center in Fornax dSph.
This suggests the possibility that many such perturbers will pile up, which is arguably realized by the large population of globular clusters in Fornax.

sources.summary: The paper highlights Fornax as an exception among low-mass satellites, with a positive abundance-slope change suggesting a recent burst of star formation.

quotes.quote: Based on a compilation of chemical compositions from various works CohenHuang2009,CohenHuang2010,Starkenburg2013 , quote [ Fe/H ] _ knee 1.9 for Sculptor, Ursa Minor, and Fornax and [ Fe/H ] _ knee 2.5 for Draco and Carina see also deboer2014 .

As for the others, while our data do not rule out the presence of a change of slope in the Mg-Fe relation for Sculptor, Ursa Minor, and Fornax at [ Fe/H ] 2 , they cannot confirm it either, due to increased uncertainties and relatively small sample sizes in the low metallicity end.

The one exception is Fornax, for which a sharp positive change of slope can be seen at [ Fe/H ] -1.2, similar to the case of more massive satellites, suggesting also in the case of Fornax the occurrence of a burst of star formation in the recent past.

sources.summary: The paper discusses Fornax as a classical dwarf in past Magellanic-group proposals, with roughly even probability of Magellanic association.

quotes.quote: In particular, four of the classical dwarfs -- Carina, Draco, Fornax and Ursa Minor -- each have a 50 probability of once belonging to the Magellanic system, thus providing a possible explanation for the ``plane of satellites'' conundrum.

LyndenBell1982a added Carina dSph to the list of galaxies possibly associated with the Great Magellanic Galaxy, while LyndenBell1982b introduced another dSph grouping -- Fornax, Leo I, Leo II and Sculptor (FLS), but treated them separately from the Magellanic group, and Majewski1994 added Sextans and Phoenix dSph to the FLS group.

In particular, four of the classical dSph -- Carina, Draco, Fornax and Ursa Minor -- have a roughly equal chance to either be accreted with the Magellanic system or originate from elsewhere (in case of Carina, the chances are even higher, 2:1 ).

sources.summary: The paper notes limited evidence for Fornax being dynamically associated with the LMC and finds that some recovered Carina/Fornax orbits become unbound under uncertainty sampling.

quotes.quote: They found that, as far as the VPOS satellites are concerned, especially the bright ones, Sculptor would be on its first infall, while Carina and Fornax would have a fraction ( 25 and 4 respectively) of their Monte Carlo orbit realisation linked to the LMC, the former possibly being a past satellite.

For instance, only Carina and Fornax have been suggested to be dynamically associated with the LMC e.g. Pardy2020 , although this association does not seem to be conclusive e.g. Patel2020,Battaglia2022 .
We see that in most cases the errors mainly affect the orbital period rather than the amplitude, although for Carina and Fornax a fraction of the recovered orbits are unbound.

sources.summary: The paper includes Fornax as a dispersion-dominated dwarf spheroidal and reports visually better fractional-gravity fits favoring large s but without decisive statistics.

quotes.quote: For dispersion-dominated systems, we consider 7 Milky-Way dwSph for which high quality determination of the spatially-resolved dispersion velocity has been obtained via stellar tracers ( ; see also data collection by and references therein) and for which tidal effects are not appreciably influencing the inner kinematics: Carina, Leo I, Leo II, Sculptor, Draco, Sextans, and Fornax.

Finally, in the cases of Fornax and DragonFly 44 there is a clear preference for large values of s 1.5 , but the improvement in the FG fits with respect to the Newtonian case, albeit clear at a visual inspection, is not statistically significant enough to make definite conclusions. As it can be seen in the top panel of Figure fig|Dragonfly44 for Sculptor and DragonFly 44, the most relevant degeneracy between the fitting parameters involves s and the _ sym , in such a way that FG models with larger s tend to be more isotropic.

sources.summary: The paper treats the Fornax dSph as its main case study for globular-cluster orbital decay, emphasizing its measured cluster population, high GC frequency, absent nucleus, and short dynamical-friction timescale estimates.

quotes.quote: Using Fornax as a case study, we examine the orbital evolution of GCs in both the conventional dark matter scenario and under MOdified Newtonian Dynamics (MOND; Milgrom 1983). Although our work is aimed at exploring possible explanations for the survival of GCs in dSph and dE, our analysis will be focused mainly on the Fornax dSph because the recent detailed determinations of the structural and dynamical parameters of both Fornax and its GC population (i.e. position, age, mass and radial velocity of the GCs are known), allow us to test our assumptions. Fornax is the prototypical dSph with a high GC frequency, has no nucleus, and short DF timescale estimates.

sources.summary: The paper uses Fornax dSph as a dwarf-spheroidal constraint: its globular-cluster survival, dispersion profile, and MOND modeling inform dark-matter mass-profile interpretations.

quotes.quote: Similar results are provided by the survival of the globular cluster system in the Fornax dSph .

According to the Stoehr etal analysis, the observed dispersion profile of Draco and Fornax can be obtained by embedding the stellar distributions in CDM haloes of masses 10^9 M _ , extending to several kpc.
Available data for the Fornax dSph can in fact be modelled quite well by MOND, assuming the MOND scale parameter to be a_0 = 2.1 10^ 8 cm s ^ 2 .

gmw_00000482.name: 3C 239
gmw_00000482.overview: 3C 239 is a high-excitation Fanaroff-Riley class II radio galaxy located at a redshift of 1.781 [arXiv:2007.02945]. It is a Compton-thick source whose X-ray emission is dominated by a reflected component [arXiv:0710.5220], and its host galaxy exhibits faint tidal tails [arXiv:2007.02945].

sources.summary: The paper describes 3C 239 as a high-redshift FR II HERG in a crowded field, with tidal features and detected X-ray nucleus and eastern lobe.

quotes.quote: 3C 239 . This is a FR II radio galaxy at z=1.781 ( Laing1983 ) optically identified as a high-excitation radio galaxy (HERG; Jackson1997 ). According to Hammer1990 , this galaxy lies in a crowded field where there are two different populations of galaxies, one at the same redshift as 3C 239 and one in the foreground. They suggested that the first population belongs to a galaxy cluster surrounding 3C 239 which could be gravitationally lensed by the foreground population. The last statement was supported by Best1997 since they claim that a foreground galaxy within 5 \ of the host of 3C 239, next to the eastern lobe, could cause the presence of a double hotspot and arc-like structures in that lobe. The HST images ( Best1997 ) show a bright galaxy with two emission regions aligned with the radio axis and a string of emission to the south-east misaligned to the radio axis by 45 , as well as two faint tidal tails to the north and west of the galaxy, extending 40 kpc.

sources.summary: The paper identifies 3C239 as a clear Compton-thick 3CR source whose X-rays are dominated by reflected emission, with a large Fe line equivalent width and ionized line energy.

quotes.quote: A clearcut Compton thick example is 3C239: here only the reflected component is visible, with a spectral index similar to the unabsorbed sources and a very low upper limit on the intrinsic N _ H . The true origin of this component (reflection instead of underluminous unabsorbed emission) is shown by the large equivalent width of the Fe line. The line energy is not compatible with neutral iron [ 6.67 (+0.17-0.13) keV ] , suggesting that at least in this case the visible component is indeed reflected from a hot medium which preserves the intrinsic spectral index; the width of the line is very weakly constrained ( < 0.5 keV).

gmw_00000483.name: 3C 249
gmw_00000483.overview: 3C 249 is a lobe-dominated quasar located at a redshift of z=1.554 [arXiv:2007.02945]. X-ray observations have detected its nucleus, both of its radio lobes, and diffuse soft X-ray emission extending beyond the lobes [arXiv:2007.02945].

sources.summary: The paper reports 3C 249 as a lobe-dominated quasar with a hard-X-ray nuclear position, detected X-ray nucleus, both lobes, and diffuse soft X-ray emission.

quotes.quote: 3C 249 . This source is a lobe-dominated quasar at z=1.554 ( Law1995 ). It has not been studied in detail in the literature and, as for the previous source, we could not find a radio map in which the radio core was detected. However, we obtained the position of the nucleus using hard X-rays (see right panel of Fig. fig:3c249 ), in which the nucleus was detected with a 2 confidence level. We were able to detect the X-ray nucleus and both lobes in the 0.5 - 7 keV band. Additionally, diffuse X-ray emission was found beyond the position of the radio lobes in the 0.5 - 3 keV band (with a 2 confidence level), as shown in the left panel of Fig. fig:3c249 .

gmw_00000484.name: 3C 257
gmw_00000484.overview: The high-redshift radio galaxy 3C 257 [arXiv:1610.02057, arXiv:astro-ph/0606323] is the most distant object in the 3CR catalog [arXiv:1605.03196, arXiv:2007.02945]. It exhibits a distorted host morphology and is likely undergoing an active major merger [arXiv:1605.03161, arXiv:1605.03196] with ongoing star formation [arXiv:1605.03196, arXiv:2007.02945].

sources.summary: 3C 257 has extended and offset line emission, broad integrated lines, an irregular velocity field, and gas kinematics associated with a small embedded radio source.

quotes.quote: 3C 257 3C 257 at z = 2.48 has extended line emission along an axis going from the north-west to the south-east, extending over 3.4 2.0 \ (27.5 kpc 15.1 kpc after deconvolving with the size of the seeing disk of 0.8 0.7 ). The continuum emission is extended with a peak towards south and an extended fainter area towards north-west, roughly aligned with the emission-line gas (contours in Fig. fig:maps ). The line emission is much brighter in the south-east, and is clearly spatially offset from the continuum. The integrated spectrum shows [ OIII ] 4959,5007, H , and H , [ NII ] 6583, and [ SII ] 6716,6731, where the two lines of the [ SII ] doublet are blended. A single Gaussian component per line with FWHM = 1049 7 km s ^ 1 is sufficient to fit the line profiles (the error is that of [ OIII ] 5007).

The velocity field of 3C 257 is irregular (Fig. fig:maps ), with a velocity maximum south-west from the center, and declining velocities towards either end of the emission-line region. In 3C 257, we find a FWHM gradient that runs nearly perpendicular to the major axis of the gas, with FWHMs increasing from 500 km s ^ 1 to 1300 km s ^ 1 from the north-east to the south-west. Prime examples for this phase might in particular be 3C 257 and MRC 0251 273, where the jets are not only embedded within the bright emission-line region, but also misaligned with the major axis of the gas.

sources.summary: The paper treats 3C257 as the highest-redshift target, with blue early-type galaxies on the model red sequence and disturbed, dusty close companions including the radio host.

quotes.quote: Another object that shows an interesting population of blue ETGs is 3C257 (see Fig. CMD_RG ), our highest redshift target ( z = 2.474 ). Interestingly, these blue ETGs lie exactly on top of the RS models. This will be further discussed in Sect. BlueETG .

Visual inspection reveals that some of these galaxies, including the host galaxy of 3C257, are close companions of target (Fig. blueETG ). These objects display disturbed morphologies that is most likely indicating an active merger phase. We argue that these galaxies are reddened by the presence of a significant amount of dust.

sources.summary: The survey describes 3C 257 as a previously known z=2.474 radio galaxy observed as a selection check; its K magnitude underestimates the K-z redshift, likely because H falls in K band.

quotes.quote: Only two objects, 4C 00.62 Rot:97 and 3C 257, Hew:91,VanB:98 were previously identified with confirmed redshifts of z=2.53 and z=2.474 respectively. The high redshift of these two radio galaxies support the effectiveness of our selection criteria.

J1123+0530 is the well known z = 2.474 radio galaxy 3C 257 Hew:91,VanB:98 , one of the most luminous radio galaxies known. As it passed our selection criteria, it was observed in order to provide a consistency check with previous radio galaxy searches.

For example, the previously observed radio galaxy 3C 257 (J1123+0530) is known to be at z=2.474 , while its K=17.51 magnitude corresponds to z 1.2 on a linear fit to the K-z diagram, indicative of the large intrinsic scatter in the K-z relation. VanB:98 point out that H falls into the K band for 3C 257 , which may explain its relative brightness in K .

sources.summary: 3C 257 is presented as the highest-redshift 3CR object, with a distorted host morphology, probable major merger, and ongoing star formation.

quotes.quote: 3C 257 (z=2.474) 3C 257 is the most distant object in our sample and it is also the highest redshift object in the 3CR catalog. Its host galaxy appears in our IR image as a bright core surrounded by a tear-drop shaped region of emission which extends primarily south and east. Its morphology is clearly distorted, most likely as a result of an ongoing major merger. There is a small amount of signal in the optical image associated with the compact brighter area, which may indicate that star formation is ongoing. This supports the relatively high star formation rate of 920^ +60 _ 50 M_ per year calculated using SED fitting of Herschel data for 3c 257. podigachowski The galaxy is surrounded by about 10 other sources within a radius of 9 in the IR image.

sources.summary: The paper identifies 3C 257 as the highest-redshift 3C radio galaxy, with distorted infrared morphology, possible merger activity, optical star formation, and Chandra detections.

quotes.quote: 3C 257 . This is the highest redshift ( z=2.474 ) radio galaxy in the 3C catalog. According to Hilbert2016 , the IR image shows a distorted morphology ( 2 , which is equivalent to 16.5 kpc at the source redshift), which implies that the source could be undergoing a merging event. Additionally, it shows some optical emission which implies some ongoing star formation. We defined the position of the X-ray nucleus using hard X-rays as in previous sources (in this band, the nucleus was detected with a 3 confidence level). In the Chandra full band image, we detected the nucleus and the western lobe (see Fig. fig:3c257L ).

gmw_00000485.name: 3C 280.1
gmw_00000485.overview: 3C 280.1 is a lobe-dominated quasar located at a redshift of z=1.667 [arXiv:2007.02945]. It features a strong radio jet, a detected X-ray nucleus, and a bright eastern radio lobe [arXiv:2007.02945].

sources.summary: The paper describes 3C 280.1 as a lobe-dominated quasar with a strong radio jet, detected X-ray nucleus and lobes, and a bright eastern lobe.

quotes.quote: 3C 280.1 . This source is a lobe-dominated quasar at z=1.667 ( Laing1983 and Veron2006 ). The 5 GHz radio map in Lonsdale1993 shows a strong jet. In this case, we were able to define the core position using the radio emission (in this position, the X-ray nucleus was detected in the 5 - 7 keV band above a 5 confidence level). We detected the nucleus and both lobes in the 0.5 - 7 keV band (see Fig. fig:3c280.1L ). Furthermore, the eastern lobe of 3C 280.1, which corresponds to the strong jet detected by Lonsdale1993 at radio frequencies, is the brightest lobe in our sample.

gmw_00000486.name: 3C 322
gmw_00000486.overview: The Fanaroff-Riley II radio galaxy 3C 322 exhibits an extended infrared morphology with a core elongated in the northwest-southeast direction [arXiv:1605.03196, arXiv:2007.02945]. It is situated in a galaxy overdensity [arXiv:1605.03161, arXiv:2007.02945], where X-ray observations have detected both its nucleus and northern radio lobe [arXiv:2007.02945].

sources.summary: The paper reports poor X-ray statistics for 3C 322, possible X-ray/radio-lobe correspondence, no diffuse field emission, and IC lobe emission consistent with the measured X-ray flux.

quotes.quote: X-ray morphology The short exposure after background screening and the small number of counts for 3C 322 makes it difficult to localise clearly its X-ray emission (Fig. fig:3c322images , Left). The adaptively smoothed image suggests that its X-ray emission is extended but the radio emission extends further than the X-rays to the north, based on the location of the northern radio hotspot (see Fig. fig:3c322images ) as traced by the contours at 1.4 GHz. A similar behaviour is seen to the south, but to a lesser extent. While this is the highest-redshift source, and the X-ray statistics are not good enough for a detailed comparison between the radio and X-ray emission, the double nature of the X-ray emission suggests a correspondence with the radio lobes (the hotspots are towards the extreme spatial limit of the radio lobes).

The final image does not show any extended diffuse emission in the field of view of the 3C 322 radio galaxy. Given the low photon statistics ( 50 net counts in the 0.3-7.0 keV band from the whole EPIC), and the elliptical shape of the X-ray emission aligned with the radio source, we did not perform a radial profile analysis for 3C 322. However, under the hypothesis that all the photons corresponds to the emission from a cluster-like environment it is possible to set an upper limit on the expected luminosity.
