Sculptor is located at a distance of 80 kpc and has a measured King limiting radius for its stellar distribution of 1.6 kpc . Given these parameters it is one of the more spatially extended dSphs. The mass content of Sculptor has been estimated in several recent papers , and it has been shown that Sculptor may contain some degree of rotational support .

The result is that, given the rotational parameterization and the using entire distribution of 1352 Sculptor stars, there is no statistically significant detection of rotation. From figure fig:A the 90 . .l. upper limit on the rotation is found to be 2 km s ^ 1 .

sources.summary: The paper models Sculptor as a well-observed dSph satellite, fitting its photometric and kinematic data with an Aquarius CDM subhalo and adopting a=0.5 for its stellar-density cusp.

quotes.quote: We restrict our attention to the five satellites with abundant, high-quality stellar kinematic data: Fornax, Sculptor, Leo I, Carina, and Sextans Mateo:2007xh,Walker:2008fc . Assuming isotropic velocity dispersions, we identify an Aquarius subhalo with a dark matter potential which results in a good simultaneous fit to each satellite's photometric and kinematic data. In addition to focusing specifically on CDM and making direct use of realistic potentials from the Aquarius simulations, our modeling differs from previous work in that we simultaneously fit both photometry and kinematics.

For Sculptor, Carina, and Sextans the data constrain the central slope of the stellar density profile less strongly, so for these satellites we choose a = 0.5 . The p-values for these best-fitting subhalos are 0.6, 0.5, 0.6, 0.2 and 0.8 for Fornax, Leo I, Carina, Sculptor, and Sextans, respectively. Thus in all cases the fit appears good from a statistical point of view.

sources.summary: The paper says Sculptor is well fit by the chemical-evolution model, with broad metallicity distribution, slow enrichment, multiple stellar populations, and an old but extended star-formation history.

quotes.quote: Our chemical evolution model for Sculptor produces one of the best fits to the abundance distributions (Fig. fig:scl ) out of all of the dSphs, particularly for the asymmetrical MDF. In kir10a , we could not reproduce the width of Sculptor's MDF with an analytical model of chemical evolution. Our more sophisticated model, which more properly treats Fe as a secondary nucleosynthetic product with multiple origins (Types II and Ia SNe), yields a broad, well-matched MDF for the appropriate choice of parameters.

The resulting MDF has both metal-poor and metal-rich stars and is less-peaked than for the more luminous dSphs. In fact, maj99 found that Sculptor undoubtedly contains multiple stellar populations based on its HB and red giant branch (RGB) morphologies. Our chemical evolution model conforms to the photometric description of Sculptor's SFH.

sources.summary: Sculptor dSph is contrasted with Fornax and the LMC: it lacks the same barium enhancement, did not reach high metallicity, and stopped forming stars about 6 Gyr ago.

quotes.quote: Fig. bar shows the detailed abundances of Barium compared to Iron, [ Ba/Fe ] , based on high resolution spectroscopic observations of individual RGB stars in the Sculptor dSph, the Fornax dSph and the Large Magellanic Cloud, compared to RGB stars in the Galactic disk and halo. Barium is of particular interest because at these [ Fe/H ] values it is produced almost entirely by the s-process. This also makes it a good indicator of how many potential s-process sources there have been and when they were most productive. Fig. bar shows that both the LMC and Fornax have significantly enhanced [ Ba/Fe ] compared the Galaxy at [ Fe/H ] > -1 . It seems that this enhancement only starts at [ Fe/H ] 1 . Sculptor thus does not show the same effect, presumably because it never reached a high enough metallicity before all star formation stopped. It might also be because Sculptor stopped forming stars before the feedback of s-process elements from AGB stars became important to the chemical enrichment.

It can be seen that the galaxy that never forms stars with [ Fe/H ] > -1 (e.g., Sculptor, see Fig. zfh ) also appears to contain no AGB (C-stars) and no sign of enrichment by these stars during its star formation history (e.g., Fig. bar ). Of course Sculptor also stopped forming stars around 6 Gyrs ago, and for several Gyr before this it formed stars at a very low rate (see de Boer et al., in prep), thus it might be a case of low number statistics.

sources.summary: The paper includes Sculptor in a Fermi Pass 7 joint WIMP search, using J values from stellar velocity dispersions, a 0.5 degree ROI, empirical background PMFs, and dark-matter distribution uncertainties.

quotes.quote: We use Bootes I, Draco, Fornax, Sculptor, Sextans, Ursa Minor, and Segue 1 because none are in a crowded field or near known ray sources. We utilize the updated values of J presented in 2011arXiv1108.3546T . The J values are derived based on modeling the velocity dispersion profiles of stars in each dwarf 2007PhRvD..75h3526S,2008ApJ...678..614S,2009JCAP...06..014M . We define a Region of Interest (ROI) to be a region of the sky with a radius of 0.5 containing all Pass 7 photons of evclass=2 available publicly on the Fermi Science Support Center (FSSC) , in the Mission Elapsed Time interval of [ 239557417-334619159 ] seconds (August 4, 2008 15:43:36 UTC to August 9, 2011 21:45:57 UTC), and with energies [ 1-100 ] GeV (at these energies, the point spread function (PSF) is always less than 1 ).

Here, the confidence belts are constructed without prior knowledge of the number of counts within the central ROI around each dwarf. Under the assumption that the empirically derived background PMFs, exposures, and J values are correct, the belts have the proper coverage.

sources.summary: The paper describes Sculptor as counter-orbiting in the satellite plane, with proper-motion predictions offset from observations but an orbital pole closely aligned in the opposite direction.

quotes.quote: The orbital pole of Sculptor is offset by about 180^ \ from the direction preferred by the other satellites, so Sculptor appears to be counter-orbiting within the same orbital plane, as has already been noticed by Metz2008 . In their search for possible streams of satellite galaxies and GCs, LyndenBell1995 point out that Sculptor lies very close to the plane of the Magellanic Stream and that it is therefore probably associated with the stream. Based on this association and several assumptions, including that all objects in a stream have the same specific angular momentum in both absolute value and direction, they predict Sculptor's PM to be _ = 0.5 mas yr ^ 1 \ and _ = - 0.8 mas yr ^ 1 . This PM would result in an alignment of Sculptor's orbital pole with our average orbital pole direction, but unfortunately the published PMs of Sculptor are clearly offset from this prediction.
Interestingly, even Sculptor, which appears to be counter-orbiting within the VPOS, has a similar absolute value for its specific angular momentum h = 17.6 kpc km s ^ 1 .
In addition, Sculptor's orbital pole is also closely aligned with J_6^ mean \ ( 13^ \ inclined), but pointing in the opposite direction.

sources.summary: The paper treats Sculptor as an old stellar-system dwarf spheroidal, estimates its stellar mass and supernova history, and finds early bursts could plausibly form a dark-matter core.

quotes.quote: By using both deep multicolor photometry and large spectroscopic samples, deB12a,deB12b have recently reconstructed the SFHs of the Fornax and Sculptor dSphs. They find these to be essentially different systems: while Fornax experienced a complex SFH extending to less than only a few hundreds Myr ago, Sculptor had already completed its stellar build-up around 8 Gyr ago. By integrating the star formation rate in time, we estimate that the total stellar mass of the Fornax dSph is M_ * =(3.12 0.35) 10^7 M_ , while we obtain M_ * =(8.0 0.7) 10^6 M_ for the Sculptor dSph.

Our results shows that at redshifts higher than 4 (6), a single burst of SNe with low coupling is indeed able of creating a core of at least 100 pc in Sculptor (Fornax). For Fornax, this implies that a total of N_ SNII 3 10^5 SNeII explosions occurred to the present day, while a total of N_ SNII 8 10^4 exploded in Sculptor over its history. By coupling these SNe rates with the MAHs of the DM progenitors, as given by recent CDM N- body simulations, we find that it is energetically plausible to create cores of several hundred parsecs in both dwarfs.

sources.summary: The paper compares Sculptor metallicity-distribution data with merger-tree simulations, describing its mean metallicity, bimodal distribution, and mismatch with the simulated high-metallicity peak.

quotes.quote: As an illustration, we compare the MDFs of some of the merger tree simulations with those of observed Local Group dwarf galaxies: Fornax, Sculptor, Leo I and WLM. With absolute magnitudes of, respectively, M _V=-13.3 mag, 11.1 mag, 11.8 mag and 14.92 mag irwin95,mateo98,lokas09,devaucouleurs91 these galaxies fall in the luminosity interval covered by the simulations. Each galaxy is compared with a merger tree simulation that closely matches its mean [ Fe/H ] : [ Fe/H ] =-0.99, -1.68, and -1.43 for Fornax, Sculptor, and Leo I kirby11_3 and WLM with [ Fe/H ] =-1.28 leaman13 , respectively, and [ Fe/H ] =-1.1, -1.66, -1.31, and -1.33 for MT2, MT3, MT5, and MT1 respectively.

Like MT3, Sculptor has a bimodal MDF with a high-metallicity peak around [ Fe/H ] 1.3 and a low-metallicity peak below [ Fe/H ] 2 . We merely wish to illustrate that such bimodal MDFs are also found among real dwarfs in this luminosity range. Moreover, the agreement is not perfect: MT3 contains more low-metallicity stars than Sculptor and the high-metallicity peak is less strong.

sources.summary: The paper measures carbon abundances in Sculptor red giants and reports that its dSph [C/Fe] decline is tracked in the luminosity trend, with residuals correlated with metallicity.

quotes.quote: We measured carbon abundances of red giants (8 previously known) in the dSphs Sculptor, Fornax, Ursa Minor, and Draco. We also identified giants that are too carbon-rich to quantify their abundances. The stars observed here are a subset of the stars observed by kir09,kir10 , who measured Mg, Si, Ca, Ti, and Fe abundances in about 3000 red giants in eight dSphs.

The slope of the line roughly tracks the decline of [ C/Fe ] in the dSphs, especially in Sculptor and Draco. The scatter in [ C/Fe ] at fixed luminosity exceeds the scatter expected from measurement uncertainty. The residuals are correlated with [ Fe/H ] , which is represented by the color coding in Figure fig:dsphcfelogl .

sources.summary: The paper fits Sculptor dwarf-spheroidal kinematic data jointly with Fornax, using their core preference to constrain solitonic ultra-light axion dark matter and the axion mass.

quotes.quote: We use the data for Fornax and Sculptor with equal weight in the combined likelihood. The axion mass, m_a , is a global parameter which is the same for both Fornax and Sculptor. Our final model thus has seven parameters in total: m_a, _ s ^ F , _s^ S ,r_s^ F ,r_s^ S , ^ S , ^ F , with F, S, labelling Fornax and Sculptor respectively.

We investigated the validity of this claim by performing an MCMC analysis of the simplified stellar kinematic data of WP11 Walker&Penarrubia2011 for Fornax and Sculptor. This data shows a preference for cores over cusps, but other studies prefer cusps e.g. Breddels&Helmi2013,2014MNRAS.441.1584R . The preference of the WP11 data for large cores shows up as an upper bound on the axion DM mass of m_a<1.1 10^ 22 eV at 95 C.L., leaving the NFW parameters unconstrained and marginalised over.

sources.summary: The paper studies metal-poor stars in the Sculptor dwarf spheroidal, using their abundances and dispersion to probe early star formation and chemical homogeneity.

quotes.quote: We present the high-resolution spectroscopic study of five 3.9 [ Fe/H ] 2.5 stars in the Local Group dwarf spheroidal, Sculptor, thereby doubling the number of stars with comparable observations in this metallicity range. We carry out a detailed analysis of the chemical abundances of , iron peak, and light and heavy elements, and draw comparisons with the Milky Way halo and the ultra-faint dwarf stellar populations. We show that the bulk of the Sculptor metal-poor stars follow the same trends in abundance ratios versus metallicity as the Milky Way stars.

This paper presents the high-resolution spectroscopic analysis of five new metal-poor stars in the Local Group dwarf spheroidal galaxy Sculptor. Combining this new dataset with earlier work, there are now ten stars that have been observed at high spectroscopic resolution, and fourteen in total accounting for the medium-resolution analysis of , making Sculptor the first dwarf spheroidal galaxy in which trends and dispersion in the very metal-poor regime can start being established in some detail. These trends reveal the nature of the first generations of stars (e.g. mass, numbers, spatial distribution), and the level of homogeneity of the primitive interstellar medium (e.g. size/mass of star forming regions, nature and energy of the explosion of supernovae).

sources.summary: The paper contrasts Sculptor dSph with Draco, arguing its larger stellar mass produced more frequent early europium events and a smoother enrichment path while reaching a similar final Eu level.

quotes.quote: In the Sculptor dSph, a rise from the Eu floor happens at [ Fe/H ] 3.5 , a lower metallicity than Draco's, i.e., [ Fe/H ] 3 . Then, this Eu jump is followed by a continuous increase in [ Eu/H ] if we ignore an outlier showing a very low Eu abundance ( [ Eu/H ] = 2.75 ) at [ Fe/H ] 2.4 . We note that three unpublished data sets of [ Ba/H ] at [ Fe/H ] 2.4 Tolstoy_09 suggest Eu/H =-2.2 .

Such a relatively continuous feature is naturally understood if we take into consideration that the observed stellar mass of Sculptor is about eight times more than that of the Draco. We expect that the occurrence frequency of early Eu production events in the Sculptor will increase by the same factor, and as a result, more than 20 occurrences of Eu production are likely to build the smooth enrichment path as observed. Irrespective of its higher event rate, the final enrichment level should be the same as for Draco since the mass ratio of initial gas between the two dSphs is broadly equivalent to the ratio of the Eu event rate between the two.

sources.summary: The paper models Sculptor's metal-rich and metal-poor subpopulations in one potential, obtaining particle-mass ranges consistent with its main dwarf-spheroidal fits.

quotes.quote: We model the stellar density and velocity dispersion profiles of the metal-rich (MR) and metal-poor (MP) subpopulations in Sculptor Battaglia2008 . The MR subpopulation is known to be better described by radially biased orbits in the outer region due to its rapidly declining velocity dispersion profile Battaglia2008, Strigari2014 . Therefore, we follow Battaglia2008 and adopt the Osipkov-Merritt OM, Osipkov1979,Merritt1985 anisotropy profile on the MR subpopulation, and use either constant or OM anisotropy on the MP subpopulation.

The 1 ranges for constant and OM anisotropy on the MP subpopulation are _ 10 [ m_ /10^ 23 eV ] =1.21_ 0.15 ^ +0.17 and _ 10 [ m_ /10^ 23 eV ] =1.08_ 0.22 ^ +0.28 , respectively (also listed in table:discussion ). Both are in good agreement with table:fit .
We have also extended the soliton-only model to account for an NFW halo at a larger radius, and further considered distinct stellar subpopulations in both Sculptor and Fornax.

sources.summary: The paper follows up X-ray binary candidates in the Sculptor Dwarf Galaxy and finds Sculptor effectively lacks bright X-ray sources, implying present-day globular-cluster X-ray binaries are dynamically formed.

quotes.quote: We perform followup observations of previously-identified X-ray binary candidates in the Sculptor Dwarf Galaxy by combining a second \ observation with \ and Gemini photometry, as well as Gemini spectroscopy of selected targets. Of the original nine bright X-ray sources identified, we are able to classify all but one as quasars, active galactic nuclei, or background galaxies. We further discover four new X-ray sources in the second-epoch \ observation.

Three of these new sources are background sources and one is a foreground flaring star. We have found that Sculptor is effectively devoid of X-ray sources above a few 10^ 34 erg s ^ 1 . If Sculptor is able to retain primordial binaries at a similar rate to globular clusters, this implies that bright X-ray binaries observed in globular clusters in the present epoch are all formed dynamically.

sources.summary: The paper uses Sculptor's two-component stellar populations and measured mass-profile slope to test ultra-light dark matter, finding low axion masses fit its slope while high masses conflict with it.

quotes.quote: Walker:2011eg measure the half-light radii and velocity dispersions of both subcomponents in Fornax and Sculptor, and effectively resolve two discrete points in a mass profile dominated by dark matter. Walker:2011eg report the measured slope of the mass profiles defined as: log M log = log [ M(r_ h,2 )/M(r_ h,1 ) ] log [ r_ h,2 /r_ h,1 ] r_h=3/4 r_ 1/2 is the projected half-light radius. The measured slopes for Fornax and Sculptor dSphs are =2.61^ +0.43 _ 0.37 , and 2.95^ +0.51 _ 0.39 respectively Walker:2011eg and they rule out cuspy NFW profiles ( d log M/d log <2 at all radii) with a significance > 96 and > 99 , respectively. Figure f:fig1 shows the parametrized halo profiles based on simulations Schive:2014bp against observations of the half-mass radius of UFDs and dSphs. We show the observed measured mass profile slopes of Fornax and Sculptor with green and red lines respectively.

Similar to the results of Schive:2014bm,Marsh:2015iw,GonzalezMorales:2017br , low mass axions ( m<10^ 22 eV) can explain the observed mass profile slopes in Sculptor and Fornax. However, at such low masses, the predicted halo masses of the UFDs (such as Segue I) is too high given their dynamical state as we will return to in the next section. On the other hand, similar to the results of Calabrese:2016fx , high mass axions ( m>10^ 22 ) can explain the halo masses of the UFDs such as Draco II, Triangulum II, and Segue I, however, such high masses would predict halo profiles that do not agree with the observed mass profile slope of Fornax and Sculptor.

sources.summary: The paper uses Sculptor's independent SFH studies and chemical abundances, finding a flat [Eu/Mg] trend that favors a quick source and requires any delayed source to exceed Sculptor's SFH timescale.

quotes.quote: With the goal of better understanding the process and its nucleosynthetic site(s), we gathered the available chemical abundances of stars in the Milky Way and its three largest satellite dwarf spheroidal (dSph) galaxies: Sagittarius, Fornax, and Sculptor. The aim is to put these chemical abundances in context with the star formation histories (SFHs) of these galaxies.
Two independent studies of the Sculptor SFH are shown in Fig. fig:sfhabu : the solid line shows that of deBoer12 , and the dashed line is from Bettinelli19 .

On the contrary, the Milky Way and the Sculptor dSph galaxy have a relatively flat trend of [ Eu/Mg ] with [ Fe/H ] , which suggests an process (main production site of Eu) with short timescales. However, the larger dSph galaxies, Sagittarius and Fornax have an unambiguous increase in [ Eu/Mg ] with [ Fe/H ] , suggestive of a delayed process. The available observational data in the Local Group can therefore only be explained by (at least) two process sources: a quick site and a delayed site. The quick source has to have timescales shorter than those of AGB stars and SNIa (i.e. t_ quick 100 Myr) to explain the flat trends in the Milky Way and Sculptor. The delayed source has to have characteristic timescales (i.e. a timescale where the majority of events occur) that are longer than the SFH of Sculptor and longer than the time since the peak in star formation rate (SFR) in the solar neighborhood.

sources.summary: The paper models Sculptor as a dwarf spheroidal with a 3.4e8 solar-mass dark halo, one 7 Gyr star-formation episode, and abundance trends fit by its chemical-evolution model.

quotes.quote: For Sculptor dSph galaxy, we have adopted similar theoretical prescriptions to (who first modelled the chemical evolution of Sculptor) and to . We assumed a dark matter halo of mass M_ DM =3.4 10^ 8 M_ ( Battaglia2008 ) and a core radius R_ DM =1 kpc . The effective radius of the luminous component of the galaxy has been set to R_L= 260 pc ( Walker2009 ).

The SF of Sculptor has been derived from the CMD fitting analysis by and it consists of one episode of star formation which lasts 7 Gyr. Our predicted SFR as a function of time is shown in panel (a) of Figure fig: SFR+MDF_Scl . It is characterized by an initial fast increase, due to the short time-scale of the infall, followed by a decline caused by the onset of the galactic wind.

sources.summary: The paper compares Sculptor chemical-evolution constraints, finding sub-solar Type Ia Mn and Ni yields, early concentrated star formation, and consistency with substantial sub-Chandrasekhar contributions.

quotes.quote: measured Mn abundances for Sculptor and fitted the same models as to find Mn _ Ia =(-0.30 0.03) . These authors also suggest that systems with early concentrated star formation (such as Sculptor) have lower Mn _ Ia , and hence higher fractions of sub-Chandrasekhar mass Type Ia supernovae, than systems with more extended star formation. Sculptor has a sub-solar Type Ia Mn yield that is 0.1 lower than that for the .

As for the result, the Sculptor result is consistent with a mixture of and from the models or near 100 from the or models. Similarly, both Sculptor or Leo II have sub-solar Ni _ Ia like the . However, there is also the suggestion of metallicity dependence in the Type Ia yields with Sculptor having a 0.1 lower Mn yield than , despite both systems having similar early bursts of star formation.

sources.summary: The paper presents Sculptor as an old, intrinsically metal-poor dwarf spheroidal whose earliest chemical enrichment differs from the Milky Way and may preserve hypernova signatures.

quotes.quote: Of these, almost half of the most metal-poor stars ( Fe/H 3.4 ) belong to the ancient Sculptor dSph galaxy, which is intrinsically metal-poor, Fe/H =-1.8 and is dominated by an old stellar population > 10 Gyr old Bettinelli19 .

The ratios of [ Mg/Ti ] in the Sculptor dSph differ from other galaxies, both the Milky Way and other dwarf galaxies, with a declining trend towards lower [ Fe/H ] . Combined with the lack of CEMP stars in this galaxy, this is further evidence that the earliest chemical enrichment of Sculptor was significantly different from that of the Milky Way. The lack of known stars in other dwarf galaxies at Fe/H <-3.5 makes it currently impossible to conclude whether this trend of [ Mg/Ti ] is unique for Sculptor, or a common trait of dSph, or even UFD galaxies.

At the same time, given the only modest size and star formation rate of Sculptor, the imprints of hypernovae will not be lost or rapidly diluted by subsequent star formation events. The Sculptor dwarf spheroidal galaxy may thus be the ideal system for further discoveries.

sources.summary: The paper uses Sculptor as the southern dwarf-spheroidal dark-matter target for CTA simulations, modeling 300 hours of gamma-ray observations to test portal dark-matter scenarios.

quotes.quote: Two dwarf spheroidal galaxies are used as targets: Draco in the northern hemisphere, and Sculptor in the southern hemisphere. Both are classical DM targets, extensively studied before, and the uncertainties on the DM flux are expected to be much lower compared to other DM targets. For these resaons, they are suited to test the IS and the ID model presented in sections ISmodel and IDmodel .

As a typical exposure time , 300 hours of observations of these two targets are simulated using the ctools software package and adopting CTA-North array for Draco and the CTA-South array for Sculptor. Tab. target_parameters gathers the useful parameters for these two targets. Gamma-ray events with energy between 30 GeV and 100 TeV are generated from each target source with ctobssim assuming a point-like source, using the corresponding instrument response functions (IRF ), and a pointing offset of 0.5 \ from the source coordinates.

sources.summary: The paper detects a significant Sculptor distance gradient and finds oblate or triaxial shapes possible, with intrinsic ellipticity above 0.46 and possible nonzero triaxiality.

quotes.quote: firstpage - lastpage We combine the Dark Energy Camera Legacy Survey (DECaLS) DR8 photometry with Gaia photometry to study the 3-D structure of Bootes I, Draco, Ursa Minor, Sextans and Sculptor dwarf galaxies using blue horizontal branch (BHB) stars as distance indicators. We construct a new colour-absolute magnitude of BHB stars that we use to measure the distance gradients within the body of the dwarf galaxies. We detect a statistically significant non-zero gradient only in Sextans and Sculptor. Through modeling of the gradient and 2-D density of the systems by triaxial Plummer models we find that the distance gradients in both dwarf galaxies are inconsistent with prolate shape, but compatible with oblate or triaxial shapes. In order to explain the observed gradients, oblate models of Sextans and Sculptor need to have a significant intrinsic ellipticity larger than 0.47 for Sextans and 0.46 for Sculptor.
For Sculptor the result of triaxial model shows possible oblate shape with intrinsic ellipticity larger than 0.46 and possibly non-zero triaxiality.

sources.summary: The paper includes Sculptor as a classical southern dwarf spheroidal target, modeling its dark-matter profile uncertainties and contrasting its J-factor behavior with other targets.

quotes.quote: For definiteness, we pick here two classical dSphs (Draco and Sculptor, respectively in the Northern and Southern sky) and one prototypical ultra-faint (Reticulum II), which were found to be among the best-ranked targets for DM annihilation in . In the latter studies, the DM profile parameters were reconstructed from a Markov Chain Monte Carlo (MCMC) engine coupled to a Jeans analysis with the CLUMPY code https://clumpy.gitlab.io/CLUMPY/ ; see for more details on the methodology. We use these chains to calculate the median profile, that we adopt as a reference for our analyses here We do not use the best-fit profile parameters, because the scarcity of data in ultra-faint dSphs make them display an unphysical behaviour (e.g., a very flat and extended profile). Using `effective' structural parameters matching the median profile cures this issue.

For s wave annihilation and in the absence of any Sommerfeld enhancement, the uncertainty on the reconstruction of the DM profile parameters leads to factors of a few for classical dSphs (Draco and Sculptor), and a factor 20 for the Reticulum II ultra-faint dSph (top-left panel of fig:JS_smooth_error_bars ). This is the same in the saturation regime, off resonance, where the Sommerfeld factor does not depend on the velocity. Yet, in the Coulomb regime, and at resonance peaks in the saturation regime, the additional velocity dependence goes in the opposite direction with respect to ^ 2 . For instance, for a given value of the scale radius r_ 2 , a larger value of _ 2 gives larger ^ 2 () but at the same time a larger typical velocity which enters the Sommerfeld factor through v ^ 1 or v ^ 2 , leading to a reduction of the generalised J factors. This leads to 68 uncertainty bands that are typically smaller in the Coulomb regime --- , less than an order of magnitude for Reticulum II --- than in the no-Sommerfeld case. For p wave annihilation (top-right panel of fig:JS_smooth_error_bars ), the uncertainty band spans about two orders of magnitude for Draco in the no-Sommerfeld regime, but `only' one for Sculptor.

The most dramatic dependence is observed for dSphs (solid lines), for which we can go from favorable ( >1 ) to unfavorable ( <1 ) contrasts; for the p wave case in particular (right panel), the contrast for Sculptor goes from 10 (for _ 10^ 3 ) to 10^ 2 (for _ >1 ).

sources.summary: The paper finds no v_z gradient for Sculptor; its small gradient while moving away from pericentre is interpreted as possible evidence that recent Milky Way interaction disrupted coherent kinematics.

quotes.quote: We do not find gradients in v_z for Sculptor or Sextans. The absence of gradients in v_z in these galaxies suggests that they are not experiencing rotation along the line-of-sight direction.

Secondly, the amplitude of the gradients in v_z for Sculptor and Sextans (area marked as "II") is small, and compatible with zero within 1 . These galaxies are currently moving away from their orbits pericentres ( v_R^ GC > 0 ). Such recent interaction with the MW is likely to have severely affected their internal kinematics. The tidal shock would have disrupted the kinematics of the systems, which would explain the absence of coherent kinematics among their stars.

sources.summary: Sculptor RRds are compared with Fornax and LMC; their distributions suggest different chemical enrichment histories and a truncated short-period tail.

quotes.quote: We compare the properties of the RRds in Fornax with those in the Magellanic Clouds and in nearby dSphs, with special focus on Sculptor.

All the pulsation diagnostics adopted in this investigation suggest that old stellar populations in Fornax and Sculptor dSphs underwent different chemical enrichment histories. Fornax RRds are similar to SMC RRds, while Sculptor RRds are more similar to the metal-rich component of the LMC RRds. stars: variables: RR Lyrae -- galaxies: dwarf -- (galaxies:) Magellanic Clouds There is solid theoretical christy66,bono94b and empirical cox1983 evidence that the pulsation period of the RR Lyrae stars (RRLs) depends on their physical properties: stellar mass ( M ), luminosity ( L ), effective temperature ( T_ eff ) and chemical composition ( X,Y,Z , that is, H, He and metal abundances by mass fraction).

The distribution of Sculptor and LMC RRds is similar: both have their peak in density around P_0 0.48 days and they are extended to longer periods, but with lower density. The main difference is that the short-period tail of the LMC ( P_0 0.46 days) is still highly populated, while the distribution of Sculptor RRds seems to be truncated. Although, the number of RRds in these two galaxies is quite different (30 vs 2083 objects) one can safely assume that the difference is not just a matter of statistics.

sources.summary: The paper uses the Sculptor dwarf spheroidal galaxy to test Gaia DR3 RR Lyrae PLZ/PWZ calibrations, deriving internally consistent but systematically small distance moduli.

quotes.quote: We test the performance of our PLZ and PWZ relations by determining the distance moduli of both galactic and extragalactic stellar associations: the Sculptor dwarf spheroidal galaxy in the Local Group (finding _ 0 =19.47 0.06 ), the Galactic globular clusters M4 ( _ 0 =11.16 0.05 ) and the Reticulum globular cluster in the Large Magellanic Cloud ( _ 0 =18.23 0.06 ).

The three systems are between metal poor and metal intermediate, with [ Fe/H ] = -1.66 dex 2004MNRAS.352..153M for Reticulum, [ Fe/H ] = -1.10 dex see 2015ApJ...799..165B for M4, and [ Fe/H ] = -1.85 dex Mullen2022 for Sculptor dSph. The three systems contain numerous known : 32 in Reticulum 1976ApJ...208..932D, 1992AJ....103.1166W , 47 in M4 2001AJ....122.2587C,2014PASP..126..521S , and 536 in Sculptor dSph 10.1093/mnrasl/slw093 . For this analysis, we used the mean V magnitudes published by 2013AJ....145..160K for Reticulum, by 2014PASP..126..521S for M4, and by 10.1093/mnrasl/slw093 in Sculptor dSph. The infrared photometry available for each system is in the Spitzer IRAC 3.6 and 4.5 m filters respectively 2018MNRAS.480.4138M, 2015ApJ...808...11N, 2018MNRAS.481..578G , similar to W1 and W2 , and showing no significant offset in mean magnitude as shown by 2017ApJ...841...84N . It is worth noting that only W1 photometry was available for Sculptor dSph, not W2 .

sources.summary: The paper fits Sculptor line-of-sight velocity-dispersion data with STVG Jeans models and reports that one fitted parameter shows more than 5 tension with the comparison value.

quotes.quote: In our analysis, we will predict the theoretical velocity dispersion profile projected along the line of sight by solving Eq. eq:sigmalos and fit it to the measured line-of-sight velocity dispersion profiles of the eight dSphs, namely Carina, Fornax, Sculptor, Sextans, Draco, Leo I, Leo II, and Ursa Minor, reported in Table tab:1 . As a product of this procedure, for each galaxy, we will estimate the best-fit values of the STVG's parameters and , the velocity anisotropy parameter , and the stellar mass-to-light ratio M_*/L_V , and their corresponding uncertainties using an MCMC analysis. In more detail, the kinematic data sets of the following dSphs: Carina, Fornax, Sculptor, and Sextans; were obtained with the Michigan/MIKE Fiber Spectrograph . On the other hand, the kinematic data sets of Draco, Leo I, Leo II, and Ursa Minor were obtained with the Hectochelle fiber spectrograph at the MMT .

We employed a MCMC algorithm to explore the four-dimensional parameter space = , , , M_*/L , and to estimate the values of the parameters that can fit the observational data sets of the line-of-sight velocity dispersion profiles of the dSphs galaxies Carina, Draco, Fornax, Leo I, Leo II, Sculptor, Sextans, and Ursa Minor.
However, in the case of Fornax, Leo II, Sculptor, and Ursa Minor the estimation of the parameter shows more than 5 tension with .

sources.summary: The paper summarizes Sculptor dSph’s stellar mass, star-formation history, and abundance trends, then models how rare enrichment events affect its metal-poor stars.

quotes.quote: Sculptor dSph Sculptor dSph (mass in stars M_*=3.9 10^6  M _ ) foot:mass is the first dSph to be discovered in 1938 by Harlow Shapley 1938BHarO.908....1S . This dSph appears at a high latitude and observations are less affected by the extinction. Thus, Sculptor dSph is one of the most studied dwarf galaxies in the Local Group. At first, this galaxy is recognized as a system similar to globular clusters 1939PASP...51...40B with no young star or gas. Later, it is found that Sculptor dSph has an extended star formation history compared to globular clusters and that the intermediate-age population exists as well as the old ( > 10 Gyr) population e.g. and references therein 2001MNRAS.327..918T .

In Sculptor dSph, the impact of individual events (mainly collapsars) is seen at [ Fe/H ]   3 . The oscillation in the [ Eu/Fe ] ratio reduces with increasing metallicity, because the -process events occur sufficiently to make the impact of individual events weak and that the abundance of Eu in the galaxy gradually increases.

sources.summary: The paper treats Sculptor as the dwarf spheroidal host of AS0039, comparing its star-formation duration, mass, and early evolution to simulations of Pop III hypernova signatures.

quotes.quote: We observe the narrow scatter of stellar abundances in normal Pop II stars showing similar X/Fe values compared to those of stars in Sculptor and the MW. This similarity is likely the result of the short star formation duration of 0.5 Gyr in the simulated UFD analog, while Sculptor has had a star formation duration of t 1-3 Gyr, as reported in previous studies ( Kirby2011, Weisz2014, Bettinelli2019, delosReyes2022 ), and the MW is undergoing ongoing star formation ( Elia2022 ).

It is worth noting that the simulated galaxy in this study has less mass, approximately two orders of magnitude lower than the Sculptor galaxy, where AS0039 was discovered. The Sculptor galaxy is classified as a dwarf spheroidal (dSph) galaxy and has a mass of M_ 1.8 10^6 . Nevertheless, the results of this study indicate that stars like AS0039 could have formed during the early stages of the evolution of the Sculptor galaxy. This is supported by the fact that the formation of Pop III stars occurs early in the Universe with a cosmic time of t_ H 0.75 Gyr , and the remnants of Pop III stars are only found in Pop II stars that formed shortly after.

sources.summary: The paper uses Sculptor's RR Lyrae period and Bailey-diagram structure as evidence that its underlying stellar population has a large metallicity spread.

quotes.quote: A similar feature in the period distribution of RRc stars was also detected by kal95 in the Sculptor dwarf spheroidal (dSph) galaxy. The macho peak in this sample was located at P 0.290 days. According to theoretical and empirical evidence, fundamental RR Lyrae (RRab) present an anti-correlation between period and amplitude, i.e. RRab stars with longer periods present smaller luminosity amplitudes. On the other hand, RRc stars present, in the Bailey diagram (luminosity amplitude vs period), a distribution that resembles a ``bell'' shape bono99 . The Bailey diagram of RRc variables in Sculptor shows two shifted ``bell'' shapes, and the bulk of stars are located across the two peaks detected in the period distribution.

RR Lyrae variables in Sculptor appear as the template for this effect, since current spectroscopic measurements support the evidence that its mean metal content is Fe/H 1.5 dex with a dispersion of 0.9 dex tall02 .

sources.summary: The paper presents Sculptor as a nearby dwarf spheroidal with two ancient stellar components, showing metal-rich stars are more concentrated and kinematically cooler than metal-poor stars.

quotes.quote: We used the ESO Wide Field Imager (WFI) in conjunction with the VLT/FLAMES spectrograph to study the properties of the resolved stellar population of Sculptor out to and beyond the tidal radius. We find that two components are discernible in the spatial distribution of Horizontal Branch stars in our imaging, and in the [ Fe/H ] and v _ hel distributions for our large sample of spectroscopic measurements. They can be generally described as a ``metal-poor'' component ( [ Fe/H ] < -1.7 ) and a ``metal-rich'' component ( [ Fe/H ] > -1.7 ). The metal-poor stars are more spatially extended than the metal-rich stars, and they also appear to be kinematically distinct.

Our FLAMES results show that Scl contains two distinct stellar components, one metal-rich, 0.9 > [ Fe/H ] > -1.7 , and one metal-poor, 1.7 > [ Fe/H ] > -2.8 . The metal-rich population is more centrally concentrated than the metal poor, and on average appears to have a lower velocity dispersion, _ metal-rich = 7 1 km/s, whereas _ metal-poor = 11 1 km/s (see Figure 4).

sources.summary: The paper measures detailed abundances for Sculptor red giants, confirms a broad metallicity range, and argues its abundance trends differ from Galactic halo stars, limiting Sculptor-like systems as major halo contributors.

quotes.quote: We have used high-resolution, high signal-to-noise spectra obtained with the VLT and UVES to determine abundances of 17 elements in 4 red giants in the Sculptor dwarf spheroidal galaxy. Our [ Fe/H ] values range from --2.10 to --0.97, confirming previous findings of a large metallicity spread. We have combined our data with similar data for five Sculptor giants studied recently by Shetrone et al. to form one of the largest samples of high resolution abundances yet obtained for a dwarf spheroidal galaxy, covering essentially the full known metallicity range in this galaxy. These properties allow us to establish trends of [ X/Fe ] with [ Fe/H ] for many elements, X. The trends are significantly different from the trends seen in galactic halo and globular cluster stars.

The many discrepancies in the relationships between [ X/Fe ] as seen in Sculptor compared with Galactic field stars indicates that our halo cannot be made up in bulk of stars similar to those presently seen in dwarf spheroidal galaxies like Sculptor, corroborating similar conclusions reached by Shetrone et al., Fulbright and Tolstoy et al.

sources.summary: The paper studies Sculptor RGB and HB morphology with optical and near-infrared photometry, finding a color spread, metallicity range, and mean metallicities from RGB/AGB bumps and isochrone fitting.

quotes.quote: The Sculptor dwarf spheroidal galaxy has a giant branch with a significant spread in colour, symptomatic of an intrinsic age/metallicity spread. We present here a detailed study of the Sculptor giant branch and horizontal branch morphology, combining new near-infrared photometry from the Cambridge InfraRed Survey Instrument (CIRSI), with optical data from the ESO Wide Field Imager. For a Sculptor-like old and generally metal-poor system, the position of Red Giant Branch (RGB) and Asymptotic Giant Branch (AGB) stars on the colour-magnitude diagram (CMD) is mainly metallicity dependent.

Our measured photometric width of the Sculptor giant branch corresponds to a range in metallicity of 0.75 dex. We detect the RGB and AGB bumps in both the near-infrared and the optical luminosity functions, and derive from them a mean metallicity of [ M/H ] = -1.3 0.1 . From isochrone fitting we derive a mean metallicity of [ Fe/H ] = -1.42 with a dispersion of 0.2 dex.

sources.summary: The paper models the Sculptor dSph as an early-forming system with no significant recent star formation, extended self-enrichment, and unusually small [Mg/Fe] dispersion.

quotes.quote: Sculptor The Sculptor dSph has formed stars early, over a period of a few Gyrs. No significant intermediate age population has been found, hence excluding star formation within the last 5 Gyr . The evidence for low element enhancement agrees with an extended star formation and self-enrichment over a period of at least 2 Gyr e.g. babusiaux05, shetrone03, tolstoy03 . Sculptor's properties are well reproduced by model 630 ( L= 2.86 10^6 L_ , [ Fe/H ] =-1.82 ). The outstanding feature of Sculptor, as compared to the two previous dSph, is the small dispersion in the [ Mg/Fe ] vs [ Fe/H ] diagram (Fig. MgFevsFe ).

sources.summary: The paper applies tidal-stripping models to Sculptor, highlighting its clear metal-rich/metal-poor kinematic separation, centrally concentrated metal-rich stars, and comparatively external orbit.

quotes.quote: We apply these ideas to the Sculptor and Carina dwarf spheroidals. Differences in their orbits might help to explain, under the assumption of similar initial configurations, why in the former a clear kinematical separation between metal poor and metal rich stars is apparent, while in Carina this segregation is significantly more subtle.

Further new evidence on the presence of multiple populations came a couple of years later, when Tolstoy et al. (2004) tolstoy04 added to wide-field imaging also the information from wide-field spectroscopy, which yielded metallicities and line-of-sight velocities for hundreds individual stars in the Sculptor dSph. These authors found that metal rich stars in Sculptor are more centrally concentrated and have on average lower velocity dispersions than those of the metal poor component, which on the contrary, show hotter kinematics and are more extended.

Figure fig:orbits shows that despite their similar present-day distance from the host, an orbit such as that suggested by Sculptor's proper motions (Orb1) is significantly more external than Carina's (Orb2), with apocenters exceeding 150 kpc and pericenter distances of 75 kpc.

sources.summary: The paper treats Sculptor as a classical dwarf spheroidal with low-metallicity RGB stars, comparing CaT and high-resolution metallicities and noting an extremely metal-poor star.

quotes.quote: However, one of the compelling results from studies of large samples of RGB stars is a significant lack of stars with metallicities [ Fe/H ] 3 in the classical dSph galaxies Sculptor, Fornax, Carina and Sextans compared to the metallicity distribution function of the Galactic halo helm06 . These metallicities are inferred from the line strengths of the Ca II NIR triplet (CaT) lines. Recently, kirb09 reported to have found a RGB star in Sculptor with a [ Fe/H ] value as low as --3.8 using a comparison between spectra and an extensive spectral library.

A direct detailed comparison between the low-resolution CaT metallicities and high-resolution measurements for large samples of RGB stars in the nearby dwarf galaxies Fornax and Sculptor is given by batt08b .

However, when comparing the sensitivity of the CaT equivalent width measurements of RGB stars in Sculptor and Fornax with high-resolution [ Fe/H ] and [ Ca/H ] measurements batt08b find that the CaT equivalent width is actually a more robust estimator of Fe than Ca.

sources.summary: The paper treats Sculptor as a DART dwarf spheroidal test case, noting its high latitude yields the least foreground but a relatively high giant-star fraction; for Sculptor, the Mg-line criterion underperforms velocity selection.

quotes.quote: Fornax, Sculptor and Sextans As a test case of different mixtures of MW interlopers we look at the expected contamination along three different line-of-sights, specifically to the Sextans, Sculptor and Fornax dSphs. To study the likely foreground contamination towards these objects, we used the Besan on model robin03 . All interloper stars were selected within a 4 deg ^2 solid angle around these galaxies, at a distance between 0 and 200 kpc.
Sculptor, being the galaxy at highest latitude has the least foreground in general, but a relatively high fraction of its foreground are giant stars, difficult to weed out with our method.
The line criterion is less effective than the velocity criterion for Sextans and Sculptor.

sources.summary: The paper measures manganese abundances in Sculptor stars, finding sub-solar Mn/Fe ratios, a plateau followed by decline at higher metallicity, and chemical-evolution agreement only with metallicity-dependent SNe Ia Mn yields.

quotes.quote: We provide manganese abundances (corrected for the effect of the hyperfine structure) for a large number of stars in the dwarf spheroidal galaxies Sculptor and Fornax, and for a smaller number in the Carina and Sextans dSph galaxies. Abundances had already been determined for a number of other elements in these galaxies, including and iron-peak ones, which allowed us to build [ Mn/Fe ] and [ Mn/ ] versus [ Fe/H ] diagrams. The Mn abundances imply sub-solar [ Mn/Fe ] ratios for the stars in all four galaxies examined.
In Sculptor, [ Mn/Fe ] stays roughly constant between [ Fe/H ] 1.8 and 1.4 and decreases at higher iron abundance.
The observed [ Mn/Fe ] versus [ Fe/H ] relation in Sculptor, Fornax, and Carina can be reproduced only by the chemical evolution models that include a metallicity-dependent Mn yield from the SNe Ia.

sources.summary: The paper uses Sculptor halo-profile measurements to argue for a roughly kiloparsec dark-matter core; it also notes that its extended star-formation history conflicts with early core-formation scenarios.

quotes.quote: Recently, Walker Pe narrubia (2011; WP11) have measured the slope of the halo mass profile ( ) in the two brightest MW dSphs, Fornax and Sculptor ( M_ /M_ 3 10^7 and 8 10^6 respectively; de Boer et al. 2012a,b). The values, M / =2.61_ 0.37 ^ +0.43 and =2.95_ 0.39 ^ +0.51 , rule out NFW profiles ( < 2 ) at confidence levels 96 and 99 , respectively.
As expected from eq. ( eq:gamma ), the values of measured in Fornax and Sculptor suggest r_c 1 kpc.

The cored profiles found in Sculptor and Fornax suggest z_ core 6 . However, stellar ages are at odds with this scenario, as both galaxies show extended periods of star formation continuing for 6-7 Gyr in Sculptor (de Boer et al. 2012a), and for 12-13 Gyr in Fornax (del Pino et al. 2011; de Boer et al. 2012b).

sources.summary: The paper describes Sculptor abundance trends as controversial: some alpha ratios decline with metallicity, while DART and medium-resolution data show a Ca plateau ending near -1.8.

quotes.quote: This is consistent with the conclusion of kir11b , who found that no dSph has a high \ plateau that extends to > -2.5 , except for [ Ca/Fe ] in Sculptor. The existence of an \ plateau in Sculptor is controversial. The \ ratios measured by she03 and gei05 decline monotonically with metallicity. Measurements from the Dwarf Abundances and Radial Velocities Team show a plateau in [ Ca/Fe ] ending at = -1.8 tol09 . kir09,kir11b confirmed this result with medium-resolution spectroscopy of 376 Sculptor red giants.

sources.summary: The paper describes Sculptor as an extended, complex dwarf spheroidal whose surface-density distribution is fit by the DIS model and whose stellar component is kinematically hotter than Carina's.

quotes.quote: A similar feature is seen in Sculptor breddels14 , indicating that some dSphs might be more complex 2-component galaxies amorisco11 .

In contrast, the stellar distribution of the much more extended Sculptor dSph (upper right panel of figure 2) deviates strongly from a Gaussian. The red solid lines in both figures show the surface density profiles of DIS systems following equations 3 to 7 with = 3.3 and = 1.5 for Carina and Sculptor, respectively. With respect to the dark matter component, the stellar system in Sculptor ( _* = 0.81 _d ) is kinematically hotter than in Carina ( _* = 0.55 _d ), leading to a more extended structure. It turns out that the equations 3 to 7 lead to surface density distributions that fit all dSphs very well, even those with more complex, extended stellar components like Sculptor.

sources.summary: The paper evaluates how Gaia and TMT proper motions could constrain Sculptor's mass and dark-matter density slope, testing whether its dwarf spheroidal core is cusped or cored.

quotes.quote: Walker et al.'s Magellan/MMFS Survey survey identified 1355 red giant candidates in the dwarf spheroidal galaxy Sculptor. We find that the Gaia satellite will be able to measure the proper motions of 139 of these with a precision of between 13 and 20 km/s. Using a Jeans analysis and 5-parameter density model we show that this allows a determination of the mass within the deprojected half-light radius to within 16 and a measurement of the dark matter density exponent to within 0.68 within that radius. If, even at first light, the TMT observes Sculptor then the combined observations will improve the precision on these proper motions to about 5 km/s, about 5 years earlier than would be possible without Gaia, further improving the precision of to 0.27. Using a bimodal stellar population model for Sculptor the precision of improves by about 30 .

sources.summary: The paper uses Sculptor as an observed dwarf-galaxy constraint, citing cored-profile results at 500 pc, multi-Gyr star formation, and a directly measured inner-slope quantity for model comparison.

quotes.quote: Schwarzschild modelling has been used for (e.g.) Sculptor and Fornax finding cored profiles Jardel2012,Breddels2013,Jardel2013b,Jardel2013a . Methods based on multiple stellar populations concluded that Fornax (slope measured at 1 kpc) and Sculptor (slope measured at 500 pc) have a cored profile Battaglia2008,Walker2011,Agnello2012,Amorisco2012 .
This conclusion is at odds with the fact that star formation went on for 12-13 Gyr in Fornax deBoer2012b , and for 6-7 Gyr in Sculptor deBoer2012a .

These models are compared with a set of data composed by the galaxies, the galaxy sample excluding those restudied by Adams2014 , the THINGS dwarves Oh2011a,Oh2011b , the THINGS galaxies Chemin2011,Oh2015 , the LITTLE THINGS galaxies, provided by Oh, and Fornax, and Sculptor, whose inner slope was calculated by without adopting a DM halo model, and directly from stellar spectroscopic data. They measured the quantity M / , finding a value of =2.61_ 0.37 ^ +0.43 for Fornax, and =2.95_ 0.39 ^ +0.51 for Sculptor.

sources.summary: The paper models the Sculptor dSph CMD, reproducing its main features while attributing faint blue discrepancies to metal-poor populations absent from the one-zone model.

quotes.quote: In this work, we apply our photo-chemical model to reproduce the observed CMD of the Sculptor dSph and find that we can reproduce the main features of the observed CMD. The main discrepancies are found at fainter magnitudes in the main sequence turn-off and sub-giant branch, where the observed CMD extends towards bluer colors than the synthetic one; we suggest that this is a signature of metal-poor stellar populations in the data, which cannot be captured by our assumed one-zone chemical evolution model.

The Sculptor dwarf galaxy was discovered by shapley1938 . Although it might appear simple at first glance, from the study of the kinematical, chemical and spatial distribution of its stellar populations, tolstoy2004 were able to disentangle in this galaxy an inner, kinematically `cold', metal-rich stellar population from an outer `hot' metal-poor one, later on confirmed by battaglia2008 and walker2011 . Other studies based on photometric datasets also were able to identify (or confirm) the existence of stellar populations distinct in metallicity majewski1999 , age and spatial distribution deboer2011,deboer2012 .

sources.summary: The paper summarizes Sculptor Eu-abundance literature: eight of twenty-one stars show r-process enhancement, but only one qualifies as an r-process I star and none as II stars.

quotes.quote: Sculptor Seven papers report Eu abundances or upper limits for a total of 21 stars in Sculptor shetrone2003,geisler2005,tafelmeyer2010,kirby2012,simon2015,jablonka2015,skuladottir2015 . Eight of these stars show enhancement in process elements, including one I star from the sample of kirby2012 . The other seven stars with Eu enhancement (four from shetrone2003 and three from geisler2005 ) are also enhanced in the s process element Ba.

Of the remaining 13 stars, three have subsolar Eu detection or upper limits geisler2005,jablonka2015 , nine have upper limits above solar that do not provide significant constraints tafelmeyer2010,simon2015,jablonka2015 , and one is identified as a CEMP-no star Carbon enhanced metal-poor star with no enhancement in neutron-capture elements beers2005 . skuladottir2015 . In summary only one of the 21 stars in Sculptor with Eu measurements qualifies as an I star and none are II stars.

sources.summary: The paper describes Sculptor as having abundance data from 13 papers and 964 unique stars, with major Kirby-series contributions and additional VLT/FLAMES, VIMOS, and high-resolution spectroscopy.

quotes.quote: The abundance data are provided by 13 papers, most of which come from the papers by Kirby and his colleagues Kirby2009,Kirby2010,Kirby2012a,Kirby2012b,Kirby2015 . The total number of stars (without duplication) is 964. Other large contribution is provided by Lardo2016 (94 stars) using the VLT/VIMOS and by Battaglia2008 (93 stars) using the VLT/FLAMES. Among them, only Battaglia2008 provide data based on CaT equivalent widths. Other studies Starkenburg2013,Shetrone2003,Simon2015,Geisler2005,Tafelmeyer2010,Skuladottir2015 give minor contribution in statistics but much information on elemental abundances thanks to high resolution spectroscopy.

sources.summary: The paper treats Sculptor as a classical dwarf-spheroidal satellite in a homogeneous very metal-poor stellar-parameter sample for comparing dwarf-galaxy and Milky Way halo populations.

quotes.quote: We present a homogeneous set of accurate atmospheric parameters for a complete sample of very and extremely metal-poor stars in the dwarf spheroidal galaxies (dSphs) Sculptor, Ursa Minor, Sextans, Fornax, Bo otes I, Ursa Major II, and Leo IV.

The comparison between ultra-faint, classical dwarf spheroidal galaxies (UFDs, dSphs), and the Milky Way population offers a fantastic opportunity to probe different galaxy masses, star formation histories, levels of chemical enrichement. Both nucleosynthetic processes and galaxy formation models largely benefit from the diversity of the populations sampled that way.

We selected 36 stars in total in the classical dSphs Sculptor (Scl), Ursa Minor (UMi), Fornax (Fnx), and Sextans (Sex) and the ultra-faint dwarfs Bo otes I, Ursa Major II (UMa II), and Leo IV (Table Tab:spectra ). This sample covers the 4 Fe/H < -1.5 metallicity range.

sources.summary: The paper estimates Sculptor's enclosed mass, compares it to previous mass-follows-light and distribution-function results, and derives cuspy and cored halo mass ranges.

quotes.quote: Sculptor (L_ 2 10^6 L_ ) The enclosed mass we infer for Sculptor, M(<1.8 = (3.5^ +1.1 _ 0.9 ) 10^7 M_ , is again close to the total mass quoted by Lokas2009 for models where mass follows light, (3.1 0.2) 10^7 M_ . Strigari2017 use a distribution function based approach to conclude that observed stellar velocities in the Sculptor dwarf are consistent with an NFW DM halo with a maximum circular velocity of 20 < / < 35 . This is similar to the range of maximum circular velocities we find under the assumption of cuspy 1,5,1 profiles, 18 / 32 . This corresponds to a total halo mass of M_ tot = (2.0^ +1.6 _ 1.7 ) 10^9 M_ . For cored 1,5,0 profiles, we find 40 / 74 , and M_ tot = (2.6^ +1.9 _ 2.1 ) 10^ 10 M_ .

sources.summary: The paper treats Sculptor as a well-studied two-population dwarf, comparing its luminosity, velocity dispersion, metallicity, and half-light radius with simulations and identifying two metallicity populations in observational data.

quotes.quote: Sculptor, Fornax and Andromeda II, particularly well studied examples of two-population dwarfs, are identified on the plot. It can be seen that our sample contains galaxies of comparable luminosities to these three dwarfs. A number of simulated galaxies match the size and velocity dispersion measurements for Fornax, although its mean metallicity is above the locus traced by our galaxy sample. The sample contains matches for Sculptor in velocity dispersion and mean metallicity, although the half-light radius of Sculptor is below those seen in our simulations at comparable luminosities.
This is described in Appendix  AppendixC , where our method only identifies two populations in Sculptor, with a metallicity cut between the two consistent with battagliaSculptor .

sources.summary: The paper examines Sculptor red giants and finds no globular-cluster-like CN/CH or Na/CN abundance anomalies, supporting a distinct low-density dwarf-spheroidal star-formation environment.

quotes.quote: Consequently, we have investigated the anti-correlation and bimodality of CH and CN band strengths, which are markers of the abundance variations in GGCs, in the spectra of red giants in the Sculptor dwarf spheroidal galaxy. Using spectra at the Na D lines, informed by similar spectra for five GGCs (NGC 288, 1851, 6752, 6809 and 7099), we have also searched for any correlation between CN and Na in the Sculptor red giant sample. Our results indicate that variations analogous to those seen in GGCs are not present in our Sculptor sample. Instead, we find a weak positive correlation between CH and CN, and no correlation between Na and CN. We also reveal a deficiency in [ Na/Fe ] for the Sculptor stars relative to the values in GGCs, a result which is consistent with previous work for dSph galaxies.

sources.summary: Paper models Sculptor dSph with distinct red and blue horizontal-branch stellar populations plus a dark matter halo, finding three-component models fit spatial and velocity-dispersion data well.

quotes.quote: We model two populations in the Sculptor dSph, with each population described by the DF ( for:df1 ), and with a separate component describing a dark matter halo DF ( for:dmdf ). The two populations are the stars on the blue (red) horizontal branch BHB (RHB), which are less (more) metal rich and more (less) extended spatially.

It is clear that both three-component models provide excellent fits to the data, and that also the models predictions on the line-of-sight velocity dispersion profiles provide an excellent description of the data. Table tab:Sculp gives the models' parameters. These simple test cases prove that the extension of the DF ( for:df1 ) to the whole system of classical dSphs is possible and promising, whether the galaxy is represented as a single stellar population or in more sophisticated model that reflects the chemodynamic history of the system.

sources.summary: The paper uses abundances of 98 Sculptor stars to trace neutron-capture enrichment, finding AGB-star influence with increasing metallicity and concluding both s and i processes shaped the dwarf spheroidal galaxy's heavy elements.

quotes.quote: To study the build-up of the s and i products at low metallicity, we investigate the abundances of Y, Ba, La, Nd, and Eu in 98 stars, at 2.4< [ Fe/H ] <-0.9 , in the Sculptor dwarf spheroidal galaxy. The chemical enrichment from AGB stars becomes apparent at [ Fe/H ] 2 in Sculptor, and causes [ Y/Ba ] , [ La/Ba ] , [ Nd/Ba ] and [ Eu/Ba ] to decrease with metallicity, reaching subsolar values at the highest [ Fe/H ] 1 .

Thus, we conclude that both the s and the i process were important sources for the chemical enrichment in the Sculptor dSph galaxy . However, if the i process in AGB stars also created significant amounts of Eu in Sculptor, an increase of [ Eu/Mg ] with [ Fe/H ] would be expected, which is not observed in the currently available data (see Paper I). To fully quantify how much the i process contributed to the overall build-up of the n capture elements in Sculptor, a complete set of i process yields are needed, both for super AGB stars Doherty15,Doherty17,Jones16 as well as low-mass metal-poor stars Campbell10,Cristallo16 .

sources.summary: The paper finds Sculptor's J factor broadly consistent with earlier studies, with low contamination and distance uncertainty dominating the precision of its dark-matter annihilation estimate.

quotes.quote: Sculptor The value of the J factor for Sculptor dSph is not sizeably different from each other. This is because the contamination rate of the Sculptor is quite small as mentioned in sec:common_features , and the P_M cut procedure works very well. fig:Jresults also shows that the error bar of Geringer-Sameth2015 is smaller than ours, which is partially because they imposed additional kinematical and cosmological constraints on DM profiles, namely, the criteria on truncation radius and central density. It is also worth notifying that the J factor of Sculptor is significantly dependent on its distance to the solar system due to the low contamination uncertainty, as mentioned in sec:common_features . This strong correlation suggests that the major uncertainty of the J factor is from the uncertainty of the distance D , and it means that further study of the distance determination is required to obtain a more precise J factor for the Sculptor dSphs.

sources.summary: The paper uses Sculptor as a case where chemo-dynamically distinct stellar populations have led to conflicting Jeans, mass-estimator, and distribution-function conclusions about cored versus cuspy dark matter.

quotes.quote: One specific example is the case of Sculptor, one of the more extensively analyzed dwarf galaxies in the mass modeling literature. Sculptor has been observed to have two chemo-dynamically distinct subpopulations of stars with different half-light radii, which can be leveraged to constrain the DM density at two different radii. applied separate Jeans analyses to the two stellar components and found that either a cored halo or an NFW halo were statistically consistent with their data. applied a mass estimator to the data for the two components and concluded that their analysis ruled out an NFW profile at 99 significance. used a separable distribution function method and found strong statistical preference for a cored DM profile, while found that with a more flexible distribution function model, the statistical preference went away and the data was consistent with an NFW halo.

sources.summary: The paper treats Sculptor as a Local Group dwarf with uncertain offset HI clouds, adopts an HI limit at its stellar position, and says associated gas would make it an inner-halo outlier.

quotes.quote: The only exceptions to adopting the M12 detections are Sculptor and Fornax. We do not include them because the HI clouds detected are not centered on the optical component of the galaxies and there is abundant nearby HI that makes the association highly uncertain. Instead, we adopt HI limits at the stellar position of these galaxies. Sculptor formed the vast majority of its stars in the distant past and is in a region where there are multiple HVCs; therefore we think it is unlikely the nearby gas is associated.
We note that for Sculptor and Fornax, if the offset HI gas was associated (see sect:data ), they would be clear outliers in being small dwarf galaxies within 150 kpc of the MW that have HI gas.

sources.summary: The paper presents Sculptor as a dwarf spheroidal in the Araucaria NIR data collection, covered by 2MASS/DENIS but previously lacking homogeneous deep NIR photometry.

quotes.quote: This paper includes part I, and presents the data collection for the dwarf spheroidal galaxies Carina, Fornax and Sculptor. While these galaxies are devoid of Classical Cepheids, they do host populations of other standard candles, like RRL and TRGB stars, which have proved useful in precise and reliable distance determinations pietrzynski03,pietrzynski08,pietrzynski09,gorski11,karczmarek15,karczmarek17 . All three galaxies were scanned by the Two Micron All-Sky Survey 2MASS; TWOMASSdb03,TWOMASS06 , and only two of them -- Carina and Sculptor -- by the Deep Near-Infrared Survey of the Southern Sky DENIS; DENIS98,DENISdb05 . Deep and high quality photometry was obtained by the VISTA Hemisphere Survey VHS; VHS13,VHSdb19 for Carina, but not for Fornax nor Sculptor. While the detailed comparison of these surveys and our database can be found in Sec. sec:comparisonsurveys , it is apparent that homogeneous and cohesive deep NIR photometry for all three galaxies has not been available in the literature.

sources.summary: The paper uses Gaia EDR3 to identify outer candidate member stars around Sculptor, reporting two radial-velocity matches and one RR Lyrae candidate beyond the limiting radius and possible extended halos or tidal debris.

quotes.quote: We use Gaia EDR3 data to identify stars associated with six classical dwarf spheroidals (Draco, Ursa Minor, Sextans, Sculptor, Fornax, Carina) at their outermost radii, beyond their nominal King stellar limiting radius. For all of the dSphs examined, we find radial velocity matches with stars residing beyond the King limiting radius and with > 50 astrometric probability (four in Draco, two in Ursa Minor, eight in Sextans, two in Sculptor, twelve in Fornax, and five in Carina), indicating that these stars are associated with their respective dwarf spheroidals (dSphs) at high probability. We compare the positions of our candidate ``extra-tidal'' stars with the orbital tracks of the galaxies, and identify stars, both with and without radial velocity matches, that are consistent with lying along the orbital track of the satellites. Cross matching with publicly available catalogs of RR Lyrae, we find one RR Lyrae candidate with > 50 astrometric probability outside the limiting radius in each of Sculptor and Fornax, two such candidates in Draco, nine in Ursa Minor, seven in Sextans, and zero in Carina. Follow-up spectra on all of our candidates, including possible metallicity information, will help confirm association with their respective dSphs, and could represent evidence for extended stellar halos or tidal debris around these classical dSphs.

sources.summary: The review discusses Sculptor mainly through dark-matter constraints, noting simulation consistency for some subhalos and fuzzy-dark-matter models that fit or fail its observed mass-profile slope.

quotes.quote: Only five subhalos from the GIZMO cosmological simulation are consistent with Ursa Minor, Draco, Sculptor, Leo I, and Leo II. As a result, the other DM subhalos are too dense.

For m_ 22 = 0.1, the predicted halo mass of the dwarf galaxies is too high given their dynamical state in the galaxy, and higher m_ 22 does not agree with the inferred slopes of Sculptor and Fornax. Low mass axions ( m_ 22 = 0.1) can explain the observed mass profile slopes in Sculptor and Fornax 2017MNRAS.472.1346G,2015MNRAS.451.2479M,2014PhRvL.113z1302S . However, at such low masses, the predicted halo masses of the ultra-faint dwarf galaxies such as Segue I are ruled out by dynamical friction arguments. In contrast, high mass axions ( m_ 22 = 10) can explain the halo masses of the ultra-faint dwarf galaxies such as Draco II, Triangulum II, and Segue I. For this axion mass, the predicted mass profiles do not agree with the observed slope of Fornax and Sculptor.

sources.summary: The paper includes Sculptor among dispersion-dominated dwarf spheroidals and finds fractional gravity is preferred, with nearly isotropic orbits in the fit.

quotes.quote: For dispersion-dominated systems, we consider 7 Milky-Way dwSph for which high quality determination of the spatially-resolved dispersion velocity has been obtained via stellar tracers ( ; see also data collection by and references therein) and for which tidal effects are not appreciably influencing the inner kinematics: Carina, Leo I, Leo II, Sculptor, Draco, Sextans, and Fornax.

In particular, for Carina, Leo I, Sculptor, and Sextans there is a clear preference for FG both in terms of _r^2 and in terms of the BIC. In such cases, the fractional index takes on typical values s 1.2 , the stellar mass-to-light ratios M_ /L are appreciably larger than for the Newtonian case and more in line with the prior from stellar population synthesis models, and the DM masses M_ 200 are substantially larger of factors several with respect to the Newtonian fits.

As it can be seen in the top panel of Figure fig|Dragonfly44 for Sculptor and DragonFly 44, the most relevant degeneracy between the fitting parameters involves s and the _ sym , in such a way that FG models with larger s tend to be more isotropic. In fact, for Sculptor and DragonFly 44 the FG fit shows preference for almost isotropic orbits, while the Newtonian fit favors tangentially-dominated motions.

sources.summary: The review highlights Sculptor dSph giants in the DART/FLAMES sample, where Ca/Fe trends are detectable and the most metal-poor Sculptor stars sit below the halo Ca/Fe median.

quotes.quote: As an example, Figure 1 shows preliminary results from the Dwarf Abundance and Radial velocity Team (DART) ESO large program; using UVES FLAMES + GIRAFFE on a sample of Sculptor dSph giants, Hill (private communication, 2005) collected nearly 100 stellar spectra, a sample larger than all of the literature high resolution dSph surveys combined. This survey will be able to show subtle declines and trends that the other surveys could never detect. The decline seen in [ Ca/Fe ] with increasing [ Fe/H ] reported by Shetrone et al. (2003) and Geisler et al. (2004) are easily detected. The spread in [ Ca/Fe ] at a given metallicity is being investigated by DART.
This can also be seen in Figure 1 which shows that the most metal-poor Sculptor stars have [ Ca/Fe ] ratios less than 0.2 dex while the halo median at this metallicity is roughly 0.15 dex larger.

sources.summary: The paper measures RGB-star metallicities for Sculptor as one of four nearby dSph galaxies, finding no stars below -3 dex and a metal-poor tail differing from the Galactic halo.

quotes.quote: As part of the Dwarf galaxies Abundances and Radial-velocities Team (DART) Programme, we have measured the metallicities of a large sample of stars in four nearby dwarf spheroidal galaxies (dSph): Sculptor, Sextans, Fornax and Carina. The low mean metal abundances and the presence of very old stellar populations in these galaxies have supported the view that they are fossils from the early Universe. However, contrary to naive expectations, we find a significant lack of stars with metallicities below 3 dex in all four systems. This suggests that the gas that made up the stars in these systems had been uniformly enriched prior to their formation. Furthermore, the metal-poor tail of the dSph metallicity distribution is significantly different from that of the Galactic halo.

sources.summary: Sculptor is treated as a mostly old dSph with two ancient metallicity/kinematic components, a metallicity gradient, star formation stopping around 7 Gyr ago, and persistent metallicity-method discrepancies.

quotes.quote: Such discrepancies persist even in the case of the purely old Sculptor dSph, where one would na ively expect a very good match when comparing with medium or low resolution metallicity measurements.

More specifically, in the case of Sculptor and Sextans the dominant population is of an old age (e.g., Hurley--Keller, Mateo Grebel , Monkiewicz, Mould, Gallagher et al. for Sculptor; Lee et al. for Sextans). Sculptor shows two distinct old stellar components in terms of metallicity and kinematics (e.g., Tolstoy et al. ), as well as a metallicity gradient (Harbeck et al. ). De Boer et al. ( ) suggest that Sculptor stopped forming stars 7 Gyr ago.

sources.summary: The paper models the Sculptor dSph's two stellar populations with Michie-King distribution functions, deriving its mass profile from 0.2 to 1.2 kpc and finding cored halos preferred over NFW cusps.

quotes.quote: Specialising to the case of the Sculptor dSph, we develop a technique to fit simultaneously both populations with Michie-King DFs. This enables us to determine the mass profile of the Sculptor dSph with unprecedented accuracy in the radial range 0.2< < 1.2 kpc. We show that cored halo models are preferred over cusped halo models, with a likelihood ratio test rejecting NFW models at any significance level higher than 0.05 . Even more worryingly, the best-fit NFW models require concentrations with 20 , which is not in the cosmologically preferred range for dwarf galaxies.

sources.summary: The paper treats Sculptor as the Milky Way satellite whose counter-rotating orbit may support a tidal-dwarf origin for the Disc of Satellites.

quotes.quote: The common direction of orbit around the Milky Way, found for most satellite galaxies with measured proper motions, can be considered to be an indication for this scenario. The only exceptions are Sagittarius and Sculptor. While Sagittarius is near to the Milky Way disc and thus may have suffered precession or even a scattering encounter with another satellite zhao98 and consequently can be expected to be far from the direction of the initial orbit, the Sculptor dwarf spheroidal galaxy is another case. The direction of Sculptor's orbitel angular momentum vector indicates that it indeed orbits within the DoS, but on a counter-rotating orbit. While this might, on the first glance, seem to pose a problem for the common origin of the DoS galaxies as TDGs, the opposing direction could in fact be another indication for this origin.

sources.summary: The paper's isotropic Jeans analysis finds Sculptor strongly favors a cuspy dark-matter profile, driven by its steeply rising central line-of-sight velocity dispersion.

quotes.quote: Interestingly, even in this limit, not all of the dSphs prefer large constant-density cores: the Sculptor dSph prefers a cusp while Carina, Draco and Leo I prefer cores.

The most interesting case is Sculptor (solid blue), which shows a strong preference for living in a cuspy dark matter halo with 1 . Sculptor's line-of-sight velocity dispersion profile rises more rapidly in its center than any of the other well-studied MW dSphs, which drives the data to prefer cuspy profiles when isotropy is assumed.
Sculptor strongly favors a cuspy inner profile ( 1 ), while Fornax and Sextans prefer mild cusps ( 0.7 ), albeit with large uncertainties.

sources.summary: The paper uses Sculptor dSph as a comparison dwarf satellite, saying its [Mg/Fe] decreases to sub-solar values at higher metallicity and that slow enrichment follows from low star formation and initial gas mass.

quotes.quote: For example, the Sculptor dSph shows a near solar average value of the [ Mg/Fe ] ratio at [ Fe/H ] 1.5 tolstoy09 , while the inner and the outer halo stars at this metallicity show the average [ Mg/Fe ] of 0.1-0.2 (Table tab:mean_dev ). The difference becomes more substantial at higher metallicity, where the [ Mg/Fe ] decreases with metallicity in Sculptor, reaching the sub-solar mean [ Mg/Fe ] ratio at [ Fe/H ] >-1.0 .

In the case of Sculptor, kirby11 show that the [ /Fe ] [ Fe/H ] relation for their medium resolution spectroscopic sample is well reproduced by a model with a low star formation rate, low initial gas mass and the 1 Gyr duration of star formation which may have started more than 10 Gyr ago. This scenario suggests that the chemical enrichment of the system proceeds slowly so that the overall metallicity of the system remained low when Type Ia SNe start to enrich the system with Fe tolstoy09 .

sources.summary: Sculptor is modeled near the external-field-dominated regime; its inferred mass-to-light profile and cored PDM density are sensitive to stellar mass-to-light ratio and luminosity.

quotes.quote: Sculptor Sculptor's dynamics is on the verge to being in the external field-dominated regime. It is external field-dominated if M_*/L = 1 / , but it is clearly not if M_*/L = 5 / (see Fig.  fig:sculptor , and also the discussion of model parameters in Section  sect:discussvariables based on Sculptor). Fig.  fig:sculptor illustrates that M_ dyn /L 9.5 M_*/L if M_*/L 2 . For larger stellar mass-to-light ratios, M_*/L > 2 , we find that the EF becomes less dominant and the dynamical mass-to-light ratio becomes radius-dependant, most prominently at the core radius r_ king .

sources.summary: The paper studies carbon abundances in Sculptor, reporting its first CEMP star and first nitrogen measurement while finding its CEMP-no fraction lower than the Galactic halo.

quotes.quote: Here we present detailed abundances from ESO VLT/UVES high-resolution spectroscopy for ET0097, the first CEMP star found in the Sculptor dwarf spheroidal, which is one of the best studied dwarf galaxies in the Local Group. This star has [ Fe/H ] =-2.03 0.10 , [ C/Fe ] =0.51 0.10 and [ N/Fe ] =1.18 0.20 , which is the first nitrogen measurement in this galaxy.

In addition to the detailed abundances for ET0097, we present estimates and upper limits for C abundances in 85 other stars in Sculptor derived from CN molecular lines, including 11 stars with [ Fe/H ] 2 . Combining these limits with observations from the literature, the fraction of CEMP-no stars in Sculptor seems to be significantly lower than in the Galactic halo.

sources.summary: The paper discusses Sculptor's extremely metal-poor stars and CEMP-no fraction, suggesting faint supernova frequency or shallow potential wells may shape its early stellar populations.

quotes.quote: To date, the most metal-poor star known in external galaxies is found in Sculptor and has a metallicity near --4.0 TAF10 ; two more stars of similar metallicity are known in Sculptor anna10,Simon15 and six more below --3.0 Starkenburg13,Simon15 .
