Other local galaxies with at least one such star are Draco Shetrone2001,Fulbright2004,CH09 , Sagittarius and Monaco et al. in preparation Sgr04,Sgr06,Sbordone15 , Fornax TAF10 , Ursa Minor KC12,ural , Sextans Aoki09,TAF10 , Coma Ber anna10b , Ursa Maior II anna10b , Segue 1 RK14,anna14 , Hercules K08,Aden , Boo I Gil13,Ishi14 , and Boo II KR14 . Particularly relevant to the present discussion is the discovery of a CEMP-no star in the Sculptor dwarf spheroidal SK15 . The conclusion of the authors is that the fraction of CEMP-no stars in Sculptor seems to be significantly lower than in the Galactic Halo. In our scenario this could be explained either with a lower frequency of faint SNe in Sculptor, or with shallower potential wells that can allow the formation of the second generation of stars.

sources.summary: The paper uses the Sculptor dwarf spheroidal as a worked target for measuring dark-matter halo asphericity, adopting an NFW profile and forecasting Gaia/TMT constraints from member-star astrometry.

quotes.quote: In the rest of this letter, we will determine the confidence with which Gaia space telescope and Thirty Meter Telescope (TMT) observations, using the instrument IRIS, can, using the quantity defined in Eq. ( jean2d ), distinguish a spherical dark matter halo inhabited by a galaxy similar to the Sculptor dwarf spheroidal from a triaxial dark matter halo with axis ratios of the order seen in CDM simulations. For simplicity, we will assume that the total matter density follows an NFW profile (R)= _0 R_0^2 R(R+R_0)^2 where r_0=0.5 kpc and _0=8 10^7 \ M_ / kpc ^3 corresponding to the best fit values for the Sculptor dwarf in strig2006 .

Although TMT can offer precise astrometry for well over 10^4 stars in the Sculptor dwarf tmtdsc , we will restrict our attention to the 1355 stars in the MMFS survey mmfs for which the authors have assigned a membership probability greater than 90 . The uncertainties with which Gaia can determine proper motions are determined using the post-launch precision study in Ref. gaia2015 with a 15 improvement due to Sculptor's favorable location with respect to Gaia's observation pattern.

sources.summary: The paper uses Sculptor as a high-stellar-mass dSph case, noting detected LMXBs, sharply peaked MSP flux predictions, and MSP emission below LAT sensitivity.

quotes.quote: Thus far, 2005MNRAS.364L..61M detected five LMXBs in a deep Chandra survey of the Sculptor dSph, implying that MSPs may also be present. As shown in Figures fig:pois and fig:survival , the spread in cumulative MSP flux for a low stellar mass dSph, such as Segue 1, spans several order of magnitude, whereas high stellar mass dSphs, such as Fornax and Sculptor, have sharply peaked flux PDFs. For dSphs with M_ * > 10^ 4 M_ , the contribution from MSPs within the dSph is predicted to exceed the foreground in most cases (e.g., Fornax, Sculptor, Draco).
We estimate that MSP emission within the highest stellar mass dSphs, Fornax and Sculptor, is a factor 10 below the current LAT sensitivity threshold (Figure fig:sum_fig ).

sources.summary: The paper reviews claims that Sculptor's two metallicity subpopulations imply a constant-density core and statistically reject an NFW cusp, then tests that inference against simulated dwarf galaxies.

quotes.quote: The existence of two kinematically and chemically distinct stellar subpopulations in the Sculptor and Fornax dwarf galaxies offers the opportunity to constrain the density profile of their matter haloes by measuring the mass contained within the well-separated half-light radii of the two metallicity subpopulations. Walker and Pe n arrubia have used this approach to argue that data for these galaxies are consistent with constant-density `cores' in their inner regions and rule out `cuspy' Navarro--Frenk--White (NFW) profiles with high statistical significance, particularly in the case of Sculptor.
walk used a likelihood method to separate samples of stars in Sculptor and Fornax into two metallicity subcomponents and applied these mass estimators to each of them.
walk derived values of for Sculptor and Fornax which exclude an NFW cusp at 99 and 96 per cent confidence levels, respectively, instead favouring a core.

sources.summary: The paper includes Sculptor in its axisymmetric Jeans analysis of classical dwarf spheroidals, with soliton-plus-NFW parameters broadly consistent with soliton-only modeling.

quotes.quote: More precisely, we solve axisymmetric Jeans equations and apply them to the kinematic data of the eight classical dSphs: Draco, Ursa Minor, Carina, Sextans, Leo I, Leo II, Sculptor and Fornax in order to investigate the global shape of ULA dark halo as well as to constrain on particle mass of ULA dark matter.

The Galactic Dwarf Spheroidal Galaxy Data In this work, we adopt the photometric and spectroscopic data of the resolved member stars in the eight luminous dSphs in the Milky Way: Draco, Ursa Minor, Carina, Sextans, Leo I, Leo II, Sculptor, and Fornax. This is because each have 200 stars with line-of-sight velocities from spectroscopy and well-constrained stellar distributions from photometry, so that these sample volumes might be enough to obtain more stringent limits on the mass range of m_ than those from other fainter dSphs such as ultra faint dwarf galaxies.

From these results, most of the dSphs (Draco, Carina, Sextans, Leo II and Sculptor) have larger r_ than b_ and r_ 90 , and the five parameters (Q,r_c,m_ , _z,i) for these galaxies are roughly consistent with these estimated by the soliton-only models.

sources.summary: The paper models Sculptor abundance evolution, noting Ca/Fe stays flat then declines, Sculptor provides the strongest Si/Fe_Ia upper limit, and its SNe Ia appear more prompt than in Leo I/Fornax.

quotes.quote: For example, [ Ca/Fe ] in Sculptor (central panel in Figure fig:scl ) is flat until Fe/H 2.4 and then declines nearly linearly with increasing [ Fe/H ] . We modeled this behavior as a constant X/Fe _ CC until Fe/H _ Ia followed by a sloped line thereafter.
The most constraining upper limit, provided by Sculptor, is Si/Fe _ Ia < .
Instead, it could be that a different, more delayed class of SN Ia dominates the [ Ni/Fe ] evolution in Leo I and Fornax, whereas the SNe Ia in Sculptor are more prompt.

sources.summary: The paper identifies Sculptor as a satellite with detectable LISA sources but cautions that its old single-burst star-formation history may make the fiducial estimate optimistic.

quotes.quote: We forecast that out of the known satellites, Sagittarius, Fornax, Sculptor, and the Magellanic Clouds can be detected with LISA.

We list stellar masses and distances of known satellites from mcc12 and report the number of LISA detections for our fiducial model in Table table:known_satellites . Of all known satellites, only Sagittarius, Fornax, Sculptor, and the Magellanic Clouds host detectable LISA sources.
On the other hand, Sculptor is perhaps better described by a single star formation event that occurred 12 Gyr ago, and thus our estimates are likely to be too optimistic for its case.

sources.summary: The paper finds Sculptor has a preferred cuspy inner slope but a secondary mild-core solution, and notes conflicting earlier core and cusp inferences for its dark-matter density.

quotes.quote: We apply our model to four Local Group dwarf spheroidal galaxies and find results consistent with those obtained with the Jeans modelling based code GravSphere : the Fornax dSph has a strong indication of possessing a central DM core, Carina and Sextans have cusps (although the latter with large uncertainties), while Sculptor shows a double peaked PDF indicating that a cusp is preferred, but a core can not be ruled out.

Finally, Sculptor peaks at = -1.08 , but it has a wide secondary peak, predicting a 18 probability of having a mild core with = -0.75 . A small core was derived for Sculptor by using kinematical data and a mass-dependent profile fit in brook15a , in agreement with the Walker11 and Agnello12 methods that, employing multiple stellar populations within a galaxy, also predicted a core in such dwarf (see also Zhu16,Breddels13a,Hayashi20 ). Other studies, however, surprisingly predict a cusp for Sculptor after all richardson14 , highlighting the importance of deriving the DM density of this dSphs with several different methods.

sources.summary: The review describes Sculptor dSph as having multiple populations, with early star formation dominant overall and a centrally concentrated, metal-richer population from later central star formation.

quotes.quote: The Sculptor dSph presents a less extreme example of multiple populations deBoerSculptor,Be19,Re22 . It experienced a single SF event limited to the first 1-2 Gyrs after the Big Bang, producing about 70 per cent of its stars. However, it continued to form stars in its central parts after the epoch of reionization, leading to a centrally concentrated, metal-richer population. In the outer parts, the stars are overwhelmingly metal-poor and probably formed during the reionization epoch, or soon after its end.

sources.summary: The paper argues the C98 clouds are physically associated with Sculptor dSph, lie within its optical radius, and remain gravitationally bound as part of its interstellar medium.

quotes.quote: The probability of the C98 Sculptor clouds to be Milky Way features at anomalous velocities (HVCs) superimposed by chance on the Sculptor dSph is estimated to be less than 2 . The third hypothesis assumes that the clouds are physically associated with the Sculptor dSph, and is supported by the following evidences: (a) the radial velocities for both clouds are very close to the optical velocity of the Sculptor dSph ( V=4 3 km s ^ 1 ). (b) 88 of the total flux is contained within the optical radius of the galaxy, and () the clouds are located symmetrically relative to the center of the Sculptor dSph. Arguments are presented that the C98 Sculptor clouds are still gravitationally bound to the dwarf galaxy, and are part of its interstellar medium.

sources.summary: The paper describes Sculptor (Scl) as a nearby dwarf spheroidal whose RGB spectroscopy shows abundance patterns distinct from the Milky Way and two stellar components.

quotes.quote: The DART large programme at ESO made v _ hel and [ Fe/H ] measurements from FLAMES spectroscopy of 401 red giant branch (RGB) stars in the Sculptor (Scl) dSph .

Scl is a close companion of the Milky Way, at a distance of 72 5 kpc , with a low total (dynamical) mass, (1.4 0.6) 10^7 M_ , and modest luminosity, M _V = -10.7 0.5 , and central surface brightness, _ 0,V = 23.5 0.5 mag/arcsec ^2 with no HI gas .

It is clear that the overall distribution of [ /Fe ] versus [ Fe/H ] in Scl does not match our Galaxy, except for a small number of the most metal poor stars in Scl which overlap with Galactic halo stars. Our FLAMES results clearly show that Scl contains two distinct stellar components with different spatial, kinematic and abundance properties .

sources.summary: The paper uses Sculptor as the highest phase-space-density Local Group dwarf, modeling its halo with a King profile and comparing entropy and excess-mass constraints.

quotes.quote: From a compilation of Local group dSph galaxies by Mateo , we infer that the largest value of Q is observed in Sculptor with a core radius of 110 pc and 1-D stellar velocity dispersion of 6.6 km/s. Dalcanton and Hogan show that one may assign a Q value of 2 10^ 4 / ^3/( )^3 to this galaxy. They also assign Q values to seven other local group galaxies with measured stellar velocity dispersions between 6 and 11 km/s and M_v > -14 .

A comparison with the King profile entropy for Sculptor galaxy yields the result that in order for the entropy to not decrease we must have Q_ prim > 10^ 5 / ^3/( )^3 , weaker than the constraint obtained from the excess mass function analysis.

sources.summary: The paper tunes simulations to the Sculptor dSph, noting its low-metallicity tail, simple old star-formation history, high mass estimates, and comparison of observed metallicity distributions to model outputs.

quotes.quote: Sculptor Our simulations were aimed at reproducing the chemical properties of the Sculptor dSph (hereafter, Scl), rather than those of the full sample of Helmi (2006). The reason for this choice is that the low metallicity tail of Scl extends to slightly lower metallicities than those of the other three dSphs (a fact which will strengthen our conclusions). Furthermore, the SF history of Scl appears to have lasted only a few Gyr, and after this initial period it appears to have stopped, as no stellar population younger than about 10 Gyr has been detected: such a simple history should be relatively easy to reproduce. The total mass of Scl was quoted to amount to a few 10^7 (Queloz 1995), but more recent estimates (Battaglia 2006) have put it at a much higher value ( 5 10^8 ).

sources.summary: The paper uses Sculptor as an observational constraint on tidal scenarios, noting its 5-15 Gyr stars, estimated proper motion, lower observed mass-to-light ratio, and larger stellar mass than UMi.

quotes.quote: The dSph satellite Sculptor has stars with ages in the range 5-15 Gyr (van den Bergh 1994), and the proper motion has been estimated by Schweitzer et al. (1995). Both satellites are at a distance of 60-70 kpc. It is interesting to note that UMi has (M/L)_ obs =95 43 , while Scl has (M/L)_ obs =10.9 7.5 (Irwin Hatzidimitriou 1995).

The present masses of the stellar components (assuming (M/L)_ true =3 ) of UMi and Scl are, respectively, about 6 10^5 M_ and 4 10^6 M_ (Irwin Hatzidimitriou 1995), which may be too low to accrete gas from a hypothetical gas cloud on a similar orbit (.f. Magellanic or similar stream), or let alone retain gas over time-spans of many Gyr.

sources.summary: The paper ranks Sculptor as a well-constrained, promising classical dSph and the favored southern-sky target, with J-factor reconstruction robust against different stellar binnings.

quotes.quote: (On the other hand, Fornax and Sculptor are found to provide robust results against different binnings.) Second, we note that the analysis presented here uses a fixed profile for the light distribution which, when combined with our assumption of constant velocity anisotropy, restricts the possible halo profiles we can recover.

Our analysis places Ursa Minor as the best candidate for the northern sky (marginally better than Draco, which has long been a favourite target of northern hemisphere observatories) and Sculptor for the southern sky, when it comes to a favourable median and low uncertainty in the J factor.

With these criteria in mind, we find three categories: well-constrained and promising (Ursa Minor, Sculptor and Draco), well-constrained but less promising (Carina, Fornax and Leo I), and poorly constrained (Sextans and Leo II).

sources.summary: The paper traces Sculptor orbits under Milky Way potentials and finds filamentary infall would require unusually low proper motions and a very massive dark halo.

quotes.quote: Here we test this scenario by making use of the proper motions of the Fornax, Sculptor, Ursa Minor and Carina dwarf spheroidals, and trace their orbits back through several variations of the Milky Way's potential and account for dynamical friction.

In the case of Sculptor, to have fallen in from far outside the virial radius, using the most favourable proper motion at 3 below the mean, the dSph would need to weigh more than 10^ 10 for Models I and II and at 1 it is barely possible regardless of dSph mass.

Moreover, the only scenario for Sculptor, Ursa Minor and Carina to be consistent with the infall hypothesis is for their proper motions to both be significantly lower than the mean, and for them to have total masses of more than 10^ 10 .

sources.summary: The paper discusses the Sculptor dwarf galaxy as a counter-orbiting Milky Way satellite within the disk of satellites, using it to test models of satellite orbital-pole alignment.

quotes.quote: In addition, the Sculptor dwarf galaxy is counter-orbiting, but also within the DoS.

The models therefore not only produce orbits similar to the majority of those described by the satellite galaxy orbital poles, but can also account for the counter-orbiting direction of the Sculptor dwarf galaxy.

Note also that the analysis does not account for the alignment of the orbital poles with the DoS normal, nor does it factor in that the Sculptor satellite galaxy is counter-orbiting with respect to the six best-aligned orbital poles, but also orbiting in the DoS.

sources.summary: The paper applies projected virial arguments to Sculptor's multiple stellar populations, rejecting any NFW halo and requiring a cored dark halo of about 120 pc even with flattening or self-gravity.

quotes.quote: We apply our methods to the Sculptor dwarf spheroidal, and show that there is no NFW halo compatible with the energetics of the two populations. The dark halo must possess a core radius of 120 pc for the virial solutions for the two populations to be consistent. This conclusion remains true, even if the effects of flattening or self-gravity of the stellar populations are included.

sources.summary: The paper cites Sculptor as a dwarf spheroidal with modeling and multiple-population evidence favoring cored dark-matter profiles, while noting the simulations do not resolve its innermost region.

quotes.quote: Schwarzchild modeling avoids some of the assumptions inherent in Jeans modeling, and has been applied to Fornax and Sculptor, with the conclusion that both have cored DM density profiles Jardel2012, Jardel2013, Jardel2013b, Breddels2013a .

Studies using this method Walker2011, Amorisco2012, Battaglia2008, Agnello2012 conclude that both Fornax (with slope measured out to 1 kpc) and Sculptor (measured interior to 500 pc) favor cored density profiles, and exclude cuspy density profiles at high significance.

The presence of cored density profiles at radii less than 500 pc in satellites fainter than M_V 12 as seen in Sculptor by Walker2011 is not ruled out by our simulations, as we do not resolve this region.

sources.summary: The paper reports H.E.S.S. observations of Sculptor as a dwarf spheroidal dark-matter target, finding no gamma-ray signal and deriving annihilation cross-section limits dependent on the halo model.

quotes.quote: The Sculptor and Carina dwarf spheroidal galaxies were observed with H.E.S.S. for 11.8 and 14.8 hours, respectively Viana 1036 . No gamma-ray signal was detected at the nominal positions of these galaxies above 220 GeV and 320 GeV, respectively. Constraints on the dark matter velocity-weighted annihilation cross section for both, the Sculptor and Carina dwarf galaxies range from v 10^ 21 down to 10^ 22 cm ^3 s ^ 1 , depending on the dark-matter halo model.

sources.summary: The paper finds Sculptor dSph has a binary fraction consistent with Milky Way field binaries and compatible with Milky-Way-like binary population parameters.

quotes.quote: We find that the Fornax, Sculptor, and Sextans dSphs are consistent with having binary populations similar to that of Milky Way field binaries to within 68 confidence limits, whereas the Carina dSph is remarkably deficient in binaries with periods less than 10 years.
By this standard, Fornax, Sculptor, and Sextans all have binary fractions consistent with Milky Way field binaries to within 68 confidence limits.

This set of parameters lies squarely within the allowed parameter region for the Fornax, Sculptor, and Sextans dSphs, indicating that a Milky-Way like binary population is compatible with the data in those galaxies.

sources.summary: Sculptor was a classical dSph target; its larger size made profile-shape dependence stronger, and source-subtraction issues in its FoV could shift MAP1/MAP2 bounds substantially.

quotes.quote: The targets included three classical dSphs (CDS), Carina, Fornax, and Sculptor, and three ultra-faint dSphs (UDS), BootesII, Hercules and Segue2, for a total of 123 hours of observing time.

Fornax and Sculptor, due to their larger size, have instead a larger area involved in the fit which makes the dependence on the shape stronger than in the Hercules case (which, for the diffusion scheme and type of maps adopted in this plot, nearly resembles a point-source).

As already noticed in Paper II, if the source subtraction in the visibility plane was not completely successful (as especially for the Fornax and Sculptor FoVs), the bounds that can be derived from MAP1 and MAP2 can significantly differ, up to two orders of magnitude.

sources.summary: Sculptor is modeled as a Milky Way dSph; the paper maps its dark halo parameters and places it among lower-M/L systems with less flattened halos.

quotes.quote: In this section, we describe the best-fit dark halo model for each of seven MW dSphs (Carina, Fornax, Sculptor, Sextans, Draco, Leo I, and Leo II) and five M31 dSphs (And I, And II, And III, And V, and And VII) and present confidence maps between each parameter.

In Figure  fig:fig2 and  fig:fig3 , we present 68  ( 1 ), 95  , and 99  confidence levels of contours in the two-dimensional plane of Q- _z , _0- _z , _z , b_ halo _z , and i- _z for all sample dSphs: Carina, Fornax, Sculptor, Sextans, Draco, and Leo I are shown in Figure  fig:fig2 , and Leo II, And I, And II, And III, And V, and And VII are shown in Figure  fig:fig3 .

In light of these predictions, it is interesting to remark that the dark halos of Fornax, Sculptor, Leo I, and Leo II with relatively low M/L s tend to be less flattened than Carina, Sextans, and Draco with high M/L s.

sources.summary: The paper includes Sculptor in the classical dSph kinematic analysis and finds that Sculptor has especially small J-factor uncertainties due to abundant kinematic data.

quotes.quote: For the classicals, we utilize stellar-kinematic data published by Waletal2015 for Draco, by Matetal2008 for Leo I, by Kocetal2007 for Leo II, and by Waletal2009a,Waletal2009b for Carina, Fornax, Sculptor, and Sextans.
For instance, the member stars for Carina, Fornax, Sculptor and Sextans are identified by the expectation-maximization algorithm described in Waletal2009b .

It is found from Table table3 and this figure that among the classical dSphs, Draco has the largest J factor value, while Fornax and Sculptor have the smallest uncertainties of J owing to an abundance of kinematic data.

sources.summary: The paper treats Sculptor as a classical dwarf spheroidal whose VMP stars show alpha enhancement, Sr/Mg behavior, and especially strong evidence for early inhomogeneous mixing.

quotes.quote: We show that once the NLTE effects are properly taken into account, all massive galaxies in our sample, that is, the MW halo and the classical dSphs Sculptor, Ursa Minor, Sextans, and Fornax, reveal a similar plateau at [ /Fe ] 0.3 for each of the process elements: Mg, Ca, and Ti.
Sculptor remains the classical dSph, in which the evidence for inhomogeneous mixing in the early evolution stage, at [ Fe/H ] < -2 , is the strongest.

For Sr/Mg, there are two clear sequences at [ Mg/H ] 2.6 , one scattering around [ Mg/H ] 0.37 , which gathers the MW halo stars and the most massive (classical) dwarfs, such as Sextans, Ursa Minor and Sculptor, while the UFDs Leo IV, UMa II, and Bo otes I keep remarkably consistent and low abundance ratios, at the level of [ Sr/Mg ] 1.3 .

sources.summary: The paper describes Sculptor as a classical dwarf spheroidal with a starburst-like history, a flat velocity-dispersion profile, and observed profile bumps considered by the simulations.

quotes.quote: They show that the SFH of classical dSphs range from intense star bursts, where almost all stars of the galaxies were formed at the same time, as Sculptor for example, until galaxies with a constant star formation rate like Fornax or Leo I.
In Sextans we see a slight drop in velocity dispersion around 1 kpc, while Sculptor, Draco and Fornax show flat profiles their figure 2 Walker2007 .
In reality we see similar bumps in Carina, Leo I and Sculptor Walker2009 .

sources.summary: The paper reports Sculptor abundance behavior in chromium, zinc, yttrium, and neutron-capture elements, including detectable barium and strontium to very low metallicity.

quotes.quote: Applying the corrections as illustrated in Fig. fig:nlte_corr_cr , we computed abundances that are slightly supersolar with average values of [ Cr/Fe ] _ NLTE = 0.15 in Fornax, Sagittarius and Sculptor, and [ Cr/Fe ] _ NLTE = 0.0 in Sextans (Fig. fig:nlte_mg_cr_mn ).
We therefore measured zinc mainly for Sculptor and Sagittarius.

We find detectable barium and strontium abundances down to metallicities of [ Fe/H ] 4 in Sculptor cf., Kirby2009, Frebel2010a , and [ Fe/H ] 3 for nearly all investigated dSph galaxies independent of their mass (Fig. fig:ba_eu ).

sources.summary: The paper finds Sculptor has comparable internal and external fields, disturbed phantom dark matter with cancellation spikes, and prominent two-peak behavior in the line-of-sight density profile.

quotes.quote: For Sculptor and Fornax, where the internal and external gravitational fields are comparable, the phantom dark matter distribution appears disturbed with spikes at the locations where the two fields cancel each other; these features have little connection with the distribution of the stars within the dwarfs.
For the two remaining dwarf galaxies, Sculptor and Fornax, the internal and external fields are comparable.

Along the line of sight between the Milky Way centre and the dwarf, Sculptor and Fornax (and to a lesser extent Leo II) exhibit an additional effect, namely the presence of two peaks in the phantom dark matter density profile for mass-to-light ratio M/L=3 M_ /L_ for Sculptor and M/L=1 M_ /L_ for Fornax and Leo II.

sources.summary: The paper identifies Sculptor as one of the most extended dwarf spheroidals in its dark-matter templates and finds extension weakens annihilation limits at high masses.

quotes.quote: In particular, Sculptor among the classical dSphs and Ursa Major II among the ultra-faint ones show the largest extensions, 0.65 ^ and 0.74 ^ respectively.
For most dSphs, the ratio is between 1.0 and 1.3 at low DM masses, and increases up to 2.0 -- 3.0 for masses between 100 GeV -- 1 TeV, as for e.g. Sculptor, Ursa Minor, Fornax and Ursa Major II.
This is also the case of Sculptor which, at masses of about 1 TeV, is below the 95 containment band.

sources.summary: The paper compares Sculptor with other low-mass satellites, discussing evidence for a low-metallicity abundance knee and limits on confirming its Mg-Fe slope change.

quotes.quote: Indeed, previous studies of stars in Draco, Sextans and Ursa Minor shetrone2003vlt , Sculptor Hill2019 , and Sextans theler2020chemical suggest the presence of a ``knee'' at [ Fe/H ] -2.

Based on a compilation of chemical compositions from various works CohenHuang2009,CohenHuang2010,Starkenburg2013 , quote [ Fe/H ] _ knee 1.9 for Sculptor, Ursa Minor, and Fornax and [ Fe/H ] _ knee 2.5 for Draco and Carina see also deboer2014 .

As for the others, while our data do not rule out the presence of a change of slope in the Mg-Fe relation for Sculptor, Ursa Minor, and Fornax at [ Fe/H ] 2 , they cannot confirm it either, due to increased uncertainties and relatively small sample sizes in the low metallicity end.

sources.summary: The review describes Sculptor as a relatively massive, well-studied dwarf with simple star formation and smooth, continuous process chemical evolution despite debate over source timing.

quotes.quote: Sculptor, Fornax, and Sagittarius are sufficiently massive that they should be dominated by chemical evolution rather than enrichment. Sculptor is one of the best-studied dwarf galaxies with a very simple star formation history and smooth process chemical evolution Duggan2018, Hill2019, Skuladottir2019, Molero2021, delosReyes2022 . There is still debate about the relative importance of prompt vs delayed process sources in Sculptor, but it clearly has reached the point of continuous chemical evolution with [ Eu/Mg ] 0 .

sources.summary: The paper includes Sculptor among its MUSE dwarf-galaxy targets and derives Sculptor's dark-matter distribution and likelihood through its own Jeans analysis.

quotes.quote: More specifically, in addition to Leo T, we consider MUSE observations of the dwarf spheroidal galaxies Sculptor, Eridanus 2, Grus 1, and Hydra II.

For Sculptor, we follow the same procedure but we derive the DM distribution and the relevant likelihood by ourselves. This is accomplished by means of a Jeans analysis, using data from  and employing the same method of  Bas:2021 .

sources.summary: The paper treats Sculptor as a dwarf-spheroidal comparison point, tying its high central density, current satellite distance, and outer H I configuration to tidal-stirring predictions.

quotes.quote: Sextans, Sagittarius and Fornax) , namely 0.01 < _0 < 0.1 M_ /pc^3 (Mateo 1998) The remnant of GR8 is comparable to the densest dSphs, Draco, Sculptor and Ursa Minor, having _0 > 0.1 M_ /pc^3 (Lake 1990).

Orbits of this type would be consistent with the the present distances of some of the Milky way satellites, the LMC, Sculptor, Ursa Minor, Draco and Sagittarius What would be the fate of a fragile LSB satellite on such orbits?

When viewed from within the orbital plane, the streams projected onto the plane of the sky could look like two gas clouds symmetrically distributed around the bound stellar component (Figure 32). The resulting configuration is very reminiscent of that of the outer HI distribution in Sculptor (Carignan et al. 1998).

gmw_00000492.name: BzK 4171
gmw_00000492.overview: BzK 4171 is a massive, high-redshift star-forming galaxy located at a redshift of approximately 1.465 [arXiv:0711.4995, arXiv:1404.7393]. It features a nearly edge-on rotating disk [arXiv:0711.4995] with a large molecular gas reservoir that supports low-efficiency, disk-wide star formation [arXiv:0905.3637, arXiv:1005.4965].

sources.summary: The paper presents BzK 4171 as a normal massive high-redshift star-forming galaxy detected in CO, with a double-horn profile consistent with a rotating, nearly edge-on disk.

quotes.quote: Motivated by this evidence, we observed two representative massive galaxies at high redshift with the IRAM Plateau de Bure Interferometer, searching for the redshifted emission lines from the CO [ 2-1 ] transition at rest frame 230.539 GHz, with the aim of constraining their molecular gas content. The two galaxies, BzK 4171 (J123626.53+620835.3, VLA radio frame) and BzK 21000 (J123710.60+622234.6), at redshift z=1.465 and z=1.522 , respectively (measured from Keck DEIMOS spectroscopy), were selected in the Great Observatories Origins Deep Survey Northern field (GOODS-N) using the BzK technique (Daddi et al 2004b), which provides complete K limited samples of normal, massive galaxies at 1.4<z<2.5 .

For BzK-4171 the 24 m estimate (based on the Chary and Elbaz 2001 luminosity dependent templates) also agrees within this factor, while for BzK-21000 the 24 m estimate of SFR is 2-2.5 larger than the radio and UV ones, mild evidence for possible mid-IR excess usually linked to obscured AGN activity (Daddi et al. 2007b; D07b hereafter). Stellar masses of 0.5--0.8 10^ 11 M_ were derived from optical and UV rest frame photometry, following the method of Daddi et al. (2004b). SFRs and stellar masses are given here for the Chabrier (2003) stellar initial mass function. The two target galaxies lie in the mass-SFR correlation established at their redshifts (D07a), and are thus ordinary massive high- z sources with ongoing star formation.
BzK 4171 is best fitted by a double horn profile, indicative of a rotating disk and consistent with the UV morphology of a nearly edge on disk.

sources.summary: The paper reports VLA CO(1-0) observations of BzK-4171, including its redshifted observing frequency, spectral-window setup, and unresolved detected emission.

quotes.quote: CO(1--0) observations of BzK-4171 and BzK-16000 were obtained in the D, and D plus C array configurations, respectively. D-array observations of both sources were performed during 2010 March and 2010 April (VLA project: AC975). C-array observations of BzK-16000 were done during 2012 April (VLA project 12A-264). At the redshifts z_ CO(2-1) =1.4652 and z_ CO(2-1) =1.5249 Daddi2010a , the CO(1--0) line is shifted to 46.7594 GHz and 45.6537 GHz for BzK-4171 and BzK-16000, respectively. The calibrators and correlator configuration used for the D-array data were the same as those used to observe BzK-21000.

sources.summary: The paper treats BzK-4171 as a z=1.465 massive disk galaxy whose CO luminosity and disk-like morphology imply low-efficiency, disk-wide star formation resembling scaled-up local spirals.

quotes.quote: The CO [ 2-1 ] luminosities derived from IRAM Plateau de Bure Interferometer (PdBI) observations of two sources --- BzK-4171 and BzK-21000 at z=1.465 and z=1.522 respectively --- imply star-formation efficiencies (SFEs) similar to those of local spirals, an order of magnitude lower than those in submillimeter selected sources. The low SFEs suggest that these objects behave as scaled-up versions of local spirals, with long lasting, low-efficiency star-formation. Given the disk-like UV rest-frame morphology of these objects in deep HST+ACS imaging, and the good agreement between the UV and radio/mid-IR star formation rates, D08 suggested that star formation in these galaxies is taking place in disk-wide extended gas reservoirs.

sources.summary: The paper reports CO(1-0) detection in BzK-4171, deriving a large molecular gas mass, a CO brightness-temperature ratio consistent with thermalized gas, and disk-like star-formation efficiency.

quotes.quote: We find H _2 gas masses of 8.3 1.9 10^ 10 \ M_ , 5.6 1.4 10^ 10 \ M_ and 1.23 0.34 10^ 11 \ M_ for BzK-4171, BzK-21000 and BzK-16000, respectively, assuming a conversion _ CO =3.6 \ M_ (K km s ^ 1 pc ^ 2 ) ^ 1 .

We find brightness temperature ratios between the CO 2-1 and CO 1-0 emission lines of 0.80_ 0.22 ^ +0.35 , 1.22_ 0.36 ^ +0.61 and 0.41_ 0.13 ^ +0.23 for BzK-4171, BzK-21000 and BzK-16000, respectively.
In BzK-4171 and BzK-21000 the values of r_ 21 suggest thermal equilibrium, while in BzK-16000, the low value for r_ 21 suggests that the CO emission is sub-thermal at J=2 .

gmw_00000493.name: BzK 21000
gmw_00000493.overview: BzK 21000 is a massive, normal star-forming galaxy at redshift z=1.52 [arXiv:0711.4995, arXiv:1109.1140] that is classified as an ultraluminous infrared galaxy [arXiv:0906.5346]. It features an extended, rotating molecular gas disk [arXiv:0711.4995] with sub-thermally excited CO emission [arXiv:0905.3637, arXiv:1005.4965] and a clumpy gas structure [arXiv:1404.7393].

sources.summary: The paper describes BzK 21000 as a massive high-redshift star-forming galaxy with CO emission, possible mid-infrared excess, velocity shear, and extended rotating-disk-like gas.

quotes.quote: Motivated by this evidence, we observed two representative massive galaxies at high redshift with the IRAM Plateau de Bure Interferometer, searching for the redshifted emission lines from the CO [ 2-1 ] transition at rest frame 230.539 GHz, with the aim of constraining their molecular gas content. The two galaxies, BzK 4171 (J123626.53+620835.3, VLA radio frame) and BzK 21000 (J123710.60+622234.6), at redshift z=1.465 and z=1.522 , respectively (measured from Keck DEIMOS spectroscopy), were selected in the Great Observatories Origins Deep Survey Northern field (GOODS-N) using the BzK technique (Daddi et al 2004b), which provides complete K limited samples of normal, massive galaxies at 1.4<z<2.5 .

For BzK-4171 the 24 m estimate (based on the Chary and Elbaz 2001 luminosity dependent templates) also agrees within this factor, while for BzK-21000 the 24 m estimate of SFR is 2-2.5 larger than the radio and UV ones, mild evidence for possible mid-IR excess usually linked to obscured AGN activity (Daddi et al. 2007b; D07b hereafter). Stellar masses of 0.5--0.8 10^ 11 M_ were derived from optical and UV rest frame photometry, following the method of Daddi et al. (2004b). SFRs and stellar masses are given here for the Chabrier (2003) stellar initial mass function. The two target galaxies lie in the mass-SFR correlation established at their redshifts (D07a), and are thus ordinary massive high- z sources with ongoing star formation.

BzK 21000 shows a narrower velocity profile with only a hint of double-horns. Fig. Pic (bottom panels) shows position-velocity diagram, derived using a pseudo-slit oriented along the major axis defined in the HST+ACS imaging. BzK 4171 is unresolved, while BzK 21000 shows evidence for shear, overall consistent with rotation in a disk. As a cross-check, we separately fitted velocity channels bluer and redder than the systemic velocities. For BzK 4171 the positions of the blue and red horns are separated by 0.5 0.5'' . For BzK 21000 we find 1.3 0.3'' separation ( 11 2.5 kpc; 4.3 ), definying a position angle of 10 17 deg, in very good agreement with the UV position angle, confirming that the CO emission is extended.

sources.summary: The paper finds BzK-21000 has subthermal CO excitation, with CO(3-2) comparable to CO(2-1) and a CO SLED resembling local spirals rather than SMGs or QSOs.

quotes.quote: The CO [ 3-2 ] flux of BzK-21000 at z=1.522 is comparable within the errors to its CO [ 2-1 ] flux, implying that the CO [ 3-2 ] transition is significantly sub-thermally excited.

For BzK-21000 we derive a solid measurement of r_ 32 =0.50 0.12 . The r_ 32 measurement clearly shows that the CO [ 3-2 ] transition is sub-thermalized with respect to CO [ 2-1 ] , a result significant at the >4 level.

For BzK-21000 , the CO [ 3-2 ] and [ 2-1 ] fluxes are comparable within the errors. The CO spectral line energy distribution of BzK-21000 is consistent with the Milky-Way disk measurements by fix99 (Fig. fig:cosed ), and similar to what is observed in local spirals e.g., bra92,you95,mau99,cro07 . The CO SLED of BzK-21000 is in stark contrast to what has been generally seen so far in high-redshift sources like QSOs and SMGs, for which gas has been found to be thermalized up to at least the J=3 transition wei05b,wei07a,wei07b,rie06 .

sources.summary: The paper uses CO observations and LVG modeling of BzK-21000 to confirm a massive molecular reservoir with low-excitation, disk-like gas rather than high-excitation ULIRG or quasar conditions.

quotes.quote: We find H _2 gas masses of 8.3 1.9 10^ 10 \ M_ , 5.6 1.4 10^ 10 \ M_ and 1.23 0.34 10^ 11 \ M_ for BzK-4171, BzK-21000 and BzK-16000, respectively, assuming a conversion _ CO =3.6 \ M_ (K km s ^ 1 pc ^ 2 ) ^ 1 .

We find brightness temperature ratios between the CO 2-1 and CO 1-0 emission lines of 0.80_ 0.22 ^ +0.35 , 1.22_ 0.36 ^ +0.61 and 0.41_ 0.13 ^ +0.23 for BzK-4171, BzK-21000 and BzK-16000, respectively. At the depth of our observations it is not possible to discern between thermodynamic equilibrium or sub-thermal excitation of the molecular gas at J=2 . However, the low temperature ratio found for BzK-16000 suggests sub-thermal excitation of CO already at J=2 . For BzK-21000, a Large Velocity Gradient model of its CO emission confirms previous results of the low-excitation of the molecular gas at J=3 .

We see that all these models can reasonably reproduce the data for BzK-21000. This is expected as our CO 1-0 flux measurement confirms the LVG-based prediction of Dannerbauer2009 , I_ CO \ 1-0 0.15 Jy km s ^ 1 . Therefore, we verify that models with high T_ kin and/or n( H _2) , as seen in ULIRGs or high-redshift QSOs, are unlikely for the gas in this galaxy, as suggested by Dannerbauer2009 .

sources.summary: The paper presents BzK-21000 as a z=1.52 normal star-forming galaxy with high dust mass, spiral-like CO conversion factor, low SFE, and main-sequence star-formation mode.

quotes.quote: We explore the gas-to-dust mass ratio ( ) and the CO luminosity-to- conversion factor ( ) of two well studied galaxies in the GOODS-N field, that are expected to have different star forming modes, the starburst GN20 at z=4.05 and the normal star-forming galaxy BzK-21000 at z=1.52 . Detailed sampling is available for their Rayleigh-Jeans emission via ground based mm interferometry ( 1.1-6.6 mm) along with PACS and SPIRE data that probe the peak of their infrared emission.

Using the physically motivated Draine Li (2007) models, as well as a modified black body function, we measure the dust mass ( ) of the sources and find (2.0^ +0.7 _ 0.6 10^ 9 ) for GN20 and (8.6^ +0.6 _ 0.9 10^ 8 ) for BzK-21000. The addition of mm data reduces the uncertainties of the derived by a factor of 2 , allowing the use of the local vs metallicity relation to place constraints on the values of the two sources.

For GN20 we derive a conversion factor of < 1.0 pc ^ 2 (K km s ^ 1 ) ^ 1 , consistent with that of local ULIRGs, while for BzK-21000 we find a considerably higher value, 4.0 pc ^ 2 (K km s ^ 1 ) ^ 1 , in agreement with an independent kinematic derivation reported previously. The implied star formation efficiency is 25 L _ /M _ for BzK-21000, a factor of 5-10 lower than that of GN20.

sources.summary: The paper identifies RG J123710 as a CO-detected hot-dust ULIRG, probably a modest-efficiency star-forming spiral, whose 70 micron detection confirms its ULIRG nature.

quotes.quote: We note that one of the eight galaxies of our sample, RG J123710, has been detected in CO gas by daddi08a . The presence of a molecular gas reservoir > 10 ^ 10 M _ indicates that high levels of star formation ( > 100 ) can potentially occur. In their interpretation of the detection, Daddi et al. claims that RG J123710 is most likely a spiral galaxy undergoing modest-efficiency star formation lower than most SMG ULIRGs. However, our detection of RG J123710 at 70 \ directly confirms that it is a ULIRG. More CO observations are needed to investigate if the rest of the hot-dust ULIRG population exhibits modest star formation efficiencies or high, SMG-like efficiencies (Casey et al., in preparation).

sources.summary: The paper resolves CO(1-0) emission in BzK-21000, finding clumpy structure and estimating that less than 40 percent of the molecular gas is in giant clumps.

quotes.quote: For one of our targets, BzK-21000, we present spatially resolved CO line maps. At the resolution of 0.18'' (1.5 kpc), most of the emission is resolved out except for some clumpy structure. From this, we attempt to identify molecular gas clumps in the data cube, finding 4 possible candidates. We estimate that <40 of the molecular gas is confined to giant clumps ( 1.5 kpc in size), and thus most of the gas could be distributed in small fainter clouds or in fairly diffuse extended regions of lower brightness temperatures than our sensitivity limit.

sources.summary: BzK-21000 is the only target with detected [OI] 63 micron emission; PDR modeling gives its UV field and gas density, suggesting extended cool neutral gas.

quotes.quote: We detect [ OI ] 63 m line emission in BzK-21000 at z=1.5213 , and measure a line luminosity, L _ OI 63 m = (3.9 0.7) 10^9 L _ . Our PDR modelling of the interstellar medium in BzK-21000 suggests a UV radiation field strength, G 320G_0 , and gas density, n 1800 cm ^ 3 , consistent with previous LVG modelling of the molecular CO line excitation.

We find that the implied luminosity ratio, L _ OI 63 m / L _ IR , of the undetected BzK-selected star-forming galaxies broadly agrees with that of low-redshift star-forming galaxies, while BzK-21000 has a similar ratio to that of a dusty star-forming galaxy at z 6 . The high [ OI ] 63 m line luminosities observed in BzK-21000 and the z 1 -3 dusty and submm luminous star-forming galaxies may be associated with extended reservoirs of low density, cool neutral gas.

gmw_00000494.name: PKS 2201+044
gmw_00000494.overview: PKS 2201+044 is a nearby giant elliptical galaxy [arXiv:1012.3620] classified as a BL Lacertae object [arXiv:1105.5226] and a Seyfert 1 galaxy [arXiv:0806.3220]. It hosts a resolved jet detected from radio to X-ray wavelengths [arXiv:1012.3620, arXiv:1105.5226] and exhibits inter-night photo-polarimetric variability [arXiv:1707.07688].

sources.summary: The paper identifies CGCG 403-019 as a BL Lac/Seyfert object with extended radio structure, treats it as an intermediate-distance extended radiogalaxy, and notes its match to a UHECR.

quotes.quote: For the intermediate and distant redshift bins there is little match between extragalactic radio jets and UHECRs; the only two matches within 3.5 \ are CGCG 403-019, a Seyfert/BL Lac at 112 Mpc, and Mrk 612, a Seyfert galaxy at 85 Mpc. The match between a UHECR and CGCG 403-019 deserveres furter comment. CGCG 403-019 is a BL Lac with a Seyfert 1 spectrum verver93 .

In the 12th AGN Catalog of verver06 it is listed by its alias PKS 2201+04 and is classified as a Seyfert 1. It is a poweful radio source with a core-jet structure with total extent 6 or 190 kpc ulvjoh84,lauet93 . Within the unified scheme of radiogalaxies, BL Lacs are posited to be pole-on FR Is, implying a much larger deprojected radio extent. We therefore consider CGCG 403-019 as an `intermediate' distance `extended' radiogalaxy. Further, CGCG 403-019 is unique in being the nearest BL Lac with extended radio structure.

sources.summary: The paper treats PKS 2201+044 as a BL Lac with a resolved optical jet, observed in near-infrared and compared across radio, optical, NIR, and X-ray bands.

quotes.quote: PKS 0521-365, PKS 2201+044 and 3C 371 are up to now the only three classified BL Lac objects showing resolved optical jets. Near-IR observations (MAD) using an innovative adaptive optics (AO) camera of PKS 0521-365 and PKS 2201+044 were performed to investigate their morphological and photometric properties. We discuss the new observations together with the available data in radio, optical, and X-ray bands. Here, we present our high resolution MAD near-IR data and our results on the comparative analysis of these three sources. We performed Ks-band observations of PKS 0521-365 ( ) and PKS 2201+044 ( ) using the European Southern Observatory (ESO) Multi-Conjugate Adaptive Optics Demonstrator (MAD), mounted at UT4 (Melipal) of the Very Large Telescope (VLT).

In the following, we summarize the main results of our comparative analysis of these three BL Lacs: The properties of the jets of the three sources are remarkable similar. The jet morphology in optical, radio, NIR and X-ray bands has close resemblances with the presence of many knots (see Figs. 1-2). In particular, the brightest knot is observed in all bands from optical to X-rays. The spectral radio index of the brightest knot is 0.7 for all the cases, which is typical of the emission due to the losses in the jet interaction with the surrounding medium. The SED (Spectral Energy Distribution) of the brightest knot is consistent with an emission dominated by a single synchrotron component.

sources.summary: The paper presents adaptive-optics near-infrared observations of PKS 2201+044, describing it as a nearby BL Lac object with a jet detected from radio to X-rays and host-galaxy elliptical morphology.

quotes.quote: We report on a detailed study of the properties of the jet of the nearby BL Lac object PKS 2201+044, one of the rare cases where the jet is detected from radio to X-rays. We use new adaptive optics near-IR observations of the source, obtained with the ESO multi-conjugated adaptive optics demonstrator (MAD) at the Very Large Telescope. These observations acquired in Ground-Layer Adaptive Optics mode are combined with images previously achieved by HST, VLA and Chandra to perform a morphological and photometric study of the jet.

The morphology of the host galaxy is consistent with a luminous (M _ V 20.8 ) giant elliptical (Falomo et al. 1993). Extensive radio observations of jet and halo were collected by Laurent-Muehleisen et al. 1993. The radio source has a total extent of 5.6 arcmin (Ulvestad Johnston et al. 1984) and it has a compact ( 3 mas) core as revealed by VLBI observations at 2.29 GHz (Preston et al. 1985).

sources.summary: The paper observes HB89 2201+044 as a nearby blazar with a resolved host, finding no intra-night variability, inter-night variability, and roughly 0.3 percent polarization change after host correction.

quotes.quote: Here we present simultaneous photometric and polarimetric observations of two relatively nearby blazars, 1ES 1959+650 and HB89 2201+044 , that were obtained using the Calar Alto Faint Object Spectrograph mounted at the 2.2 m telescope in Calar Alto, Spain. An outstanding characteristic of these two blazars is the presence of well resolved host galaxies. This particular feature allows us to produce a study of their intrinsic polarization, a measurement of the polarization state of the galactic nucleus unaffected by the host galaxy. To carry out this work, we computed photometric fluxes from which we calculated the degree and orientation of the blazars polarization.

We find that the two blazars do not present intra-night photo-polarimetric variability, although we do detect a significant inter-night variability. Comparing polarimetric values before and after accounting for the host galaxies, we observe a significant difference in the polarization degree of about 1 in the case of 1ES 1959+650 , and 0.3 in the case of HB89 2201+044 , thus evidencing the non-negligible impact introduced by the host galaxies.

sources.summary: The paper describes 2201+044 as a Seyfert-1 galaxy with intricate extended radio structure, a northwest broad jet, and VLBI jet polarization with perpendicular magnetic field.

quotes.quote: 2201+044 Many radio observations have mapped the intricate extended structure in this Seyfert-1 galaxy at different frequencies and resolutions UlvestadJohnston84,VanGorkom89 . The 1.5 GHz observations of LaurentMuehleisen93 revealed a broad jet extending towards the northwest in PA 50 and faint extended emission to the east. The previous VLBI image of Kollgaard96 showed a compact jet structure in PA 42 , aligned with the dominant kpc-sale structure; no pc-scale polarization was detected. Our new VLBI map (Fig.  fig:2201 ) shows a jet in the same position angle, i.e., 40 . Polarization was detected only in the jet, with the inferred B field being perpendicular to the jet direction.

sources.summary: The paper includes 4C+04.77 among the nearby gamma-ray loud 1LAC AGNs correlated with PAO UHECR events and considered possible production sites.

quotes.quote: We identify 7 `` ray loud'' AGNs which are associated with UHECRs within 17^ and are likely candidates for the production sites of UHECRs: Centaurus A, NGC 4945, ESO 323-G77, 4C+04.77, NGC 1218, RX J0008.0+1450 and NGC 253.

Within the distance _1 , 4 PAO events correlate with 3 AGNs: NGC 4945, Centaurus A and 4C+04.77; at _2 , 7 PAO events correlate with 4 AGNs: NGC 4945, Centaurus A, ESO 323-G77 and 4C+04.77; at _3 , 17 events are associated with 7 AGNs: NGC 4945, Centaurus A, ESO 323-G77, 4C+04.77, NGC 253, NGC 1218 and RXJ0008.0+1450.

The potential UHECR accelerators Our results pinpoint 7 `` ray loud'' 1LAC AGNs as the potential sites for the production of PAO UHECRs (see Table tab:agn ): Cen A, NGC 4945, ESO 323-G77, 4C+04.77, NGC 1218, RX J0008.0+1450 and NGC 253 (ordered by increasing number of associated UHECRs at the angular distance 16.9^ ).

gmw_00000495.name: 3C 371
gmw_00000495.overview: 3C 371 is a BL Lacertae object [arXiv:1105.5226] hosted by a large elliptical galaxy [arXiv:1208.0896] that exhibits optical and infrared variability [arXiv:1208.0896, arXiv:1005.2177]. The galaxy is optically disturbed, possesses a close companion [arXiv:1005.2177], and features a resolved multiwavelength jet with synchrotron-emitting knots [arXiv:1105.5226, arXiv:astro-ph/0504197].

sources.summary: The paper includes 3C 371 among the few BL Lac objects with resolved optical jets and compares its multiwavelength jet morphology and synchrotron knot emission with two peers.

quotes.quote: PKS 0521-365, PKS 2201+044 and 3C 371 are up to now the only three classified BL Lac objects showing resolved optical jets.

For 3C 371 refer to . In the following, we summarize the main results of our comparative analysis of these three BL Lacs: The properties of the jets of the three sources are remarkable similar. The jet morphology in optical, radio, NIR and X-ray bands has close resemblances with the presence of many knots (see Figs. 1-2). In particular, the brightest knot is observed in all bands from optical to X-rays. The spectral radio index of the brightest knot is 0.7 for all the cases, which is typical of the emission due to the losses in the jet interaction with the surrounding medium. The SED (Spectral Energy Distribution) of the brightest knot is consistent with an emission dominated by a single synchrotron component.

The detection of resolved optical jets are consistent with estimates of the doppler factors and the viewing angles ( ). The optical spectra of these BL Lacs exhibit weak narrow and broad emission line. They are similar to that to BL Lac itself for which a Seyfert 1-like nucleus is reported ( ). Finally, these BL Lacs have some properties (e.g. the source's spectral radio index and the FRI radio type) similar to those of radio-loud NLS1s with blazar-like characteristics ( ).

sources.summary: The paper says 3C371 has unusual jet-knot spectra: one knot implies an X-ray-regime synchrotron break and a young source, while Knot A can break in the optical and need not be young.

quotes.quote: This criterion was satisfied by the two brightest knots of the AGN: 1136-135 , 1150+497 , 1354+195 , 3C 273 and 3C371 . In this work, the knot closer to the nucleus is referred to as Knot A and the further one as Knot B.

The knots of 3C371 and Knot A of 1136-135 are unique in this sample since their the X-ray flux lies below the extrapolation of the radio/optical spectra to X-ray wavelengths. This allows for the interpretation that the X-ray flux is due to synchrotron emission samb02,pesce,sahay . For Knot A of 1136-135 and Knot B of 3C371, this implies that the spectral break for the synchrotron emission occurs at the X-ray regime (Figure 1) which in turn indicates that these sources are relatively younger ones.

On the other hand, for Knot A of 3C371, the spectral break can occur at the optical band even if the X-ray flux is interpreted as being due to synchrotron emission (Figure 1) and hence this source need not be relatively young. However, this is only possible if t_ O,K < T_ ON,K and there is continuous injection of particles. Otherwise, a sharp cutoff in the spectrum at the optical band would have occurred and the X-ray emission would not be due to synchrotron emission.

sources.summary: The paper treats S4 1807+698 as a BL Lac-related elliptical galaxy with radio, optical, and X-ray jets, subluminal motion, and an anti-correlation between peak flux and jet PA.

quotes.quote: S4 1807+698 The source 1807+698 (3C 371, z=0.051) is related to BL Lac objects because of its optical variability and polarization in a large elliptical galaxy whose emission is dominated by the flux from the nucleus (Fan et al. 1999; Katajainen et al. 2000), and it is detected in gamma-ray by Fermi LAT. VLA image at 1.36 GHz shows a kpc scale halo and jets elongated in east-west (Cassaro et al. 1999). VLBI maps reveal a one-sided core-jet at PA 100 , e.g., at 5 GHz (G omez Marscher 2000). The jets in the optical with HST and in the X-rays with Chandra have been detected (Pesce et al. 2001), which are roughly aligned with the parsec-scale radio jet.

The 15 GHz MOJAVE result shows a sub-luminal jet motion of 0.1 (Lister et al. 2009b). A 43 GHz VLBA image exhibits a jet flow assembling a wiggling pattern (Lister 2001). An anti-correlation between the peak flux density and the PA of the major axis of inner-jet, with the coefficient of 0.45 at a confidence level of 98 , is found, and a flux variability period of 8.3 0.6 yr is estimated with the PSD method (see, Fig. fig3 and Fig. fig5 ).

sources.summary: The paper treats 1807+698 as one of five AGN targets observed by KVN at 22-130 GHz for multi-frequency VLBI astrometry, map registration, position shifts, and jet-related alignment tests.

quotes.quote: Figure fig:source_pos shows the distribution in the sky of the five selected sources (1803+784, 1807+698, 1842+681, 1928+738 and 2007+777) along with their angular separations, which range between 3.6^o and 11^o . None of the sources had been observed previously with VLBI at 130 GHz. The observations consisted of 3 minutes long scans alternating between the sources in each of the two triangles shown in Figure fig:source_pos , and between the triangles ca. every hour and a half, at the four bands simultaneously. Alternating between multiple sources allowed us to develop a strategy to decompose the relative astrometric measurements into single source position shifts that allow, for example, the registration of the images at different frequencies.

Figure fig:sc shows self-calibration images for the five AGN sources in the multi-frequency KVN observations, including the first images of these sources at 130 GHz. The visibility datasets were modelfitted and imaged and show little divergence from point-source core dominated images; in some cases there appears to be some elongation aligned with the jet direction as seen in higher resolution maps from MOJAVE mojave . Table tab:flux lists the total flux values as measured from the maps, at all frequency bands.

sources.summary: The paper classifies 1807+698 as a merger-phase BL Lac with optical disturbance, a close companion, optical and infrared variability, and bent diffuse VLA-scale emission.

quotes.quote: * 1807+698 (3C 371) (BL Lac, z=0.051 ): Optically disturbed source with a companion (projected separation 81 kpc). It is almost periodical in the optical. Variable in the infrared. The VLBA jet of this object appears straight. The VLA jet however appears bent and diffuse emission is present.

sources.summary: The thesis treats 3C371 as an AGN jet whose knots are unusual in the sample, with X-ray flux interpreted as synchrotron emission under continuous injection.

quotes.quote: The knots of 3C371 and Knot A of 1136-135 are unique in this sample, since their the X-ray flux lies below the extrapolation of the radio-to-optical spectrum to X-ray wavelengths. This allows for the interpretation that the X-ray flux is due to synchrotron emission . For Knot A of 1136-135 and Knot B of 3C371, this implies that the spectral break for the synchrotron emission occurs at the X-ray regime (Figure fig4:is_plot ), which in turn indicates that these sources are relatively younger. Indeed, the ratio of the observation time to the injection time, t_ O,K /T_ ON,K , for these sources are smallest (Table ch4:table5 ). On the other hand, for Knot A of 3C371, the spectral break can occur at the optical band even if the X-ray flux is interpreted as being due to synchrotron emission (Figure fig4:is_plot ) and hence this source need not be relatively young.

sources.summary: The paper treats 3C 371 as an outlying, less-beamed RoboPol rotator, noting low Doppler-factor estimates and its intermediate character between BL Lac objects and radio galaxies.

quotes.quote: The three points in Fig. fig:Doppl_vs_rot_ampl at =1.1 , which is approximately order of magnitude lower than others, are rotations in 3C 371 (RBPL J1806+6949). For this source, the Doppler factor in is listed as acceptable, which is their lowest quality class, and the sampling of the source was not very good, which could have resulted in an underestimated Doppler factor see for the effects of sampling on the Doppler factor estimates Liodakis2015 . However, and give =1.36 and 0.7 consistent with . According to , this source is an intermediate object between BL Lacs and radio galaxies, which supports the suggestion that the source is less beamed. When 3C 371 is excluded from the analysis, the correlation coefficient between ( ) and ( _ max ) is =-0.57 ( p value = 0.005 ), while the slope of the ordinary least squares bisector regression Isobe1990 fit is 1.04 0.03) , implying high significance of the correlation.

sources.summary: The paper treats RBPLJ1806+6949 as a blazar with uncertain rotation endpoints and double rotations, using only the largest rotation in simulations and jointly fitting nearby flares.

quotes.quote: Due to a moderate sampling and 180^ EVPA ambiguity, the rotation start and/or end points cannot be pinpointed accurately in five cases (namely RBPLJ0136+4761, RBPLJ0259+0747, RBPLJ1048+7143, RBPLJ1806+6949 and RBPLJ2311+3425). This ambiguity affects the estimated _ max and T_ rot of the event, which should really be considered as lower limits in this case.
In the cases of RBPLJ1806+6949 and RBPLJ2232+1143 where double rotations were observed, we simulated only the largest _ max rotations.
In the cases of double rotations in RBPLJ1806+6949 and RBPLJ2232+1143 the flares closest to the rotations were also fitted together to provide a consistent F_ value.
